address: Mar Mansour Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Maison M has two main restaurants. A sushi bar and a Lebanese mezze place. We tried the second one. We had the "formule" which consisted of a huge assortment of well presented dishes. However, the number of dishes presented was too overwhelming. Many plates were left untouched due to huge amount of choices. With less choices, we would have been able to try everything and to enjoy it. Other than that, service is ok. Presentation is good and food tasted great.

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: An ancient beautiful and big citadel near to Byblos old souk... You can see how great is citadel and what it took to be built and how many times it was destroyed... Crusaders built it then ottoman destroyed it... A magnificent citadel to be seen in Byblos... It is recommended!!!

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
We arrived on a Wednesday night at 7h for happy hour to Pre Beirut and we were received by a rude security guard on steroids, telling us where do you think you are going like that? In a rude tone, I asked him how does he allow himself to talk to people like that? He said if we are here without a reservation we cannot go in especially that we are 3 guys, we just turned our backs and left the building without going in after such a reception from an illiterate security guard.
We directly called the number of Pre Beirut and a certain girl answers, so we explained to her the situation asking to talk with management, she said the security guard was not allowed to do this and its not his job but she said she won't do anything about it and seemed totally careless.
We were stunned by their lack of customer service as if they are acting like that to scare customers off on purpose.
This was the first time we face such insolent behavior from staff/security and it shows the poor quality of service you will be getting in this place. No wonder it is not working nor popular and bad reviews are pouring like rain.
My advice to you, avoid like plague.

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Interesting feature are the stairs leading to the rooftop! Location is good, food is also good, but expensive

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: If you are anyone with some class or self respect I wouldn't go there. I live in the USA and I go to lebanon to have a good time and enjoy myself i called the club to see if they had any reservations available and they told me they were full but hey could squeeze us in at that one table. They said it would be $40 minimum charge. When I got there it felt like I was in a crack house. There were no lights, it seemed as though the people there were high, the place was empty there was maybe 10 guys who were on some sort of drugs. It wasn't a place for couples and I went with my boyfriend and two other couples. As a girl we all felt really uncomfortable. We were lied to by the staff saying it was full when it was clearly empty. I came to Lebanon for two weeks and I waisted one of my Friday nights there because it was one of be most miserable times of my life. If I could give a negative rating I would.

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
If I was to rate the welcome and service, I would put ZERO. But to be fair for the Dj and the lovely music I will put half the grade.

What a shame, I called to inquire about reservation, the guy told me to be as early as 9:30 to guarantee spot. When we arrived, the bouncers at the main entrance of building started interrogating us (three ladies) as if we are at the prison entrance! Stuff like who are you here for?! and why are you here that early, until he called them to check if they are ready to receive?! And so we were granted the permission to cross the borders.
We arrived at the 6th floor where the staff was hardly ready. No welcome whatsoever, we sat at the bar and the waitress as well as another waiter who both lack training, approached us. They said that they don't have drinks menu.
We ordered:
- One  Moscow mule that came with an average taste and no cucumber (not available)
-Another friend ordered rosé yet the waiter poured her white wine; she had to ask for him to replace it
- Seven up diet that was initially  served in a plastic cup until i asked her why not the normal glass one?!
They have a policy where you are requested either to pay each drink straight or to deposit the visa card until departure. Maybe they had few bad experiences. But the way they ask for it is not really appropriate; it was as if looking at the customer in his eye doubting him and unconsciously accusing him of theft!
 
The enjoyable thing is the Music, the age range is mid -late twenties.

I think that they have a potential with a beautiful location, they need an adequate training of the staff, a less suspicious attitude but most importantly more respectful bouncers.
P.S:  Don't drag people early if you are not ready to receive.

address: Saifi Suites Hotel, Saifi, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: They never answer the phone to reserve!! No matter when do you call always no answer ive been trying to call them for several months! If you go for walk in they tell you it’s fully booked!

address: Saifi Suites Hotel, Saifi, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: If you plan to visit this restaurant PLEASE read this review first!!!
review_text:
Currently having dinner in this restaurant on April 9th 2024. 
I just couldn’t resist writing this review while sitting there to show how much I am furious about the very bad service they have…first of all …our waiter ,a Lebanese guy with dark short hair and goatee has terrible customer service manners. We try to order from him and he has such a grumpy look and barely waits for you to finish your sentence…as if he is too busy and we are wasting his pre IOU’s time. …service is terrible…we have to continuously ask them to take a look at our empty wine glasses to refill them. 
They bring appetisers without serving utensils. We have to call waiters to remove empty glasses and dishes from the table. 
We asked for a bottle of wine and bottle of water. They brought the water bottle without water glasses. 
No salt and pepper shakers on the table. 
I ordered the beef tenderloin and my son ordered the same. The attached picture shows his dish. My dish actually had 2 thin pieces of steak put on top of each to show as if it was s thick piece of tenderloin! 
Vegetables were dry and super all dente!!!
I am embarrassed to be writing this review while sitting there since I am invited for dinner…but this has been such s terrible experience …from ALL aspects.

address: Saifi Suites Hotel, Saifi, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Such a delicious variety of Italian and French food items. Wonderful ingredients and food at reasonable prices, beautiful ambiance and with a lovely staff.

address: Saifi Suites Hotel, Saifi, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Normally busy on Sundays, better to book a table in advance. It’s more like a lively family lunch place. The truffle pizza was superb but was disappointed with the smaller portions salads. They are flexible with the side dishes with main dishes. My burger was done to perfection. Very good service and very good choices of desserts. Go for Baba au rhum or ice cream fraisier their specialties. Good value for money, will go back when I don’t know where to go.

address: Mkhallissiye Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
This is a small specialty bakery with great stuff!
They use an amazing butter to make their croissant 
They have the best, yes the best croissant in Lebanon, it is delicious and better than all the French bakeries in Lebanon 
U must try it, I love it❤️❤️❤️

address: Mkhallissiye Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This place in saifi village produces some fine quality French style croissants. I visited the bakery Sunday morning for breakfast and grabbed an assortment of croissants to go. I noticed that in addition to croissants they produce several kinds of bread. For a start prices are high and expensive (I think some kind of croissants is for 5000 L.L.). I had the plane croissant au beurre d'insigny, chocolate and cheese croissants. Clearly this bakery use premium quality ingredients but unfortunately we felt the croissants way too buttery for our taste and they were not crunchy enough from the outside. The special thing to mention is the cheese croissant which they fill with emmental cheese and another creamy kind of cheese which I believe is Brie.

address: Mkhallissiye Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: A nice discovery in the middle of Saifi Village - the smell of butter and fresh bread is a calling by itself. Excellent sourdough bread, pastries and beignets. Do not leave without a bag of delicious nougats

address: Boutros Dagher Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
This bar is a truly unique and welcoming spot, tucked away in a charming little alley that adds to its character. The decor is stunning, featuring authentic, handcrafted pieces made in the family workshop, which give the place a special and personal touch. The atmosphere is warm and friendly, with an amazing team.

It’s a place that exudes authenticity and makes you feel right at home from the moment you step in. Even though it’s not on a main street, that only makes it more of a treasure waiting to be discovered. I wholeheartedly recommend it for anyone looking to enjoy a wonderful time in the company of kind and truly exceptional people.

address: Boutros Dagher Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Must visit! An extraordinary experience! The vibe,the music,the drinks and the mastery and art while doing them. It's something else. Botaniste,chapeau bas!

address: Boutros Dagher Street, Beirut Lebanon

review_text: 10 over 10 is not enough. Amazing how a small place can offer such great desserts. Cookies are mouthwatering. Cupcakes, cookie dough Oreo are a must try. Very vibrant, positive,and friendly staff. Prices are reasonable.

address: Amir Omar Street, Beirut 60170 Lebanon
review_title: It's not Just Better It's the Best
review_text: Finally a bakery in Lebanon that produces excellent/super tasty cookies, cinnamon rolls, brownies and many other delicious treats exactly the real American way like they should be done. Bravo. I have almost lost hope in getting decent cookies after tasting several bakeries productions in Beirut. Until I read great reviews about this place. I went there with no high expectations but luckily I was amazed. It's a small cute little shop in a clam area in Beirut. The goodies are very well displayed. The lady who helped me was friendly and informative. I grabbed one cinnamon roll, one kinder filled cookie, one caramel (Twix) brownie and finally one cake ball filled with Nutella. I can safely say that all what I have purchased were amazingly tasty. It's clear that this bakery uses only premium quality ingredients and it has the real American know how. The cookie is big, chewy and generously filled. The cake ball filled with Nutella and dipped in chocolate is out of this world. The brownie was rich and moist. Cinnamon roll was fresh and full of flavors. Portions are more than fair and the prices for those jewels are so reasonable. I so recommend this bakery.

address: Amir Omar Street, Beirut 60170 Lebanon
review_text:
This place is recommended for sweet cravers. Amazing dessert.
KitKat and crunch cookies are super. Gluten free cookies are available too. 
As for cup cakes, I loved the taste of red velvet cookie dough and tarboosh. 
Coockie dough oreo with chocolate dip is a must try.
Good service and fair prices.

address: Amir Omar Street, Beirut 60170 Lebanon
review_text: A small outlet in clemenceu offering great dessert. Love the cinnamon rolls, generously filled. Cup cakes taste as good as it look. The cookies are amazing especially chocolate kinder cookies. M&M dough is a good choice too. Nice staff and good prices

address: Amir Omar Street, Beirut 60170 Lebanon
review_text:

address: Nicolas Rebeiz Al Achkar Building, Talaat Joumblat, Facing CMC Hospital, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Perfect fresh tasting food in a really nice environment and cute little setting. I will definitely go to this place if you're in the area. Great Italian food and great service.

address: Nicolas Rebeiz Al Achkar Building, Talaat Joumblat, Facing CMC Hospital, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: A beautiful way to spend one’s last evening in Beirut
review_text:
We decided to have Italian food for our last dinner in Beirut. We chose this place as its near to our hotel.
The restaurant is small. Just five tables on the ground floor . Upstairs, i think there is a kitchen, no idea about seating.
The host Samir is genuinely friendly and passionate about the food. I could not decide so he gave me a few options. Anyway, we chose veg gnocchi, a spaghetti bolognese and a meat lasagna. Absolutely divine and when teamed up with white Heritage sweet wine, it was an unbeatable combination.
Food was relatively expensive but outstanding. Well worth the cost. 
As we asked for a cab and said it was our last night, Samir whipped off the table cloth and said that foreigners who visit his place are requested to follow the tradition! He made us write a comment in our language on the wooden table top. 

A great way to leave Beirut with wonderful memories. Thank you, Samir.

address: Nicolas Rebeiz Al Achkar Building, Talaat Joumblat, Facing CMC Hospital, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This restaurant has a very nice small dining area and serves good Italian food. Their pasta is particularly good.

review_text: This is a great place if you are looking for a genuine experience away from the urbanized Italian Restaurants. It is a cosy and comfy small restaurant with a big heart. review_text:
The MOODZ Lounge & Bar is located on the ground floor within the Gefinor Rotana Hotel in the Hamara District of Beirut and is a great place to simply relax and unwind after a busy day enjoying the sights of Beirut!
I really like the interior décor of the MOODZ Lounge & Bar as it is very fresh and modern. The service is tops as one would expect at the Gefinor Rotana and they have a nice menu both for their food as well as drink items.
I suggest you make it a point to enjoy the MOODZ Lounge & Bar within the Gefinor Rotana…you will not be disappointed!

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: Excellent hotel bar, lounge, and outdoor patio
review_text: I stayed for a week on business at the Gefinor Rotana in Beirut. Moodz is an excellent bar with indoor and outdoor seating, just off the hotel lobby. Great service and ambiance. Moodz made the end of each day fun and relaxing, definitely a great way to unwind! Ahmad was a great bartender!

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: Nice quiet place for casual meetings
review_text: Loved this place as my main objective to stay at this hotel was to find a quiet spot for few casual work-related meetings & Moodz certainly delivered this objective! Drinks are good! The waiters are very courteous! There's also a nice outdoor area for smokers!

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_text: I've visited this bar cafe which is located inside Rotana Jefinor Beirut. The varieties offered are nice . The most amazing thing was the service , the waiters are very polite and cheerful and the order was quickly served . I recommend this bar cafe for all Rotana Jefinor guests .

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: The bar and terrace is a great place to relax and enjoy the views of this beautiful city of Beirut!
review_text:
While we were staying at the Rotana Gefinor we went up to the Aquarius Pool Bar & Terrace quite often to enjoy the beautiful Lebanese sunset as the Aquarius Pool Bar is located on the 18th floor of the hotel. 
From the Aquarius Pool Terrance you can enjoy great views of the city of Beirut plus the AUB, AUH and of course the Mediterranean Sea. During the day you can still enjoy the Aquarius Pool Bar & Terrace while using the hotels pool! The bar and terrace is a great place to relax and enjoy the views of this beautiful city of Beirut!

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_text: Being winter and this is open pool, it was cold. It says it is "temperature controlled", but doesn't seem so.

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_text: Well I enjoyed the pool with the rooftop sea view, but the pool itself is quite small. they offer range of drinks but I think it needs some extra seating around the pool as well as some sort of sun prevention.

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_text: It was my favorite after a long business day . It's located on the 18th floor in Rotana Jefinor hotel in Beirut. The view is overlooking the Mediterranean with a fresh breathtaking as if you are in heaven . The staff are very cheerful , friendly  and well trained . address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: ... and you want to have a very enjoyable dinner or breakfast I strongly suggest that you try The Olive Garden...
review_text:
Within the Rotana Gefinor Hotel in Beirut is a fantastic restaurant called The Olive Garden.
We had a fantastic dinner and great breakfast there last week during our short stay at the Rotana Gefinor.
For dinner, the F&B Manager had arranged a special table that had rose petals on the table and had added a few small candles to have a very romantic effect. 
My wife ordered a plate of barbecued shrimp, vegetables, and a large basket of French Fries. She said it was the best shrimp dinner that she had ever tasted!
I ordered their famous Caesar Salad and a bowl of soup. The salad was so large that I had to cancel the soup, as I was too full on this great tasting Ceasar Salad. 
I enjoyed several glasses of the Olive Gardens house white wine, from the Lebanese vineyard called: St. Thomas. 
The next morning, we enjoyed a very good breakfast.
We spent one night at the Gefinor Rotana hotel from our return from our Egypt Holiday, and again the food within the Olive Garden was fantastic.
So, if you find yourself in Beirut and you want to have a very enjoyable dinner or breakfast I strongly suggest that you try The Olive Garden restaurant with in the Rotana Gefinor Hotel, trust me you will not be sorry, trust me.

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: Trust me you will enjoy the Olive Garden restaurant...
review_text:
Within the Gefinor Rotana Hotel is a great Restaurant called the Olive Garden Restaurant and Terrace.
 The Olive Garden serves a Breakfast and Luncheon Buffet plus an A la Carte Dinner menu.
I  enjoyed their  breakfast buffet during my last stay and all the food was prepared in a very professional manner. My Omelet was made to order, and it was very tasty.
One shortfall was the buffet had a very limited Fresh Fruit selection.
What made up for the shortage was a very nice a fresh Baked Goods section. Their Croissants were freshly baked and served warm and were of the same quality that you have in France!
So, if you want to enjoy a very nice buffet Breakfast I suggest that you try the Olive Garden restaurant and terrace.
Trust me you will enjoy the Olive Garden restaurant.

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: Within the Gefinor Rotana Hotel in Beirut is a great restaurant called: The Olive Garden Restaurant and Terrace
review_text:
Within the Gefinor Rotana Hotel in Beirut is a great restaurant called: The Olive Garden Restaurant and Terrace
No matter what meal you enjoy at the Olive Garden each is super and tasty.
I had both a light late-night dinner and an early morning breakfast on my last stay and both were fantastic. 
For late dinner I had a great fresh Lentil soup and the next morning I enjoyed a fantastic breakfast with a very tasty Omelet.
I must admit, I am really looking forward in my return to the Gefinor Rotana and to enjoy another top meal at the Olive Garden Restaurant and Terrace.

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: ... that you have both meals within the hotel at the fantastic Olive Garden restaurant, you will not be sorry, trust me.
review_text:
The Olive Garden is the main restaurant within the Rotana Gefinor Hotel in the Hamra district of Beirut.
The Olive Garden serves a wonderful Buffet Breakfast, and during our short stay both their lunch and dinner meals were using their Ala Carte Menu only as I assume the hotels occupancy rate was a bit low. I assume that once the occupancy rate increases they will go back to having all three meals in the Buffet style.
The Olive Garden has a lovely outdoor terrace with a nice water fountain and many flowering plants. After our two hour walk outside the hotel, my wife and I returned back to the hotel and decided to have our dinner on the outside terrace. The Ala Carte menu was simply perfect as a full buffet would simply be too much food for each of us. 
My wife ordered a very nice grilled Chicken, with a side of vegetables and a hugh amount of very tasty French Fries. I started with a nice vegetarian Vegetable Soup and then a very delicious Margaretta Pizza with fresh mushrooms. The size of the all portions were so large both my wife and I could not finish our entrée’s. 
The next morning I enjoyed the Rotana’s special Breakfast Buffet. I started with a nice plate of sliced fresh fruits followed by a great mixed vegetable Omelet. 
All-in-all it was a fantastic way to start my day as I had three important meetings planned for that day and I never developed any hunger pains throughout the day.
I strongly suggest that instead of going out of the hotel and having your breakfast and dinner at some unknown restaurant that you have both meals within the hotel at the fantastic Olive Garden restaurant, you will not be sorry, trust me.

address: Gefinor Rotana, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_text: The most amazing spread of Lebanese breakfast food I’ve yet to experience in my time in Lebanon. This is not your average open buffet!

address: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street, Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
I walked in today and tried to make lunch reservations. August 27, 11:30 AM. I said to the hostess my dad is 90 and he needs a chair with armrests not too far from the entrance. She said that was not possible. I pointed to a chair with armrests and I said there’s one. She said she couldn’t move the chair. I said really? You can’t move a chair to help a 90 year old man? She said no nonchalantly. I said we were here last month and somehow a chair was arranged. She said she needed to ask management and get back to me (??!!) I said you need to ask management if you can move a chair for a 90 year old man? She said yes. I walked out. Don’t bother ask management.

We had lunch at this place last month and service was much better. The waiters were very nice and helpful. They arranged for a chair with armrests and cushions. What was different this time? We were 7 then and the bill came out to $830. I paid $1,000. 

The hostess today was very rude. She couldn’t care less…

address: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street, Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Avoid that place ! Pure scam ! Need major adjustments for food taste + service ! Mediocre taste !! Horrible experience ! What a waste of time & money!

address: Minet El Hosn, Beirut 1333 Lebanon
review_text: Quite simply the best Lebanese cuisine in the world. Every dish bursts with fresh, well-sourced ingredients, offering a scrumptious variety of traditional and modern Lebanese flavours. Healthy, vibrant, and beautifully presented, the food is matched by the glamorous ambiance and elegant crowd. Service is flawless, attentive, polite, and professional. address: Minet El Hosn, Beirut 1333 Lebanon
review_text:
I had high expectations for Em Sherif Rooftop, but sadly it was one of the worst dining experiences I’ve had in Beirut. The service was extremely poor — the staff was inattentive and made us feel like we were not welcome. What really shocked me was that, without even asking if we were done or if we wanted anything else, the waiter just came and dropped the bill on our table as if telling us to leave. No courtesy, no professionalism, and definitely no respect for the basic dining experience.
For a place with such a name and location, I expected much more. Never going back, and I honestly don’t recommend it l.

address: Minet El Hosn, Beirut 1333 Lebanon
review_title: Lebanese Perfection — Beirut x Em Sherif café
review_text: Dining at Em Sherif Café  on my last night in Beirut was an unforgettable experience. While the dishes were familiar—ones I often order back home in London—the flavors here were in a league of their own. Every bite was fresh, rich, and deeply satisfying. No words can fully express how much I enjoyed this meal.

address: Minet El Hosn, Beirut 1333 Lebanon
review_title: Great Lebanese food in the center of Beirut
review_text: Great food. Nice location. Although smoke inside resto. Excellent Lebanese dishes. A must go ! Great mezza dishes although no fish there

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Great place in the heart of downtown Beirut. We’ve been there plenty of times. Good food and atmosphere. Portions were reduced from what it was before. More importantly the service was sub-par considering the hospitality Lebanon is known for. We caught our server looking over at our table inappropriately for the setting, gossiping with his colleague, and laughing. It’s a surprise as this never happened before and frankly this will be the last time we will come.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Come as a group of at least four. Order a little of everything.
review_text:
Beirut is noticeably on the mend after the 2020 port explosion that devasted the city leaving hundred’s dead. With a favourable terroir, land and sea, and some decades of French influence, you can look forward to great food.

There’s a simplicity in great Japanese restaurants that adheres to the adage, same, same, but different. The décor is very simple and no less elegant and contemporary for it. Located between Zaitunay Bay, Downtown and the political district, it services a demanding clientele.

That the menu has no prices on the printed version might make a first-time visitor uneasy, but worry not. Due to currency instability, which has led to the beginning of nationwide dollarisation, pricing can be fluid in Lebanese Lira. 

There is a QR code that will give you current menu pricing in US Dollars. This is very common and should not worry you. You will be far too interested in the fine range of offerings, the wines every bit as appealing.

Sea salted edamame for the immediately famished enjoyed, it was time to place both feet firmly inside.

Fresh salmon maki put a smile on my wife’s face, pre and post, much like a child getting a small ice cream cone, albeit this one in wrapped glorious dark seaweed.

Ordered from the salad menu, odd given the greens were really tasty garnish, were two helpings of crispy spicy salmon. Sashimi salmon in popcorn popping panko crumb and a light late lingering spice. If we were ordering in stages, I’m not sure we’d have eaten anything else.

Tuna and salmon sashimi were on point, fresh ginger and wasabi likewise.

Three separate sushi platters arrived next. There were a few of us. I’m not sure I’ve seen a mango ura maki before. The colour was joyful and like its neighbours disappeared systemically into us happy diners.

I opted for the wagyu foie gras, ordered and served bleu. Merci chef. Both were similarly butchered for their plating, served as two fractured asteroids one atop the other. Holding it together was a thuddingly thickened teriyaki sauce. From the nose up to my eyes, everything was alight. 

It cut like butter and melted similarly in all our mouths. As I write this an hour later, its ghost still happily haunts my mouth.

The service is good, efficient, the waiters know their menu and can talk for it.

I don’t know when I’ll be back in Beirut again, but if I am, I’ll dine here at least once.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: A favorite for sushi is Beirut. The menu has quite a good selection and everything is delicious. The setting is nice and the service is very good. Their salmon sashimi salad and their tuna sashimi salad are very good. Highly recommend.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Very bad experience. Ordered soba noodles and got white fast food noodles, fried rice came with corn and mushrooms, miso soup had mushrooms, tomyum tasted like miso with red paste spice, tempura shrimps soaked in cocktail sauce. Food came in weird order, they forgot the drinks. And so expensive. So NOT worth it. Will never go again.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon

review_text: Located in the trendy „Little Dubai“ district near Phoenicia Hotel. Very friendly bar staff, quality of food very good. Nice drinks and great music. Prices are on a higher level but compare well on an international level.

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
I was disappointed as I didn’t get the service I was expecting. 
1- After my visit to the restaurant to pay a deposit and agreed on location of table, I was disappointed on the day of dinner that they did not seat us on the selected table 
2-I requested to include the names of my daughter and her husband on a private menu, as  we were celebrating their wedding, restaurant skipped writing their names and only apologized after ruining my surprise

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: We came to this restaurant on a family lunch. The food was good and the service very nice. I have heard comment on this place in the past saying that it was a place to “see and to be seen”, implying that the food was not great, though if that was the case before, I believe it isn’t anymore.

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Enjoyed a Business lunch here ..located in downtown area, really smart dining space with very attentive service. Excellent quality cooking  and ingredients...meats were all exceptional..the Chauteaubriand and veal chop in particular. Appetisers were all really fresh ingredients and well presented. Desserts were also good, particularly the traditional French Toast with caramel sauce. High end restaurant with high quality cooking and service. Highly recommended.

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
The risotto a la Truffe was terribly salty. Couldn’t eat it
The fillet dish was supposed to be served with potatoes and spinach. The spinach was not served. And when I ask for it They have added additional price. 
The waiters are not friendly and not attentive to the customers request
My experience was below average

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: We were three girlfriends visiting the restaurant on Saturday night, despite our hotel suggestion not to.. The restaurant has a luxurious atmosphere. The cuisine is French. First complimentary little plates of carrots, cashius and Parmesan was a very pleasant surprise. Niçoise salad was ok, risotto very tasty, although fatty, chateaubriand and beef fillet of extremely high quality. Finally, dessert, apple crumble with Cointreau ice cream was the best ever!! Not to miss. The main complain that we are still talking about is the service: DISHES WERE TAKEN AWAY WHILE WE HAD NOT FINISHED EATING!!! Never happened to us in any other restaurant. It seems that they wanted us to leave the restaurant asap! Also, one of the waiters was unexperienced, almost spilled the food on my friend’s head!! Only exception, the parking valet service: boys held huge umbrellas helping us to reach our taxi protecting us from the heavy rain! Overall: nice expensive restaurant, tasty food coming at big portions. Recommended, just stand up against the overeager waiters ...

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Top end restaurant in downtown area focused on business lunch customers (as I was). 50/60’s chic style decor. Food was Mediterranean/French. Good quality food and cooking but nothing exceptional or Michelin standard. Service was attentive.

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
I go to Balthus once per month for business/friends lunch. The menu has been clinically improved. Offering many options for the healthy and the foodies.
Tuesday and Thursday are Kebbe Naye day, pre order it to guarantee your portion. Their crab salad, tuna salad made with fresh fish is second to none. The fresh ham , a la coupe is a house specialty but not always available.The omelette and meat options are excellent and the tomatoes and also the pea salads are exquisite! Must visit for a great meal and service.

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Great ingredients wonderful service professional staff. Superb wine list needs to be updated as you see a wine year on bottle and you get another. An efficient small effective menu with fresh ingredients.
Felicitations for Mrs. Frida

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This is the place to do business in Beirut whether its politicians, bankers,  lawyers or leaders of industry...they are all invariably here at least once a week. Never sitting outside, always inside. The food is delicious whatever you order and the service is impeccable (which is hard to achieve in Lebanon). My favourites are the tomatoe salad, lentil salad and the steak.

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Now very average and way overpriced
review_text: Used to be a fan but the last couple of meals were quite disappointing. While the service was good, the food was mediocre, except for a tasty ceviche. But the prices have risen to stratospheric levels for what is essentially an average bistro. I sense that with this approach, the place won't be around much longer. I will certainly not go there again.

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Beirut is a little tricky as far as location is concerned. This restaurant is somehwat hidden away, but don't let that put you off. Is is easy to locate on google maps or on Allo taxi. It is small, boutique and very, very friendly. 

So much for location.

But here is the kicker. The staff are attentive without being overbearing. Arabic, French and English are spoken with equivalent fluency. There are brilliant starters and coffee and desserts are available. But the real deal here are the steaks. 

Yes; you will pay for what you get; It is not cheap, but when it comes it is sublime. Or better. I will confess I had an Argentinian 200g fillet and chips. It was quite simply wonderful. 

I was in Beirut for two weeks and would (and will) go back here to get a real, quality and carefully well-cooked steak which you could compare with anything you could get in London, Munich, Sydney or Cape Town.

LBP, USD and mainstream credit cards are welcome (with a + for international credit cards)

If  you are hankering for a clearly wonderful beef meat meal with 100% quality, then this is your place.

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Our freind told us we must come here and try meet. And I am so happy we did. It is very nice place,  few tables,  great  salads and of course meat. Very tasty, you can feel different kind of source and spices in each bite.
Very nice wine list.

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Good service, great atmosphere, tasty burgers indeed
The vibe is pretty cosy, friendly waiters and staff

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: The meat is consistently excellent. The ambiance is always nice and cosy. It is expensive though, especially if you don't order a piece of meat; i.e.: meat price is acceptable as it's top quality, but burger and appetizers are so overpriced.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Salads are very good.  the daily plate was good Moujadra.  it is pleasent to sit outdoor when the weather is good.  Freindly service.  Can order food from adjacent Pizza co and Burger co.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Our family spent a few days in Beirut. We wanted to avoid the 30$ per person usually charged in hotels. We found this nice restaurant, well located and offering excellent breakfast (eggs on toast, avocado-eggs bagels....) at a reasonable price.  A no brainer to start the day well!

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
My brother, his wife, kids & I went to Deli.co early on in December for breakfast in downtown Beirut in a Saturday.

The place is average sized - although the outdoor seating arrangement felt a little crowded and in close proximity to other patrons. 

The waiter was pretty helpful and offered us plenty of advice and a quick service. 

I ordered gluten free waffles & was truly truly impressed at how yummy they are, while me niece ordered scrambled eggs, which were pretty moist. My sister in law loved her avo on toast while my brother found his pancakes mediocre. I also ordered a gluten free pb&j with caramelized banana which was alright. 
The the star dish, and a must try of you try this place, was the gluten free waffles; everyone agreed that they were the best waffles we’ve tasted in a longtime - not to mention gluten free!  

Over all, Deli.co’s outdoor seating arrangement had a chill vibe, especially for a Saturday and the food was tasty.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Yes for the tastes and service
review_text:
Stopped twice during a business day in Beirut at Deli co where service was excellent and sandwiches were fresh from bread to ingredients. 
Only prices are a bit over the average when compared to the same meals bought everywhere in the country however these reflect the area and the quality of food being served

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Deli.co is one of the four sisters restaurants Pizza.co, Burger Co and recently opened Ummi at Beirut souks. And it is not to be underestimated!

Same concept of ability to order from all four menus, we opted to honor Deli.co's menu only, and we did not regret!

Service is synchronized with the theme, the staff are knowledgeable off the menu, cheerful and helpful.
We enjoyed dinner on the terrace, savoring:

Signature item: the original NYC Pastrami on rye bread served with a side of dill pickles and chips: it is so flavorsome not the healthiest thing you can have so worth the try!
Open faced scottish Smoked salmon over spicy avocado on country bread, equally served with chips:premium quality of every single ingredient reflected in the taste
Classic grilled cheese: same presentation and sides
My favorite trio salad: first of all I loved the concept of being able to combine three types of salad. I also appreciated the generous quantity and in addition to that the fact that it is changed daily: we had a combination of grilled vegetables, freekeh salad with eggplants, pomegranate, cherry tomatoes and parsley and fell in love at first sight / bite! and lettuce hearts with olives and drizzled with yogurt based sauce. 
How could we miss their home made desserts:

Lemon poppy seed cake: I love the contrast sourness of lemon with the sweetness of cake and the small crunch of the seeds
Semolina cake with caramelized oranges: so moist.
Carrot cake: was my least favorite I felt it needed more spices though it had a beautiful presentation.
We had lovely times and would totally recommend you to visit.

La Petite

XoXo

address: Beirut 00961 Lebanon
review_text:
Well, this was disappointing. Went with two Lebanese people. After 15 plus years, we are always keen to find a really good Lebanese / Middle Eastern restaurant. This is not it.

The menu is straight forward - all the basics one would expect: Hummus, baba ghanoush, sambousik, kibbeh, shish taouk, and so on.  We were very excited to try the kibbeh nayeh (lamb tartare) which they said they didn’t have, because the quality of the meat wasn’t good enough. I can tell them where to get good enough lamb in Singapore, if they are struggling. The menu is pretty short anyway, so not to offer everything on it on a Saturday evening seems an odd choice.

The food generally is mediocre. Sadly. The fattoush had an odd aftertaste - like some of the lettuce wasn’t as fresh as it should have been. The garlic sauce with the chicken skewers didn’t taste the way it should. Perhaps there are different ways to prepare, but it ought to have a lemony taste. This one didn’t. We usually love it, always eat too much of it. This thou tasted strange and bland. Everything was a bit bland - we can make much better and fresher Lebanese food at home.

We had two cocktails which were supposed to have alcohol, but we could not really taste it. It tasted mostly of water. Two bears came and were so flat, we sent them back - we rarely sent anything back. Should have done the same with the cocktails. We got the safer option of two Little Creatures bottles instead.

The interior of the place is pretty, very small place though. We had hoped to sit outside as it looked pleasant in photos, ans we generally prefer outside seating. Unfortunately, that area is right next to the car drop off point, and not sufficiently protected, so its really just a waiting area. Service was good though. They have enough staff and they are friendly and effective.

I don’t think we will be back though. Why bother if they are not going to make an effort with this to being this amazing cuisine to Singapore.

I was planning to have lunch with my two kids so I tried calling them 6 times(and i saw the lady later on when i arrived putting the phone on silent each time it ring)to prepare the order before my arrival but no one answered my calls. Anyway I went to the place and asked the lady at the desk to take my order to gain some time while waiting for a table to be empty because I have my children (7 and 3 years old) but she refused saying the order would not be late at all. I got to the place at 3:30, i followed up first time on my order at 4 and they confirmed it will be served now. A table arrived after us and received their food, 2 takeaways orders came and took their orders while I'm still waiting and my food. At 4:15 I asked them to bring the bill and put all my order as takeaway because I have to leave and i can t wait anymore at 4:30 I left the place without any food, stating that I ordered my food at least 5 times and each time they apologize and confirm it will be served immediately.

Shame on such staff and management, I have 2 kids who waited 1 hour for a burger but this is the epic miss management that i have ever seen

WILL NEVER ENTER THIS PLACE AGAIN

review_text:

While walking in gemayzeh Street, we got attracted by the coziness of the place right on the corner, it's a hidden jam!!!

The server was knowledgeable and friendly at all times, he recommended delicious choices. 
Butter chicken was my favorite.

address: Pasteur St, Gemmayzeh, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: A splash of color on the table!
review_text:
We tried Spicy No 7 just before the COVID lockdown. We had ordered home a variety of dishes according to the suggestions of the manager. What a fragrant surprise! 
I have to say beforehand that the delivery service was prompt and neat. Dishes arrived hot and not spilled despite the sauces, which in my dictionary is a major plus. 
The food is colorful, tasty and quite interesting. The curry is well balanced the spices are fragrant and fresh. The rice is made to perfection. The only drawback was actually the samosas that were in my opinion at bit over fried. This been said, my opinion is favorable to this small gem as the country needs some “exotic” menus since the market is already saturated with French european cuisines and the Lebanese ones. 
Word of the wise, put all the clichés you heard about Indian cuisine and try it!

address: Pasteur St, Gemmayzeh, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
What a great place! Reminds me why the Lebanese street food culture is just great, and it’s the people not the money thrown at it that makes an eatery great! 

We sat outside with the wife and the 3 year old, and I just love sitting on a beirut sidewalk and watch life happen. We ordered the Samosas for appetizer and they were exactly how they should be and not as heavy as others that we’ve had.
The boy loves his chicken nuggets, but doesn’t really know what a chicken nugget is. So any breaded chicken breast does the trick. And he devoured the chicken escalope that we ordered for him. The wife and I shared the 7 burger, coconut curry and the delicious fries. They were all great but the burger is something of a must try, the typical “lebanese all in burger” wrapped in the Paratha is a brilliant idea and works like magic. This what happens when you thoughtfully design a dish and create a menu, a great hidden gem that lets the food shine, and does away with the need of a fancy or extravagant experience that inevitably takes away from the meal

address: Pasteur St, Gemmayzeh, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: I loved my experience savoring authentic food at this tiny neighborhood restaurant. The welcome was warm and the decor is bright, clean and cheery. The food was delivered quickly with a smile, and I loved the clay plates it was served on. I tried their butter chicken, samosas, coconut chicken and they were all excellent; I did not feel stuffed afterwards. The chef had eliminated most oil/butter from his kitchen and replaced it with yogurt, coconut milk or cashew butter. The food is more flavorful then spicy (as in hot), which suited me just fine!  The restaurant is located in Gemmayze, in an airy corner on Pasteur st.

address: Jemmayzeh, Rmail, Pasteur str., St Antoin bldg., Beirut Lebanon
Jemmayzeh Pasteur str Rmail St. Antoin bldg, Beirut Lebanon
Avenue Charles Helou Saifi, Beirut District, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
I had a reservation under my name, and my friends and I arrived properly dressed and ready for a night out. Yet, we were shocked by the extremely unprofessional and discriminatory treatment at the door.

I was denied entry because my leggings had a small tear on the side — something I immediately addressed by wearing a long jacket and clearly stating that I would not remove it inside. Even after that, they still refused to let me in.
Then they began judging my friends’ outfits as “over,” even though they were fully decent and properly dressed. Meanwhile, we watched other guests enter with outfits far more revealing — to the point where three-quarters of their bodies were exposed. There are pictures that clearly show the double standards.

This behavior is not only unprofessional — it is humiliating.
It shows a serious lack of consistency, respect, and integrity in how they choose who gets in.

The floor manager, Toufik , made the situation even worse. He gave disrespectful looks toward my friends and justified letting in a girl wearing a dress shorter than her sensitive areas by saying she was “properly dressed,” while claiming that leggings — clean, neat, and styled for a night out — “don’t fit the place.”
He even commented that leggings are only for the gym or cleaning floors. This is beyond disrespectful.

This is clear discrimination and humiliation, and it reflects an attempt to “protect the venue’s image” at the expense of people who are actually decent and well-presented — while allowing in outfits that are far more inappropriate.

Instead of respecting their guests, they choose to belittle them, misjudge them, and apply rules selectively depending on who is standing at the door. A place that treats customers like this should rethink what “reputation” truly means, because respect doesn’t come from insulting decent people.

This experience was unacceptable on every level.
No one should be spoken to or treated this way — especially with a confirmed reservation. If this is their idea of hospitality, they would be better off closing their doors and rethinking their values.

The security prevent my friend from entering, we requested the manager to clarify what happened to explain the situation. The hostess went to him and came back with " Sorry l moder ma ader" "Sorry the manager can't come". Thank you for this rude, and unprofessional service. review_text: If we want to say it in a nice and diplomatic way we would say that what happened to me was a misunderstanding. address: Pasteur Street, Gemmayze, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:

review_text: if you're in Beirut and craving awesome cocktails and delicious food, look no further! This spot lives up to its name with the best Palomas in town. The vibes here are off the charts cool!

address: Pasteur Street, Gemmayze, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Had a business dinner / gathering for over 20 guests and the least thing i can say that it was an unforgettable evening . These guys know what they are doing which rarely can be found at other similar category venues . International dishes served with style and twist , i wont be able to list all the dishes we tried but definitely the sliders were out of his world . The service and the staff were extremely attentive for each small detail which made our evening perfect . great background music and wonderful cocktails .

address: Pasteur Street, Gemmayze, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: I went there Thursday Feb 1 for a drink. Sat at the bar. Lo and behold the bartender was grabbing ice cubes in the palm of his hand and chipping at them with a knife. Super gross and unhygienic. I made a comment to him about that but he dismissed it. I ordered my check. Someone came, presumably the manager, to explain to me that the bartenders wash their hands frequently and that it’s ok. Of course it’s NOT ok. There’s absolutely no excuse for touching the ice, let alone grabbing it with full hand. Worse, it seems this was not a one-off, it’s accepted practice at this place. Even if the bartender washed his hands frequently, I do not accept my ice to be touched; however, their hands were NOT clean since they were handling cash also. Gross.

address: Pasteur Street, Gemmayze, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Arrived and the valet asked me to move my car. Nice table in front of a glass wall. Was asked to move so the waiters could open the accordion-like doors next to table. I called the waiters twice and no one answered. They put a warm heater next to my table which was nice. Good cappuccino. Waiters had no change for a $50 bill.

This restaurant is located in Miramar resort, and had a great beach scene. I am not sure though if you could access the resort from the restaurant, but it looked far walking distance. Highly recommended.

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: This is a hidden gem once you discover you'll become a regular there! The place is tucked away between EL Mina buildings on Groupie roundabout so you will need to ask around if this is your first time. Trust me, it's worth it! For around 30 USD you will get a set menu of all kinds of mezza dishes plus the catch of the day. The place does not have shisha but they will accommodate your request if you would like one. If you are a large group of 20, you can request that the whole place be booked for you only. You can purchase alcohol and play whatever music you would like. The place also has a TV and AC's for when it's too cold/hot.

Their cashiers need to be better responsive to customers' requests. Both the front staff and cashiers seem aloof and uncaring. A smiley, friendly face is a dime a dozen over there.

Well done and keep it up.

review_text: This place is one of the worse restaurants I have ever visited. We went to this place after some friends recommended it as the best place to have a breakfast in Tripoli! We drove like 30 minutes to get to this restaurant. Service was super super slow, it took 45 minutes to bring two omelette!!! We were the only people in the restaurant though. The waiter was not friendly and all. The power went off for the entire time we were there, and the manager didn’t hurt herself to even apologize for that inconvenience. To make it worst, there was re-construction for the place, so we had to sit eat in the heat, and hear all the wonderful nose coming from outside. I do not recommend this place.

review_title: A wonderful little gem of a restaurant
review_text:
Our food wasn't the best in our all inclusive hotel in Hurghada and we found this little gem of a place round the corner.
Owner and staff were lovely and friendly and made us feel very welcome.
Restaurant was clean and very tidy.
The food was so delicious I had prawns, calamari dishes galore with all the trimmings.
On our last couple of nights they made our butterfly prawns nice and spicey for us nothing was too much for them
The fruit juices were made fresh and were perfect with the meals.
Thank you team you were all wonderful and made our holiday extra special.
Many thanks
Joanne Wright

address: Corniche, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We have been visiting Tyre on a friends trip and chose to have lunch in Salinas. Staff has been so nice, location is wide and comfortable, as well as very good looking, facing the sea and tyre beach. Last but not least, seafood and lebanese dishes are awesome.

address: Corniche, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
An excellent local restaurant, chefs do an excellent job with all of the many varieties of fish they offer.  Some say anyone can cook fish, yes, but these guys are really careful and consistent, so it is not over or undercooked.  Salads are also very good, and the grilled eggplant is amongst the best anywhere.  Chicken livers are also very well prepared.   
My most regular meal is a mezze, tabbouleh, rocca, grilled eggplant, chicken livers, and then some malefeh (small grilled barracuda), or sultan ibrahim fish.  
There is also a full range of meat dishes, and I had good lamb on skewers with a friend several times.

address: Old Saidon Souk Next To The Sea Castle, Sidon Lebanon
review_text: Amazing place at the castle of Sidon. Great scenery overlooking the sea and the famous Sidon castle. The restaurant was excellent with friendly staff and delicious food. It‘s convenient for snacks and for full meals. The dishes were tasty and well presented. It can accommodate large number of people. Higly recommend it.

address: Old Saidon Souk Next To The Sea Castle, Sidon Lebanon
review_title: Terrible experience at Saida Rest House
review_text:
Terrible food, terrible service!
I went with my family on the 8th of June 2025, at 9:00 PM, during Eid al-Adha. I understand that the staff might be tired throughout the day and especially in eid but it’s never an excuse to treat customers that way that we were treated!

First, the staff had grim and very grumpy faces, which was super unprofessional! We didn’t say anything, we ordered food and it came quickly ( not surprised since they had barely anyone there except us probably due to their terrible service).

We ordered variety of food from grilled chicken to hot meza, and everything was terrible, anyone that intends to go to such place would expect amazing food and service but both were non existent. We didn’t give them any notes to their food or service we just sat and enjoyed the view.

It all went down to when they placed the check, without us asking for it, but it was fine, it was 11:40 i think. We started dividing the total based on what everyone ordered. They kept coming every 2 minutes asking if the check was ready and we told them that we still need some time until it became 11:50 and the cashier that closes the system came and started shouting at us! At first we were shocked, earlier we asked one of the staff what time do they close he said that when all customers leave, so he didn’t have any right to come barging on us with his terrible attitude demanding to pay the check so he could close the system and go home! That’s your job and like it or not you have to wait, especially for such restaurant. Plus the restaurant says that they close at 12:00 Am so we weren’t passed that time!

And the manager in charge or the one responsible for the reception came and told us that the waiter that told us that they close whenever all costumers leave doesn’t know, which was illogical how would we know that he doesn’t know! His response represents the restaurant so if you wanna blame someone blame your staff and yourself for the ingenuity and unprofessionalism!

We paid and went out, on our way out other customers were leaving too and they also said “m3ash yshofo wejna” which means that they won’t see our faces again probably they had a terrible experience too!

So for anyone that thinks of going there, don’t, it’s not worth the money spent and you could spend way less amount for better food and better service elsewhere. Never again!!

address: Old Saidon Souk Next To The Sea Castle, Sidon Lebanon
review_text:
Service very slow !
Took 40 minutes between each dish to serve !! And 1.5 hrs to serve the main dish. Not recommended especially for family gathering. Bad experience and service

address: Castle of Saida, Sidon 1600 Lebanon
review_title: Zawat review ask alot before you order
review_text:
Very disappointed with our recent visit to Zawat. As frequent visitors to Lebanon and long-time patrons of this restaurant, we were shocked to be charged nearly $250 just for fish. The menu listed prices without specifying they were per kilo, the fish wasn’t weighed in front of us, and no price was confirmed before preparing the order. Locals in Saida confirmed we were overcharged — something that feels like we were taken advantage of as foreigners.

To make matters worse, we were charged for parking despite using the restaurant’s own facilities. We’ve dined at many other well-known restaurants across Lebanon, with larger groups and similar meals, and never paid anywhere near this amount.

It’s not about the money — it’s about feeling respected and treated fairly. Sadly, this experience left us feeling scammed and extremely disappointed.

Highly recommended!

address: Etoile Place Awaly Bridge, Sidon Lebanon

address: Etoile Place Awaly Bridge, Sidon Lebanon

address: Etoile Place Awaly Bridge, Sidon Lebanon
review_text: Stopped here for coffee as part of a tour. The sweets look fantastic. I had a pineapple croissant, which I can recommend!

address: Etoile Place Awaly Bridge, Sidon Lebanon

The patisserie has two branches one by the sea side and the other one in down town saida
The down town one is only a bakery that offers seating to relax from a days shopping have a hot or cold beverage with sweets including the famous kunafa
The one by the sea is a full resturant that offers hot meals besides the patisserie 

Both are very enjoyable that must be visited

address: Saida, Abra, Kharoubi Corner, Sidon 1600 Lebanon

address: Saida, Abra, Kharoubi Corner, Sidon 1600 Lebanon

address: Saida, Abra, Kharoubi Corner, Sidon 1600 Lebanon
review_text: Great food and service. The sushi is fresh and quick. The seevice is very friendly, the specially the lady from the philipine. Sadly they do not serve alchool. But still a good place.

address: Saida, Abra, Kharoubi Corner, Sidon 1600 Lebanon
review_text:
Afordable prices ..great selection of sushi .
The only good place in Saida to have a well made Sushi , variety.of salads .

address: Saida, Abra, Kharoubi Corner, Sidon 1600 Lebanon
review_text: Clean,fast, organized, very customer friendly service, price vs quality is great, free parking, fast delivery time, tasty meals, great for business lunch or dinner, a place you will visit again whenever you are in a sushi mode.

address: Alman Road, Sidon Lebanon
review_text: An elegant restaurant surrounded by green landscape... Beautiful open air terrace away from the noise ... Quality food and presentation...

address: Sea Road El Rmaileh, Sidon Lebanon
review_text: The fish was incredibly fresh and cooked to perfection—crispy skin, tender inside, and full of flavor. Easily one of the best fish I’ve had.

address: Sea Road El Rmaileh, Sidon Lebanon
review_text: An elegant place by the sea for seafood with a well decorated and neat seating area. As expected you get to choose the fish and ask for how you would like it to be cooked. They have some special starters that taste and look different. The ones with shrimps are a must try and they tasted delicious. We choose one big fish and had it grilled and cut and served. It was tender, juicy and made to our liking. A selection of fresh seasonal fruit and coffee were a great ending for a nice and productive business lunch.

address: Sea Road El Rmaileh, Sidon Lebanon
review_title: Good food but NO CARDs are accepted
review_text: I was surprised for such a good restaurant that does NOT accept credit cards, though the food is very good

address: Sea Road El Rmaileh, Sidon Lebanon
review_text:
Fresh delicious and friendly
The chief choose the fish with us and made them on his own way
The portions are generous
Its worthy 
Definetly will be my first choice for next visit

address: Sea Road El Rmaileh, Sidon Lebanon
review_text: This restaurant is excellent on so many levels. Food, location, view, atmosphere, decoration, service , etc. Highly recommended.

address: Salha House, Rachid Nakhle Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text: Great place. Visited for lunch. Outdoor sitting by the beach, great food and very friendly staff. Don’t miss the taboulés and spicy potatoes

address: Salha House, Rachid Nakhle Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text: After more than 16 years experience with this place! The only good thing about it, is the location.Have a beer enjoy the view and go somewhere else!

address: Salha House, Rachid Nakhle Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text: Appalling. Service non existent and just laughed at a broken glass to serve wine. Portion of calamari for 4 people consistent of 9 rings. I am shocked. The first time I have left a Lebanese restaurant hungry.

address: Salha House, Rachid Nakhle Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text: Although there is a small indoor space, the glory of this restaurant is the outdoor location.  Directly on the sea, the food is fresh and delicious. Served simply, it's best to order local fish.  It's not fancy but it is certainly memorable.

address: Salha House, Rachid Nakhle Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text:
The restaurant is on a platform overlooking the sea: a very good location but not if the wind is up!
Food was fairly priced but uninspiring after Beirut.

address: Al Fanar Street, Tyre 1107 Lebanon
review_title: Nice place with great choice of music
review_text: We celebrated my friend birthday and it was amazing, great choice of music, the stuff are adorable and helpful. If you want to spend a lovely night in TYRE it is the place to be.

address: Al Fanar Street, Tyre 1107 Lebanon
review_title: Great place but too loud music
review_text:
I really enjoy this restaurant, and the service however the only think I didn’t like is that they started playing loud music very early. I went to have dinner and drinks with friends and they started playing loud music around 8:30pm. That is crazy. I couldn’t even talk to my friends at the table. I do hope that they can start playing music later on when people are done with their dinners, but not before. I used to come here a lot before and had great dinners and after party experiences, but this is way too much. I hope the owner implement some changes thinking also about the well-being and harmonious experience of each client, and not just of those who were only want to party. You have a wonderful place to make wonderful memories, but give each clients the opportunity to have a good time without loosing their hearing. 

Thanks for your understanding and I hope you receive my feedbacks well and implement them for the success of your business and the collective health and welfare of your clients. Best wishes,

address: Al Fanar Street, Tyre 1107 Lebanon
review_text:
171/5000

address: Al Fanar Street, Tyre 1107 Lebanon

review_text: Great place for a drink at night with great owner, great music and atmosphere a mix of international and lebanese. Great!! keep going on my friend!

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text: I used to live in the same building, I used to order dinner some times. Internaitonal food, good quality and good sushi, but with high prices, because it is at the corniche of Tyre the most fashion and expensive area of town.

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text: This is not my first passby to this restaurant but first to try their sushi menu, the service is so fast the dishes are just so yummy 😋 and the kids enjoyed there time, the stuff are cheerful and friendly 👍

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text:
Very nice place, good location ,good service and best of all delicious Italian food . For the sushi lovers , it is the only place that serves it in town.
I recommend for sure.

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text: This is an asian resturant its good but the staff not good should change the staff but the suchi is very delecious and tasty hope to come agaian with another staff and manager will be come back a twice time for this place

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text:
I went with my wife to have sushi for dinner as we read the reviews about the good sushi they offer. The surprise was when the second piece was too crunchy with a loud noise to discover that there is a 1 cm piece of glass in the sushi piece!
The staff justification was that the glasses in the restaurant, when broken, they splash all around & probably that's why the piece is in the rice!!
I didn't dare then to eat more sushi as it may contain more glass.

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: This was a special service where you book a table of capacity of eight persons, then the chef will make cook for you from the tipinyaki menu, with nice cook show and soft music, this experience was extraordinary, the food was more than perfect, if you are on a special date or occasion or want to impress someone just go for it. 😋😍👍

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The place is nice and lively. The views are not stunning but are better than much of what you can get in Tyre. We didn't have lunch there as we were not appealed by Mexican food in Lebanon. We had two coffees instead. And it took ages to have them served. And even longer to pay. It was nice to see locals spending the afternoon there with food and sheesha.  A perfect place to chill a bit after a long journey from Beirut.

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Good place to meet friends beside the Med
review_text: I often meet friends in Tyre here at La Playa.  It used to have an upstairs section but this has disappeared.  They serve a limited range of Lebanese food - mezza, sandwiches and plated meals.  There are always a lot of people smoking shisha, usually outside on the terrace, but increasingly inside.  (I am anti-smoking).  Good for fresh juices, coffee and moderately priced food.  Service is friendly, polite and prompt.  Good views of the sea and the evening cruisers.

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Good cafe with sea view, and if you like Shisha this place would be good for you, the prices are more than others arround and best day to visit is SAT but you need to make sure to call before you go.

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We planned a visit after hearing so much about this place . When we got there it was pretty crowded but we managed to find a comfy quiet seat on the second floor .The service was very good the food was good with lots of varieties i would say there speciality is sea food ,the prices are a bit on the high side but still affordable .I would definitely go back !!

address: Corniche Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text: very good place where is possible to smoke and drink in total relax! very kind personnel! Great strawberry and berries fruit juice!

address: Corniche Street, Sur Lebanon
review_text: I came in with 4 of my friends and got lucky enough to find a seaview table, we were curious enough to try out the shishas and amazingly all of us loved their special Blu Love, unlike any other shisha. The dinner was great, abundant and reasonably priced unlike most restaurants around that area. We had a great time, the service was great and the staff were friendly. Highly Recommended!!

address: Seaside Road, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: It was a lovely friendly place
review_text:
It was a lovely friendly classy place, enjoyed the food, the arguileh and the tea 💚
For sure I would come back to this place 👍

address: Seaside Road, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The decor is gorgeous whoever decorated the place and you are also looking across at stunning sea views. The whole street is pedestrianised at the weekend too which is a great time to go and watch the world go by. I went in here on a sweltering hot afternoon for a refreshing mint lemonade and the best fattoush I've had on this trip. The staff were friendly and pleasant. I could have sat there all day but it is just a stone's throw from the Roman ruins. I highly recommend a stop off here before or after your visit.

address: Tyr Coast, Jarjouaa Lebanon
Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon

review_text: probably the worst starbucks we have visited: the toilets are extra dirty (a disaster) and the team not friendly...to avoid

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Great Barista service and very friendly staff. I am not sure why some of the popular Starbucks Drinks are not available! I'd blame poor management for not supplying the store with enough supplies to make it a viable place to get your drinks.  Overall, this is a very popular spot for young americanized Lebanese teens, students, and professional entrepreneurs to hangout. This place is my home away from home!

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: this has to be the worst starbucks in the world. i ordered a caramel frapp and they gave me a white drink with whipped cream and asked if i would like a small drizzle of caramel for extra money. it was literally like i ordered a milk drink? also the coffee is gross and is always warm not hot. dont even get me started on their attitude. such rude workers, they think they’re cool and dont know how to treat a customer. 🤦🏻‍♀️🤦🏻‍♀️ it was my first and last time

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Had the opportunity to visit Tyre in June 2022. Discovered YAM via our hosts. The food was amazing as was the service. No less than 8 fresh catches of the day prepared in a variety of ways. We ended up eating there two, or three times! 

Thanks again to the staff and owner for a memorable experience.

Don

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Yam makes the best seafood in Soor. We had grilled and fried fish — it was delicious and so fresh. The atmosphere is warm and friendly. It is very conveniently located - just a one minute walk from the seaside. The service is excellent, and the manager Ibrahim was great. I highly recommend this restaurant!

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: I've been several times and have always been impressed. Offering stunning views of the sea, Shawatina is bright and clean. The service is friendly and efficient. The food is freshly prepared to the highest standards. A very enjoyable experience!

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: What that terrible food and serve it is the last time i will visted here we think that the lebanese food was famous and good but we are unlucky about that hope evrey thing will be good in the future

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Nice restaurant in a very good location, facing Resthouse Hotel .It has a marvelous sea view. the food is so delicious . the staff is very friendly and the service is very good. if u like lebanese food , i recommed it .

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
You want a cheap and good food in Tyre?
Abou Deeb is a good option where you can enjoy shawarma (the beef one is good), cocktail and fresh juices, chicken sandwiches, fattouch...
The service is swift.
Prices are cheap and portions are fair.

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: they don't respect their customers and lousy quality of food especially abbasieh branch I don't recommend for anyone to have my terrible experience

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Our guide took us here prior to touring Tyre. It was a good choice. There was few other patrons due to Ramadan. Had a couple of appetizers. In particular, the tabbouleh had a great level of mint in it. We ordered from menus but you could also go up to the counter to get items. There was a good range of plates, sandwiches and appetizers. The place was very clean. It had tables inside and outside. The food came quickly enough, With a late arriving flight last night, it was a fine introduction into Lebanese fare. With a 7-Up and tip, lunch was just 10,000 LL, less than $7 US.

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Nice place where to share time with the local people
review_text: Nice and clean place with typical lebanese food, where to share the time with the locals, thought a little crowded at lunch time, mainly on the weekends. They have take away service. They have many different amazing fruit juices.

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Had lunch there with family. The place offers a good variety of sandwiches and Lebanese plates. Try the Chinese chicken. Very good prices.

address: Alfa Street, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
Nabih Berri Boulevard, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Superb food and ambience !!!
Very friendly staff and management !!!
Affordable prices !!!
Visit recomended

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Located on a nice sandy beach with the clear sea. People are very relaxed and nice. 
Plenty of parking space. 
We sat at the far end of the beach bar, closer to do sea.
Otherwise, live DJ music is loud if you want to chat with the friends. Not that it’s a bad thing as it brings atmosphere up but not the best choice if you want to chat.
The waiters are nice and friendly. The sea food was fresh and decently prepared.
The drinks, especially the ones with alcohol, are inconsistent. Every new order of gin&tonic had less and less gin in it.
It’s not that it bothered us much as we truly enjoyed the location and experience overall so visited two days in a row.
Absolutely amazing sunset!!!!

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
A good place to spend the day. the staff were friendly. Amazing scenery. It is nice to spend sunset time there.
The food is good and affordable

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
The Tyre beach may be Lebanon’s nicest beach. It’s clean (comparatively to others), has beautiful sand and clear water. The water warm like a bath. 

So, if choosing a spot to wile away the day, I recommend Cloud 59. The service is excellent but not pushy, and the drinks menu as well as food menu has something for everyone. 

Minimum spend per person $15US for unlimited time. (Min cost depends on seating chosen.)

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
If you want to enjoy the beach wkith a drink and tasty seafood,  it is good to visit this plcae.
The staff is cool and friendly.
The service is fast.
Prices of food are fair, drinks prices could be better

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Best spot for food and drinks in Tyre/Sour beach
review_text:
Sour Beach is among the cleanest and best beaches in Lebanon.. And  Cloud 59 is here to give u an upscale experience.
It is one of the few spots where you can have alcoholic drinks, and food is also very good.
It is preferable that you reserve specially if u want a table for lunch. Service is very good and cheerful. 
There's a minimum charge of 10 Usd (weekend) per person that you can spend on drinks or food.
Highly Recommanded.

address: Alalesa Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Fresh juices, delicious ice cream, good prices and a local atmoshpere
review_text:
You wanna feel the vibes of Tyre and its locals??
You wanna watch the typical Lebanese family outings: kids playing and parents enjoying themselves?
Niser Juice & Ice-cream have it all. The taste and the atmosphere combined with good prices. 
Did you have an enjoyable day in Tyre? Bathed in The sea and kissed sun? or strolled the ottoman souk and wandered in the colorful old Christian quarter? 
However your trip cannot end without refreshing yourself and boosting a bit your energy or your calories intake by visiting the Niser Juice & Ice-cream. Friendly staff, fresh yummy juice (especially the avocado) and  delicious Arabic/ Italian ice-cream. Whatever you choose Niser Juice will not disappoint you. 
Niser Juice & Ice-cream is the happy ending of a day trip to tyre. Order your juice then go sit at the nearby sea to and watch the sunset or relax in the air-conditioned hall of the Niser.

address: Sour Main Road, Al-Naqoura Iskandarounah, Naqoura Lebanon
review_title: Nice place with a lot to offer.
review_text: This is a nice beach-side restaurant with a lot of potential. The staff are very nice and service is top notch. It has a nice pool but as a woman I didn’t feel comfortable swimming in that atmosphere. The drink and desert menu needs serious improvements. They don’t keep their beach well-maintained. It left me wishing for more, much more!

address: Sour Main Road, Al-Naqoura Iskandarounah, Naqoura Lebanon
review_text:
Very nice location and quiet ! Near the Lebanese Israeli border! 
The sea view fantastic and the food delicious!! To be recommended

address: Sour Main Road, Al-Naqoura Iskandarounah, Naqoura Lebanon
review_text: Went here yesterday with a few friends. The resort is spotless and the pool is great very big. We had the mixed grill plate for $16 great value and delicious washed down with an ice cold beer. The staff are great and there is a lot of them, Well recommended it is a little expensive for the area but worth it. There also is changing rooms for the pool and showers. The views are excellent of both the Med and the mountains.

address: Sour Main Road, Al-Naqoura Iskandarounah, Naqoura Lebanon
review_text: Great quality food and a view to die for. Definitely worth a visit and will come again to spend the day on the beach .

address: Costal Road, Sur Lebanon
