review_text: Guys, Tyre is about 120 miles from Zgharta/ Tripoli!!!! Why do you advertise it as being in Zgharta ??

address: Borj Ech Chemali Lebanon
review_title: The best Asian food in Tyre !
review_text:
From the service to the food and ambiance, i adore this hidden corner in Tyre !
Its friendly and polite staff just add to the calm atmosphere. 
My suggestion, if you don't know what to order ask the charming older gentleman for the recommendation, he never fails to suggest the best and sometimes if you are lucky he might share some amazing stories with you.

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Great environment and very friendly staff. You must visit! Ask for Ghinwa, Mohamed and Khider. The food and desserts are delicious

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: I don't recommend this restaurant at all. Management lies about prices and quantity of food. They offer something and you don't get it or you get the one person quantity to many persons. Then you get the invoice with very high prices than many other better restaurants in the area. Food quality is not good. All this happened when a new owner took over.

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Very bad experience. Portions of one person are served to 4 persons. Very slow service. Prices are very high with respect to portions served. Some food that were in offers are not served but charged in the invoice. Very very bad management. Staff tries to be very helpful in spite of the bad management. The previous management/owner is the best. I don't recommend this restaurant to anyone.

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: a great breakfast with vast variety of foods. lovely location and decoration. service was great. I highly recommend this restaurant

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Best Lebanese trendy food in tyre
review_text:
Simply Delicious food  ,  Always fresh clean and same test  great customer service , I believe that this was my 10th visit ,I'm in love with this place  and highly recommended 
Ps don't forget to order kahloons  kenefah

address: Shawakir Cornish, Borj Ech Chemali Lebanon
Jimayzat Tyre Hosh main road, Tyre Lebanon
Port Entrance, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The best sushi in town.. Great service, they are very friendly. International cuisine.. a very delicious food😍😍 The atmosphere is very relaxing and satisfying. Recommended 100%.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Just beside Le Phenicien is Restaurant Tony a slightly shabbier but equally charming option. Tony, the owner of this small  white and blue decorated seafood restaurant, will greet you warmly and fry you up the latest catch.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Next door to Al Fakhoury and just as easily missed.
I had two excellent, in terms of choice, quality and price, meals there and I would highly recommend it.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
The restaurant is very small with about 4 tables. The owner spends most of his time trying to attract walkers/tourists to see his place. He doesn’t speak any English. It was an Ethiopian lady that was taken all the order and taking care of it. 

Location is very good. You can have sea view and see the fishermen. Then you can park the car in public space and walk around 750 meters or go almost to the restaurant, but than buy a parking ticket of 5,000 LBP. 

Food taste was mixed. Fresh sea bass fish was great! Grilled always. Hummus too oily and same was with baba ganoush. Cheese rolls were of good taste. Service ok. However it lacked a bit of that atmosphere one aims to see at the local place.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We came here twice, to eat the catch of the day. It's a small simple quite cheap venue. But a lot of ambiance.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The owner, Tony, is unprofessional. He didn’t give me my change back. Twice. He got angry when I said I just wanted salad. The view was worth the visit but there are so many restaurants to choose from!! Tyre is incredible, but this restaurant is NOT!

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Great service and the food is even better. Order seafood with a few mezes and your day will be perfect. Nicely situated too.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Located right on the harbor, Le Phenicien is one of Tyre's most upscale restaurants. Specializing in fresh seafood and fish, the restaurant is known for its great service, tasty food and amazing view.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We had a lovely dinner of mezze and a sea bream wrapped in lettuce leaves and grilled. Excellent location by the port.  Not to be missed.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
A good choice. A little expensive but the food is good. 
Very busy, it may be a good idea to make a reservation before.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: excellent local sublime cuisine fresh fish and Lebanese salads fantastic compliments ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ excellent wine professional and well organized staff compliments

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The food at Abo Dani is simply fabulous! Super fresh fish and great taste, it really is a very pleasant place. The prices are reasonable and we were warmly welcomed. He remembered us from a visit a year earlier. Definitely our favourite restaurant in Tyr! elyssa and dany

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Had a great night, eating A delicious and FRESH Black Grouper cooked by the owner. When there was confusion about our order, they took the unwanted food back, no questions asked. Had a much better meal than at the highly rated Phenicien!

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Excellent host, good prices and fish selection top notch
review_text:
Dany does it all at his restaurant: he welcomes you, shows you the day’s catch and helps you choose a fish, then hops into the kitchen and prepares it all. An excellent host, he explained how he has worked at restaurants in Russia, Belarus and Cyprus before coming home to open his own spot. The place is unassuming and that’s a big part of its charm. We had a bottle of wine, a fish to share, salad, 2 mezze and water for $40US. 

We’d recommend.

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Amazingg place from the ambiance to the staff to the best food ever ive tasted.Highly recommended especially to sea food loversss ♥️♥️♥️

address: Port, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Dany is very welcoming and his food is excellent 👌🏻 
We ate some fish or shrimp sandwiches with a very tasty sauce 👏😍

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: A cute restaurant by the square. It's a family business, the owners are there to greet you and take care of you. Dinner was nice, enjoyed the filet that was recommended to us. It can get quite busy, reserve ahead.

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: A nice place where to eat in Tyre
review_text:
One of the best places to have dinner in Tyre...
Well know for its steak and meat in general
Don't miss it during your week-end in Tyre

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We found the restaurant while checking trip advisor. It was our first time to visit Tyre, the restaurant is small but very cozy, modern but kept their traditional design( traditional Lebanese house). The food is very tasty from fish, shrimps to hummus and batata harra especially batata harra, their recipe is very delicious. As well, owners are very kind, friendly. Thank you for everything.

address: Port Entrance st. Qassam square, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: This is a family owned business, run by the family members. Farida greeted us, very friendly and warm lady. she made us feel very welcomed and we had a little conversation with her. Called the manager and he said "no problem and we agreed to be a simple lettuce and cucumber with No tomatoes. " 
3 meals arrived 1 didn't. When I enquired after 3 or 4 minutes . The waiter said , my medium steak takes longer than the well-done steak my friend ordered. Hmmmm
He brought the salad . Lettuce and TOMATO , no cucumber. I called the manager who insisted I ordered lettuce and Tomato. Hmmmmm I didn't 
 I ordered lettuce and cucumber and made it absolutely clear that I said NO TOMATO. . He eventually apologised and swapped it.
My stake came. ( Argentinian grass fed steak) I doubt it. It was not as tough as boots but by no means was  it lean/ soft.

address: Tyre's Old Souk Fishermen Port, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Absolutely the best ful in Lebanon
review_text:
You have to taste the famous Baroud ful! It is the most delicious preparation of this traditional Lebanese fava bean dish I have ever had. It is only rivaled by one other of their own dishes 'msabha', which is chic peas. And their hummos is the tastiest, creamiest, most addictive you will ever experience. I say 'experience' because eating at Baroud is beyond just eating. No matter how many times I taste it, I can't help but literally say 'oh wow, yum!' It is simple, yet oh so satisfying. Douse it olive oil and scoop it up with the freshly baked pita bread. Comes with a plate of sliced tomatoes, Lebanese pickles, onion and fresh mint. And then there are the olives--so good!

The service is friendly and super efficient. This is where the locals go to get breakfast. (open till around noon) The seating is basic plastic chairs at wooden tables situated in the busy souk. Delicious, fresh, traditionally made healthy food at very reasonable prices.

review_title: A Flavorful Bollywood-Inspired Dining Experience at Hitchki Mirdif
review_text:
Located in the heart of City Centre Mirdif, Hitchki offers a delightful fusion of traditional Indian flavors with contemporary twists, all set against a backdrop of Bollywood-inspired decor.

The restaurant boasts a chic and trendy atmosphere, featuring eclectic interiors adorned with antique pieces and vibrant lighting. It's a perfect blend of modern aesthetics and nostalgic charm, making it an ideal spot for both casual meals and special occasions.

Hitchki's menu is a celebration of global Indian cuisine, with each dish thoughtfully curated to offer a unique dining experience.

The drink menu is quite inventive, featuring options like the Minions (a guava and watermelon slush) and the Punjabi Patiala Lassi (a saffron-infused lassi topped with malai peda).

I decided to come and have a late breakfast (brunch) around 12:30PM/1PM when my parents decided to have lunch next door. Had their waffles with butter and maple syrup and cappuccino and it was amazing. Definitely right sized compared to the hotel which has smaller waffles at a buffet and right on the money for texture and sweetness. 

Customer service is also great too but I did find it a little slow at times, especially when I finished my food and had to ask the server for a check as I was running out of time and had to be somewhere. 

I do recommend this place whether it be breakfast or a snack. It's hard to find many cafes with full breakfast service, especially when it extends till 1PM (most will only have till 11AM at the latest). Moreover, reasonably priced food.

review_title: Don’t know why I waited this long! Great brunch
review_text:
I’ve been seeing their posts on social media but this was my first time to dine here. 

Virtually all day weekend breakfast with varied menu for all types of palates. I had the chicken and rice (solid 10!), scrambled masala, sharing pancake, and avocado scramble. Eggs were cooked perfectly. Servings are small so you can sample a lot of items without worrying about food waste.

address: Al Warqa 4 Near Grand Service Station, Dubai United Arab Emirates
restaurant_name: Lulu Al Bastakyah Al Kabab Al Khas
review_text: The Kabab Koobideh at Lulu al Bastakyah al Kabab al Khas (who named this place?) is without a doubt the worst of its kind I have had in my entire life. I was brought up a fervent believer that food is a blessing from God. So I avoid using words such as vile, revolting, disgusting, and the like to describe food; “worst” is probably as far as I’ve ever gone. With the exception of their Kabab Bahraini, itself over-peppered, all the other kebabs got a unanimous thumbs down from the group of Iranian kebab lovers I had lunch with. We ordered a decent representation of what Lulu makes, namely Chicken Koobideh, Meat Koobideh, Chicken Tikka Masti, Shishlik Lamb Chops, Joojeh Kabab and the Bahraini. All the kebabs, without exception, were over cooked and dry; the Koobideh to point of breaking apart when being served. Neither Koobideh was nice. The restaurant itself is something of a joke; claustrophobically dark and small, it’s also unbearably hot; the a/c unable to cope with the exhaust vents of the loud soft-drinks display fridge at the entrance. I suppose it makes more sense in the cooler months when one can sit in the small outside “garden” area, complete with fading, fake grass. The young Pakistani boy who sits behind the till is friendly and even speaks Persian but sorry, Jack; no amount of friendliness or Persian sweet-talk can make up for the substandard food this wannabe South Iranian restaurant fails to be.

address: Mirdif 37th street Next to Abaya Mall, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Ryan our server did a good job with us.  The place is newish to the Mirdif area of Dubai and in the middle of the residential area.  They deliver.  The store has an American diner motif but Country Music in the background and serves European potato fries?  Not joking.

I had the Comanche Burger.  Big patty, cheddar cheese, fried onion ring, beef bacon, and their 'Black Forest' sauce that is supposed to be a mushroom and blue cheese sauce.  The burger was fresh made and served hot.  Everybody knows blue cheese makes everything 'pop'.  I couldn't taste the blue cheese at all.  This made the burger disappointing.  My partner had the Bo Burger, a standard, near homemade style America burger, and she enjoyed her fare.

All said, I might go back because everything was fresh and hot, but not likely to have the Comanche.

address: Uptown Mirdif Uptown Mirdif Mall Algeria St, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Located in Ibn Battuta mall, we visited this beautiful restaurant for late lunch. The red and white theme of the restaurant interior gives it a bright and vibrant look. Felt a very positive vibes the moment we entered the restaurant.

We started with the Functional Juices and both were very refreshing & Heavenly in taste. Start your meals with these, highly recomended. Rise and shine which is a blend of Oats, Honey, Yougurt, Berries & Bananna. Made well without excess sweet and healthy. Kale & Pineapple with is Kale, Mango, Spinach & Pineapple. This has good health benefits for those who are looking for healthy options.

Mushroom Soup was very nice consistency and very thick. We loved the taste of the spices mixed with it. The Burratta salad is no doubt another best seller. Freshly made Burratta cheese which was very soft and tasty.

Grilled shrimp skewers on a bed of Sauteed veggies is another fantastic dish. The shrimps do not smell cause the marination is perfect and we get the taste of the mild marination spices mixed with shrimps. The Turkey Club Sandwitch is another killer. Taste of the Eggs with Veggies inside is superb. Grilled salmon with Quinoa was very well done and the Salmon did not smell strong but instead we can feel the taste in our mouths. Usually a strong smell of the Salmon is a out off, so the Chef has handled this very nicely. A must have for Salmon lovers.

The Apple and Celery drink is another superb drink. All the shakes and Smoothies are made by Mary Jane a trained expert in juices.

The Strawberry Pistachio Cheesecake was the one we selected from their display of mouth watering Desserts. The tangy sauce with the sweetness underneath was a riot of taste in our taste buds. Later we had their Pistachio & Caramel Ice Creams. All these Desserts are a must have to satiate your cravings.

The restaurant supervisor Mary Jane deserve a special mention, her mannerisms and knowledge about the food were praiseworthy.

Overall we had a great experience here. Service is good. Taste, quality and presentation of the food is praiseworthy.

address: 38 62B St Mirdif, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
When you see shawarma everywhere you go in Dubai, sometimes it is nice for a change and that is what you will find at Doner Kebab.

Doner is all about thin slices of meat combined with delicious sauce and that is exactly what is served at Doner Kebab.

The thing I like about doners is the openness of the wrap where you can have immediate access to the meat and not have to hunt around, hoping that you would find a piece the way you would work a shawarma.

The meat itself is tender and has enough flavor marinated into it that works asking with the natural juices from the meat and is simply delicious.

Doners are delicious and puts up a strong fight against shawarma, tacos and the likes.

I can easily have one meal a week at Doner Kebab.

review_title: Disgusting Seating Area & Terrible Service, Rude Staff who talks about you in their native Tagalog language!!
review_text:
Not sure what I can exactly pin point here in this review because there's nothung good about Sushi Nations (Uptown Mirdif Branch) . 
Let me start with:
1) The hygiene issue: It's None Existent! Seating Area is gross, stains all over the sofas, the tables are dirty, even glasses are stained and not properly washed, probably just rinsed off before it is being used to serve another customer and that's it!!
2) The food: I can honestly say it's not good at all. It taste awful. 40% of the sushi rolls were canned tuna!!! and another 40% were crab and only 20% were veggies and salmon rolls. 
Now, the soups, they smell and taste like garbage!!
And it will take you around 1.5 hours till you get the first appetizer mini bowl. 
Then you'll have to wait 2 more hours until the sushi rolls are ready!! 
I got food poisoning when I went to their restaurant, started vomiting and had severe diarrhea right afterwards.
3) The service: When you first come in, if you're lucky, there will be 1 staff in any of the corners that will acknowledge you.
Then you pick a table, sit down and just wait and wait for a staff member to come in and take your order.
And from what it seems to me and to the group of friends I was with, that the staff don't like to work there. They are so depressed, slowly moving around and so irritated.
They talk down to you and with a loud voice. The Filipino chef there was making fun of me and my friends, IN FRONT OF ME!!! Saying demeaning things about Arabs, even though they live and work in an Arabic country, in Middle East, without even any regards or respect to me and my friends as customer and without even considering that I, maybe, speak Tagalog. 
I have actually took many courses, specifically to understand the language of many UAE residents and to call them out when they mock us, in their mother tongue, in front of us, assuming we are so oblivious and have no idea what they are talking about!!!! #SHAME

review_title: This is the first place I
review_text:
This is the first place I have seen that actually makes really good pizza and really good burgers at the same place. 

They keep the menu simple and do everything exceptional well. The pizzas are a healthy medium size with all the right ingredients and a thin floor and that flame oven taste. 

The burgers are as well on the small/medium side and really well put together. Fresh lettuce and juicy meat. What seems to be handmade sauces and well thought out combinations. 

And the fifth star is for the dynamite prawns - no idea why they are on the menu - but they are amazing. Not just some small shrimps tossed in some ready made sauce / no these are proper prawns cooked with talent and tossed in delicious sauce. 

Only for delivery and take away. Even the delivery speed was great every-time. I have and will recommend this place to everyone who loves a great pizza or a awesome burger.

address: 47 Street, 2 Mirdif, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Shiraz Special Kabab is a food court based Iranian restaurant in Mirdif. In fact, it’s probably one of the best food court based Iranian restaurant in town. Based in the Uptown Mirdiff Mall food court, it holds its own against Lebanese heavy weight neighbours Zaroob and Automatic. I think it’s the best designed of the lot as well. The restaurant was recommended to me by two of my swear-by food buddies, Sam and the mighty King of Kong, Mansoor. They both said the kababs at Shiraz were better than at Ostadi, my favourite place in the world for kabab masti.

I ordered their mixed grill for two which comprises of the following kababs: Chicken Masti, Bahraini, Jūjeh, Koobideh Chicken and Koobideh Meat. The kababs are served on a bed of white rice with a mohawk of saffron yellow down the middle. In addition to the kabab and rice you also get Soup-e Jo (barley soup), Mast-o-Khiyar (a yogurt-based cucumber raita), Shirazi Salad and Lavash flatbread. That’s a lot for under AED70. I also ordered a Mirza Ghassemi, a smoky aubergine-based dish which is one of many dishes suitable for vegetarians.

The chicken masti was really good; the chicken soft, juicy and tender but it wasn’t anywhere near Ostadi’s. Everything else was easily as good though, especially the jūjeh and the koobideh, which I thought was actually better. All the condiments (mast-o-khiyar, soup-e jo, etc.) were also very good.

A friendly, bubbly Filipina by the name of Evelyn took our order (happy staff make all the difference). Also noteworthy was the Filipino chef who prepared our kababs; he was a real master. I particularly liked the way he would regularly pick up a skewer off the fire to inspect. When they were done, he brushed them with melted butter before serving – brilliant. If you like good, authentic Iranian kababs this restaurant will not disappoint you. I haven’t tried their stews, but I am told they are very good too. Based on the meal I had there, I find that entirely believable. Worth a visit. 

Hey! Now read this –
Xerxes physically dines at, or orders from, each and every venue he reviews. He pays in full for whatever he and his companions eat, drink, take away or occasionally throw at each other. Xerxes accepts no money, gifts, discounts or free meals in return for reviews or favouritism. What you have read was NOT influenced in any way by the venue.

review_text: Menagerie's most formidable talent lies not in the expertly(and exceptionally) prepared food, the tastefully decorated interiors or its applaudable soundtrack ( playing indie-hits like Lord Huron and Janis Ian) but rather in its ability to just 'be'. It doesn't try to propagate a faux sense of ostentatiousness or boast about its approach to healthy living. In this exclusive little mall called Mirdif 35, Menagerie is a place untouched by the too-easily damaged veneer of golden consumerism that plagues 99% of tinsel Dubai. As a regular customer, and a fan of everything from their raw doughnuts(vegan) to almond-milk lattes with agave and the refreshing acai bowl. Menagerie 35 exists to better your day, or at least that has been my personal experience. It is also worth mentioning that it is one of the only cafes where one can read a good book, unbothered.

address: Al Mizhar 43c St, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Hatam __ A lovely Iranian Restaurant in Dubai! They serve complimentary bread and green salad platter that consist of rocket leaves,mint leaves,walnuts, olives,feta cheese, and pickled veggies upon arrival.

I used cobone vouchers for my meal. This cobone deal is still on. You can save upto 50% on the bill by getting the vouchers. The menu card prices available on Zomato website for this restaurant need some updating as these are different from the prices mentioned on the Menu card at the restaurant.

I had the following dishes:

Lentil soup:  It was non spicy and tasted good.

Mixed salad: Though they serve basic salad platter complimentary but this 4 in 1 platter was really good. It had hummus, mutable, tabbouleh and seasonal salad in it.

Mixed Hatam salad: consisted of  dolma, humus topped with grilled meat, feta cheese and green mix salad. Loved it!

Arayes: Arayes is a middle eastern grilled pita bread stuffed with lamb mince, onions, tomatoes and spices. It was very delicious! But they forgot to serve fries with it as it was shown in the menu card’s Arayes picture.

Kabab Koobideh:  Special Iranian kababs served with rice were very juicy and tasty. They make the best koobideh kababs!!

Tikka and kabab:  Marinated lamb pieces served with rice were nicely grilled.

Falooda:  It was very chilled and refreshing!

Overall it was a nice experience. Seating facility is good. Walls are adorned with beautiful paintings. They also have family cabins for privacy. Our server was kind and friendly. He was happy to help us when we asked him for the extra plates and cutleries. I would love to come back here again.

#littlepakistanikitchen #instafoodie #dubaifoodies #arabiancenter

I would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a good feed, especially families.

review_title: A one of a kind restaurant

You will experience a very welcoming atmosphere with Lebanese music played in the background while you explore different dishes and appetizers. Having your lunch here is a must if you find yourself wandering nearby. Don’t forget to taste every single dish cuz the more you try Lebanese food, the more you’ll fall in love with this fascinating country.

Un repas exceptionnel vous attend dans ce restaurant. Il donne sur un lieu sacré qui est considéré l’un de premier lieux de pilgrimage au liban. Vous allez recevoir de l’énergie positive dès que vous arrivez ici. N’oubliez pas d’essayer chac plat et chaque apéro pour que vous puissiez avoir une expérience totale de la cuisine libanaise

review_text:
Behara has hospitality experience in Switzerland and Dubai that I know of. He took over the extended family restaurant that has been around for 82 years a year ago when he bought out his uncles. He's converted it into a Lebanese/International locale with abundant charm! 

His mother makes the kebnehnayyeh fresh each morning and his dedication and passion are clearly evident. Everything we ate was fresh and served just right. If you're nice he may even bless you with a drop of his homemade vodka with cedar sap. Retsina like but so much better 🤩

Don't miss stopping here for any meal including breakfast and don't miss a walk through the park across the street to see the incredible cedar trees. It's worth the drive without a doubt. Bravo!!🙌

address: Old Souk, Pepe Abed Street Facing historical Castle main gate, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
So, my family and friends are visiting lebanon l, not for the the first time.  We have been to many places and restaurants,  and everyone was great and nice to us.  

After touring around the area,  we decided to go to this place to have a drink,  eat some appetizers and sweet dishes. When we entered the place,  they didn't say any welcome greetings. We wanted to place an orders,  but after knowing that we don't want to eat main dishes,  he told us that he is "not interested in only serving us if we don't order main dishes". Then he told us to "leave and go somewhere not here" in front of everyone.  Everyone was looking, and it was super embarrassing. 

I will never bring anyone to this place ever. The way we were treated and possibly racially profiled wasn't OK. I will bring my family and friends who are coming to lebanon to better places.

Ksaar (for me and my family) is an annual must have dining every year we vacation in Lebanon.

The cuisine is plentiful with a wide variety of choices from Lebanese / Italian & generally Mediterranean options including grilled and seafood platters.

Kids menus are also available with a bonus playground onsite and monitored by restaurant staff, however, parental or nanny supervision is highly recommended for 
young children till the age of 7 in my opinion.

The local delicacies such as sujok and mhamraa, and kubbeh are fantastic with a glass of beer ;) Pizza's, fries and burgers are also available along with pasta  

Ksaar also has outdoor seating for those with a need of a hooka bubbly.

I recommend Ksaar and rate it highly.

We checked out this place following a recommendation by locals. It’s very close to the main touristy area of Byblos(Jbeil), but remains off the beaten path. If not walking there from the main souk and sites, the Uber ride is an affordable 4$! It’s along the main road to the Saint Charbel Monastery.

This is where the locals go for fresh, clean, affordable and tasty Lebanese food. We were two and split the fattouch (delicious), hummus, and the mixed grill. Everything is of top quality and the soujouk kebabs were particularly excellent. 

We would definitely come back and recommend Kabablaki as a place to check out and eat where locals eat.

We ate at the restaurant and it seemed to attract a lot of delivery orders (takeaway).  I  believe they have convenient hours since it stays open late.

Absolutely disappointing experience at El Molino.

The tortilla bread had a moldy smell, which was honestly unacceptable. When we pointed it out, the staff took their time to respond and didn’t even bother to ask if the replacement was okay.

address: Old Port Houssami bldg, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
El Molino – A Slice of Mexico in the Heart of Byblos
Tucked in the charming old town of Byblos, El Molino is a refreshing and vibrant escape that brings the bold and colorful flavors of Mexico to the Lebanese coast. From the moment you step inside, you’re greeted by warm hospitality and a festive atmosphere that blends Mexican décor with a Mediterranean twist.
The menu is thoughtfully curated, offering a range of traditional Mexican dishes—from sizzling fajitas and loaded nachos, to authentic tacos al pastor, burritos, and enchiladas. Every dish bursts with flavor and is clearly crafted with fresh, quality ingredients. One standout is the guacamole, freshly made tableside and served with crispy homemade tortilla chips. Their margaritas—especially the spicy version—are not to be missed, offering the perfect balance of heat and citrus.

Service is friendly and attentive, with staff happy to explain dishes or offer recommendations. The ambiance is lively yet cozy, ideal for both casual outings and special celebrations. Whether you’re seated indoors or enjoying the outdoor terrace, the vibe remains inviting and relaxed.

review_text:
We were walking around Jbeil at around 12 noon. Too early for lunch but wanted just to sit for coffee. Some guy there invited us to sit at sultan al mina. From the minute we stepped in we knew we made the wrong choice but still thought for a coffee with a sea view cant really be too bad. But it was.  The place was almost empty. At the entrance was a couple of cats eating into a plastic bag. Turned out it was the fish just delivered which will be later served to clients.  We sat down and the floor under the table was dirty. address: Port Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: undoubtedly the best fatoush of my life so far
review_text: We stumbled on this place due to their signs by the roadside. What luck and what a revelation. We had a set meal: the hummus was perfect, the babaganoush was to die for. Because we were wondering about it, we were shown how they smoked the aubergine. The fatoush was just incredible and contained such a variety of herbs leaves, some of which we didn't recognise. A perfect balance. Then long double cooked chips, calamari which was cleanly crisp outside and succulent tasty with no rubberiness. It was served with a variant of tartare sauce, pleasantly acidic. Then we had a variety of crisply fried smallish fish. Utterly delicious, too much for me. When we thought this was over, they brought us a delicious, crisp fried sweet pie, like a rose water flavoured Greek galactoburico. We were in heaven. it is all fresh, much of it home produce, the owners are fishermen. It cost less than half the price of restaurants on the harbour, yet this is just yards from the sea and so friendly.  One of those finds I would not tell you about if I lived more locally!

address: Port Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text: Our driver bought us here for lunch as he knew the owners. The restaurant was empty but clean and well maintained. They put on a fabulous spread of mezze and fresh seafood. (They didn’t give us a menu the driver asked them to bring us a range) Everything was fresh, well seasoned and delicious. The bill was $90 total for three of us with soft drinks but we had so much food we could justify the price. Thank you Amal for cooking such a lovely home cooked meal.

review_text:
Just to tell you that before working in taxi business I was a restaurant director for 12 years).

I tried before this restaurant and it was one of my preference. 3 weeks ago I decided to invite someone for a small lunch ... because we have a business meeting after one hour .
My friend asked for a pizza and one beverage and I asked for shish taouk on tabliyeh ... 20mn later my friend got his pizza ... and me ??? nothing.
Before finishing his pizza , my friend proposed me to eat the last slide of the pizza ... I did because I was hungry... What about my order : Nothing..
Guys... Normally when  we  2 persons enter a restaurant to eat, they shall eat together ... right?
As I learned during my career that the Client is a KING.. In this case the KING became ... ?
.... I told them that I don't need anymore my order.. because the business meeting is in 10 mn .. and I asked for the bill ...
The director or maybe the owner* bring the bill without any apologies.... This is the KING that I am
... and this is the professionalism that he is ..
I paid with pleasure... but the note if I can put is  - 10  .. and NEVER AGAIN...

review_title: Rude owner, careless, insulting and a bully
review_text:
My partner and I like to sit next to each other so we talk quietly and share a nice experience. The owner approached us towards the end of our lunch and told us change our seating habit, this is after we finished eating and are just chilling enjoying our time. We were taken by that comment and chose to leave a few moments later. There were 4 men behind us downing scotch, talking loud on their phones while eating, nothing wa said about them. We paid and left but in retrospect lots of actions could have taken. Instead of making a scene, we called the owner on the way asking for reasons for his tone, he said "it's my restaurant and I can do as I chose, and for us not to come back again, hangs up the phone" 

Could be an identity crisis issue he is facing  but if u ask me and you are a respected client, specially a woman, the owner may have some issues...

A time like this when we support our local businesses, only to be insulted, disrespected, and humiliated in front of others with no real reasons given, I can only phathom why some men behave this way towards women in this day and age.

address: Seaside Road Jbeil District, Amchit Lebanon
review_title: A gem seafood restaurant in Amchit
review_text: This is a very small restaurant that is part of a fishery on the coast of Amchit north of Byblos. The fishery is well known under the name of its owner Pierre Abboud but few people know that it is also a restaurant. The place is very small (8 tables only) and there is no decoration. Actually you can’t even see it from the road because it is hidden by a tree. But the food is so good. Of course, since Pierre is a fisherman you get the catch of the day. All fresh and perfectly cooked  and seasoned. We had a selection of calamari with garlic and parsley, fried fish and broiled sole. Accompanied by hummus and fattoush. It was all delicious. Service is fast. And the surprise: prices are way cheaper than the trove of fancy seafood restaurants dotting the Byblos-Amchit coast. Definitely a hidden gem.

address: Main Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_text:
As a Byblos resident living 5 min away from Mon Maki, I would caution against visiting this restaurant due to the alarming inconsistency in their food quality. The last time I ordered for pick-up, the sushi was horrible, and I'm not exaggerating when I say "horrible". Specifically, I ordered sushi rolls that included salmon, mango, and avocado. The mangos and avocados were not ripe at all. I'm talking apple textured mangos and rubber textured avocados. It's unbelievable that they could even have the audacity to serve them like that. Almost as if it was done on purpose. And to top it off, the rice was also undercooked, resulting in a horrible overall experience. We were 3 people and we couldn't eat more than two pieces.
I don't usually write reviews for restaurants, but with their ridiculous prices, I felt the responsibility to share my honest experience and warn other people. Especially after reaching out to the restaurant to express my concerns, and all they did was reply to me with "we'll call you back in a bit" (they never did btw).

review_text: We had a dinner for two me and my wife, the service was excellent, we were welcomed first by a guy name Mazen and he was extraordinary & generous when he offered complimentary   fresh lemonade , later we served the food by a very warm waitress names Clarita who was helpful and attentive in every detail, the lasagna was out of this world (oven baked) , the margarita pizza was yummy as well , but the icing on the cake was the tiramisu 🤤 a must try hidden gem when you visit the area

address: Mraba'a Street, Byblos Lebanon
review_title: Hats off, from a usually fussy client!
review_text: The overall best place in Byblos if you like authentic, fresh and delicious Italian cuisine. This is the WOW place, I would say. Aesthetically a joy for the senses. The beautiful decor, the comfortable chairs, a cozy lighting and a busy open kitchen! You would commit a sin if during your stay in Byblos you don't have at least one meal here. I had a delicious freshly made Tiramisu here with an ice Americano. The Tiramisu is a one way ticket to heaven! Then I spotted a waiter grinding fresh pesto to go with freshly made bread, another work of magic. Highly attentive, courteous yet professional servers. It was delightful to see a number of tables celebrating reunions inside and on the outside patio of the restaurant. A kids' cubby house with a slide would keep parents happy and kids safe while healthy and fresh food is served. I wish I was staying longer in Byblos to sample their entire menu. Hats off, from a usually fussy client!

address: 287 Maarad street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Duo has always been a great restaurant but their Broumana branch was a little disappointing. 
From a location perspective, it is actually very nicely located on the main road overlooking the heart of the action. The experience is really a nice experience to be on one of the tables at the border of the seating area.
The menu is mostly international with meals for every taste and a daily speciality. They had Escalope on the day I visited and it was not bad. 
The disappointing factor is mainly the prices. Everything is overpriced. A can of Pepsi is for LBP 5,000 (USD 3.5), which is a complete rip off when you actually can buy it for half a dollar
The food is nice but also quite overpriced for the quality and the taste. 
The service does not mark high neither. It was a little slow and took time for them to come and take out order. Also, I was surprised with the cleanliness of some of the utensils they provided us.

DUO brags on being a unique restaurant but my experience with the Broumana branch was quite disappointing. I recommend you stay away from this place until it gets better. You actually have a ton of other choices in the area at a walking distance.

***Please like this review if you find it helpful. It has been drafted to provide you insights on your next trip or visit from the perspective of a fellow consumer and traveler***

address: Rue Daaboul, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Non Traditional Breakfast with Fresh Cunchy & Stuffed Meat Sandiwches, Friendly Atmosphere, Hidden Gems of Beirut,
review_text:
Background:

It was recommended by one of my friends. His father used to take him there when he was a kid. We went together with other friends and we had our sandwiches feast. It is a ''non – traditional'' Lebanese way of having breakfast.
It is not a man2oushe nor hummus and foul. 
The breakfast here is sandwiches that range from the typical labne and cheese to meat sandwiches such as sujuk (sausages), roast beef, Sawda (the kidney of the chicken) and lsenet (roasted cow tongue).

What to Expect:

It is an old sandwich shop in Beirut:
-The place is small
-seating is basic: stool and aluminum bar
-decoration is minimal. 
-Suitable for a group of friends or a take away for the family.

My own Experience:

The sandwiches I tried were roast beef, sawda and sujuk. The three were crunchy and freshly baked but more importantly stuffed with meat and hence you enjoy the real taste of the sandwich.
I loved how he adds  fresh mint for all the sandiwches

Wait for the owner to call your number and stay aware of it as he will be asking you if you want it spicy or not? with mayonnaise mustard and pickles. The owner is friendly and has his own way in interacting with customers. 

The atmospheres is nice and friendly. Standing with other people waiting for your number and accordingly for the delicious sandwiches!!! A spontaneous chat with the one next to you is unavoidable.

Recommendations:

You have three sizes of sandwiches:
-Half a sandwich (recommended) (2$)
-A medium size sandwich 
-And a large sandwich (4$)
 
 I recommend you to have othe half sandwich option and order two different sandiwches. Afte tthe two i  can assure you you will be satisfyingly FULL!!!
If you want to take it further try the desserts sandwiches which are usually served at home such as jam and halawa

Timing: it is good to go there for breakfast or brunch. He closes in the early afternoon when all the food is sold out.

How to get there: Hidden in the narrow alleys of corniche Al Mazraa. Ask the taxi to drop you next to Paindore and when you reach paindore ask anyone and they will show you. (1 min walking distance)

address: Place Du Musee, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Magical Candy Store from a Different Era
review_text:
Have you ever wanted to go back in a time machine to a more innocent time, Before Things Got So Bad? In Lebanon, that means before the currency devaluation, before the economic free-fall, before the political sclerosis and corruption, before the port explosion, before Covid, and before the Civil War.  

Le Marzipan, located in a little street right across from the National Museum, is just such a magical spot.  Founded in 1964, its owners have literally never left the building. When the place was shot up during the 1980's civil war, they insisted on rebuilding it EXACTLY as it was. The traditional candy cooker in the back is still used every day, and their traditional chewy liquorice and caramels are still amazing. The woman, who speaks French and English, will welcome you with a timeless Middle Eastern hospitality.  The cost for a big bag of candy –pennies to foreign tourists– will help keep a precious memory of Old Time Beirut alive.  YOU MUST SEE THIS PLACE! It's only a few meters from either the National museum or the MIM minerals museum.  Fantastic!

That's why I will consider my last experience as an accident and I will give this place another try.

address: Iben Rouchoud Tal'et Chehadeh, Beirut 2049 1111 Lebanon
review_text: I like this Knafeh since 1970 , A super Lebanese sweet , This Knafeh like home made , you will eat much

address: Iben Rouchoud Tal'et Chehadeh, Beirut 2049 1111 Lebanon
review_text:
This hidden gem is a must if you like knafeh. I know it from the lebanese youtube channel "no garlic no onion" which is an expert of all the best not known places where to eat in lebanon, and my friend he is right. It took me ages to find the place, since is not on google maps nor anywhere else, I had to use bits of information in the web plus asking the local people living in that area.
The staff are very welcome and speak also a bit of english which helped the communication with my limited knowledge of arabic; they serve the traditional knefeh in a sesame bap, with plenty of sugar syrup. They also offer take away service and they can pack medium or big portion for you to travel.
The knefeh is just mouthwatering, try it!

address: Mar Mkhayel Junction, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Great restaurant in Beirut overlooking Mar Michael church. It has many branches in Beirut but this is the largest one. It serves all meals as well as snacks and take away. Very wide selection of dishes . All dishes we tried were excellent and so tasty. If has a lovely terrace and nice atmosphere. Staff are friendly. Great parking facilities. Highly recommended.

address: Mar Mkhayel Junction, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Never again!
Yesterday, my family and I decided to celebrate my little girl’s kindergarten graduation at Al Jawad Restaurant-golf branch. Unfortunately, after finishing our dinner, we asked for a simple cupcake with a candle to surprise her and our experience was deeply disappointing:
1- Excessive Wait Time: We patiently waited for over an hour for a simple cake with a candle. Each time we inquired about the delay, the waiters assured us it would be ready in “just two minutes.” However, those two minutes stretched into an eternity.
2- Rude Behavior: The waitstaff’s attitude was shockingly impolite. They dismissed our concerns and seemed indifferent to our frustration. Not once did they apologize for the delay or acknowledge our inconvenience.
3- Refusal to Cancel Order: Despite the prolonged wait, where my kids almost fell asleep at the time of the surprise, the restaurant refused to cancel our order. When the cupcake finally arrived, it was inedible—far from the celebratory treat we had hoped for.

address: Mar Mkhayel Junction, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Four day trip to Beirut Lebanon . We went there twice. Food was amazing. Service was incredible. Rooftop area is amazing. Must try. And don’t pay attention to these other reviews. They were talking about stupid stuff go there and try it you will love it!!

review_text: The food is excellent, with a great range of options (even though both times I visited, quite a few options were not available). Cannot fault the flavor and quality of the food. address: Tannoukhiyen St, Beirut 961 Lebanon
review_text:
The restaurant is a bit hidden under an appartment block at the end of Hamra street, but well worth going. It is a very colourful place that is open 24/7, and serves vegan food exclusively. I’m an omnivore, but the taste of everything that was served was divine and at a great price.

They serve sandwiches, soups, mezze, pizza and pasta, and I was spoiled for choice. I took the lentil soup and the server recommended Intake the vegan cheese sticks with it. The sauces that were served with the cheese complimented them perfectly, especially the cashew-based sauce was finger-licking good. The soup was comforting and served with a fattoush salad. I also took one of the cheesecakes as dessert. Each dish cost around 140,000, which is very reasonable for the quality served

I highly recommend this place if you want some quality lebanese vegan food (or even if you’re an omnivore like me, because I didn’t miss anything on my plate).

The meat loaf is prepared Tripoli style and taste good .Unlike competition they do not offer vacuum packing for travel despite the fact that they are not cheaper. . Overall recommended if passing by for convenience alternatively opt for Hallab or Bohsaly much better options.

review_text: This Lebanese pastry specialist establishment has succeeded to build a good reputation in Beirut through the past years. It was time to relocate and open a new store. Recently they have a promotion between 5 & 8 everything is half price. I passed by around 6 unfortunately all items containing ashta were already gone. I settled for some house made chocolate sablets with a couple of almonds ones  in addition to a box of petit four. The deal is really great price wise. But I have to say that the taste was just average. They weren't bad but not to compared to the high quality French pastry shop in town. Maybe I should have settled for Lebanese pastries.

review_text:
Gia la Cucina d'Amore is a hidden gem in the heart of the city. The moment you step inside, you're transported to a charming indoor garden with lush greenery and a relaxing atmosphere. The space is beautifully decorated and makes for a perfect backdrop for a romantic dinner or a night out with friends.

The food is just as impressive as the setting. The menu offers a variety of classic Italian dishes made with fresh, high-quality ingredients. We highly recommend the lasagna and tiramisu - they were both incredibly flavorful and satisfying.

The staff at Gia la Cucina d'Amore are knowledgeable and attentive, making our dining experience all the more enjoyable. Whether you're looking for a special night out or a casual dinner with friends, this restaurant is a must-visit. We can't wait to come back and enjoy the indoor garden atmosphere again!

address: Roucheid El Dahdah, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
As soon as we stepped inside, the atmosphere was welcoming, and beautifully designed and set the tone to a memorable meal.
The perfect balance between cozy and elegant. The lighting, music, and decor all contributed to a relaxed, enjoyable environment that made us feel right at home.

The food was equally impressive. Every dish was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly cooked. You can really taste the care and quality in each bite. The quinoa and  shrimp was a standout, rich, and beautifully presented and the hummos with herbs was also very delicious.
In the other side, it was my first time tasting the fattet fawaregh, and I was honestly blown away. From the very first bite, the flavors were rich, perfectly balanced, and unexpectedly satisfying. The texture, the seasoning, the aroma, everything came together in the best way. I didn’t expect to love it this much, but it quickly became a new favorite. It was one of those meals you remember long after the last bite.

The staff were friendly and attentive without being intrusive. Overall, a fantastic dining experience. Highly recommended for anyone who appreciates great food in a great setting!

address: Monot Street, Beirut 0000 Lebanon
review_title: One of the last still standing nightclubs in Beirut
review_text:
Nestled in one of the smallest streets in Ashrafyeh, Almodovar quickly became one of the busiest pub/night clubs in Beirut. And there’s a reason for this. 
To start with the food is quite interesting with modern fusion Mexican menus and is better than one would expect in a night club. Literally everything is presented with exotic dips that will make your taste buds dance throughout the meal. Also, they have one of the best drinks menus for such a venue. You can order almost any kind of drink or cocktail and you will surely get it. 
However this NOT what makes Almodovar really interesting. The secret is at post 10pm this very particular place transforms into a dancing floor where one can dance to one hearts delight. The music selection is exquisite and on point and the crowds will definitely go wild. THIS is Almodovar’s little secret and it’s a good one. 
Still, a few little details can make or break the mood. For one, some high tables are too small and uncomfortable especially if it’s a group of 3+ pax. Also, on some special nights it is truly overcrowded. Claustrophobic people should avoid. 
Still it is a must do experience. Word of the wise make sure you reserve a good table. My personal recommendation is to try it on a Thursday or Sunday. Both are exceptional nights there. Enjoy!

review_text: Great place. they have a lot of variety of food and so delicious. all kind of drinks with a lot of cocktails and great great music with affordable prices. you will enjoy a lot...

review_text:

The dishes are quite similar to what you find in Lebanese restaurants but most of the recipes are cooked in a different way for example Kebab with sweet and sour cherry sauce. We ordered 4-5 different dishes and every single dish was really delicious. The price is ok, not overpriced but also not cheap. We paid roughly 60 USD for 2 person. 

The dessert was offered complimentary to us which was a nice detail.

address: Rue Huvelin Near Yared Pharmacy, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: What though on hamely fare we dine,  A man’s a man for a' that
review_text:
If you'd like to taste well-prepared Lebanese fare, or the best pizza's and drink the only beer you'll find in Beirut - well-chilled Al-Maza, occasionally with a specialist lager - or a reasonable wine, whisky, whatever, then drop in to Germanos, around the corner from Peres Jesuites' church in Saifi, Tabaris, the start of Achrafieh - call it what you will!  Outside drinkers will be a mix of Lebanese and Celtic Englishmen (if that makes sense), often with other Europeans and an assortment of professions from retired writers and active journalists - the Times correspondent can be found here, to one of the most celebrated Lebanese Forces strategists ever to be glad it's all over now!  
    In short, the conversation is mesmerising and the food more than acceptable. Should cars pass, making too much noise with their engines or horns, you can join the locals in offering an opinion of the driver's heritage, though if there are children present, Caroline will ask that you keep such assessments civil, or to yourself.  On a Friday night you can take a break from 8.30 'til around 9.45 (so hurry back for a nightcap) to hear the Lebanese Philharmonic at the aforesaid house of prayer, acting as a fine concert venue, for free and excellently performed classical (in its broad sense) music - always with the best vocal and instrumental soloists. And yes, I did say free, gratuit, kostenlos. 
    So whether a tourist or unaware resident, go and sample the real Beirut for very reasonable prices, a chance to listen or show your ignorance and the challenge of  staying abstemious. It'll be fun.

address: Rue Hussein Beyhum Zoukak El-Blate, Beirut 1107 Lebanon
review_text: For those of you wanting a traditional and delicious Lebanese/Armenian food look no further. I did not allow my friends have the hotel breakfast on the morning we visited and even though they were initially perplexed as to why...they soon found out it was very wise to do so. The lachmacun is cooked in a traditional oven and right in front of your very eyes. The best we've had, even the vegetarian version was great. It works out just under $2 per piece. Expect to eat on the street or just take it with you. it's not easy to find, our taxi driver did not know it ..lucky I had the phone number.

address: Rue Hussein Beyhum Zoukak El-Blate, Beirut 1107 Lebanon
review_text: This tiny bakery is the real deal. It's not about looks or atmosphere, it's just about what is coming out of the oven. The Lahmacun with pomegranate molasses is the best in Beirut!

address: Spears Street facing the Lebanese Red Cross, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This tiny shop provides the best crepes in Lebanon. I might sound a bit exaggerating but boy how satisfied I was after my visit to 101 Gourmet. For our Saturday night treat I headed to this place after hearing positive comments regarding the crepes they make. It's a tiny shop with only one crepe board machine. The employee was polite and helpful. He suggested I take their special pizza crepe and the white hershey's with lotus spread and digestive biscuit crashed chunks, I also took one additional crepe containing Nutella and halawa. For a start I noticed that the board on which the crepes are made is rectangular not round like the ones usually seen at crepes parlors. The employee does not spread the crepe liquid mix by hands but instead he puts it in a sort of container connected to the board and simply passes it above.  You won't find those crepes the gooey empty edges as they don't fold their crepes the usual way. The crepe dough isn't unpleasantly sweet and it's very well cooked having that lovely brown golden color. All the crepes we got were amazingly tasty. I noticed that for the Pizza crepe they use Barilla special pizza sauce and they add good quality Mozzarella cheese they add to that black olives, mushrooms and corn (which they make sure to remove the water from) and here you go !!! As for the sweet crepes they were marvelous especially the one recommended by the employee (white hershey's with lotus spread and digestive biscuit crashed chunks) !!! The crepes prices are cheaper here than the ones at the competitors. It's surely my new favorite Crepes spot in town.

address: Myrtom House Bldg, Rue du Mexique, Kantari, Beirut 00000 Lebanon
review_title: Major Let down, lacks proper taste and authenticity
review_text:
Went to this restaurant based on a friend’s recommendation saying that is the place to eat authentic Thai food in Beirut. First impression, the tables are outside on the street, no problem… we asked for the menu…was surprised that it is a mixed array of Thai, Indian, Chinese, Singaporean dishes. Prices are exaggerated and over priced. Asked for drinks menu since no drinks were listed on their menu (besides sifts drinks) …they don’t have one the server stated and then gave options orally…when asked about the price. , I was surprised when he said the price of white wine bottle is $28!!! Really !!!paying this much for a bottle of wine with tables on the street and no ambiance!!!
We ordered shrimps dumplings and chicken green curry . The dumplings are 4 small ones for $10 ….they gave no relation to what real dumplings should look or taste like , and I don’t think they were steamed since they were too mushy …I believe they were boiled.
With regards the chicken curry, it came in a bowl with so much sauce as if you are getting a soup dish! Also the sauce was too lumpy and grainy …not smooth.
This is not how Thai food should look or taste .
Any major issue I found was that they do not offer disposable chopsticks , they bring you a container with fires, knives, and string plastic chopsticks that get re washed and reused! Seriously!!! Unacceptable!
They do serve complimentary dessert …which some consider a plus

This place was a major let down …over priced, lacks authentic taste and food presentation

review_text: Amazing experience one of a kind. Love the taste, menu and location. Must try while on BEY one of the best Asian food and experience. I like the street side seating at night.

review_text: We went to this spot to eat Thai food.  Seating was outdoors with a few table for our large party. The menu as extensive from what I can remember. We ordered a few different things. The food was very tasty. Service was good. Only problem seating outside is cats running round!

address: Myrtom House Bldg, Rue du Mexique, Kantari, Beirut 00000 Lebanon
review_text: Love this place and the ambiance. If you are looking for sitting in the sidewalk style of Thai/ Asian  restaurant, then this is a must go to. We have so many favorites, that when it comes to ordering, we always over order and over eat! Fish cakes, padthai, green curry and their spinach salads are delicious. Everything gets prepared while you enjoy one of their own iced teas. On a hot day, I would recommend heading there for dinner. You might have to wait to be seated as it gets very busy and it is a small venue, but definitely worth it.

address: Michel Chiha, Beirut 1111 Lebanon
review_text: If you want Lebanese traditional breakfast then you must visit La Foule at kantari area near where you used to be Harley bikes show room. The foul is tasty with the right amount of lemon, the fatteh is amazing, the zaater fatera is very rich and fulfilling and the oven meat slices with veggies is a must try. We had eggs cooked to our order and hummus was delicious. Overall highly recommended

address: Michel Chiha, Beirut 1111 Lebanon
review_title: A Riding Star in Lebanese Food
review_text:
In few words, La Foule would be a place where I could stay all day and have breakfast, lunch and dinner day after day after day...
The interior design, the hosting, the service and the unique simple Lebanese popular food elevated to a fourth dimension are a strike of a genius.
I can’t recommend a specific dish for each and every dish is an experience by itself.

address: Michel Chiha, Beirut 1111 Lebanon
review_text: Traditional Lebanese breakfast is associated with modest restaurants and set ups referred to locally as (Fawal - Foul maker) . La Foule is the only place you could have tasty foul and yummy traditional breakfast in a clean, fancy set up with top notch service. The plates are rich, tasty and nicely presented. Overall highly recommended

review_text: This place in saifi village produces some fine quality French style croissants. I visited the bakery Sunday morning for breakfast and grabbed an assortment of croissants to go. I had the plane croissant au beurre d'insigny, chocolate and cheese croissants. Clearly this bakery use premium quality ingredients but unfortunately we felt the croissants way too buttery for our taste and they were not crunchy enough from the outside. The special thing to mention is the cheese croissant which they fill with emmental cheese and another creamy kind of cheese which I believe is Brie.

address: Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Disappointing service and poor handling of customer concerns. Management should act.
review_text:
Our experience at this restaurant in Dbayeh was disappointing due to multiple service-related issues.

Upon arrival, the valet was sitting in a parked car and did not assist with parking. After waiting approximately 10 minutes, we parked our car on the road ourselves. Despite this, the valet later approached us requesting parking fees without providing a parking ticket!

Later in the evening, the valet came to our table inside the restaurant and began arguing with us about the parking fee in an unprofessional and impolite manner. Restaurant staff did not intervene. Although we paid the requested amount, no parking ticket was issued! 

When settling the restaurant bill, we paid using $100 bills and expected to receive change. The waiter did not return the change! We waited and waited but he did not bring the change . Only after calling him back did he bring the change, without any apology. Tipping should always be at the customer’s discretion and should not be assumed.

We positively raised these concerns with the manager, including:
	•	The valet parking issue
	•	Food quality and presentation
	•	The handling of the bill and change

While the manager listened, no corrective action or resolution was offered.

We hope management reviews these issues seriously and takes steps to improve service standards and customer experience.

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
We arrived on a Wednesday night at 7h for happy hour to Pre Beirut and we were received by a rude security guard on steroids, telling us where do you think you are going like that? In a rude tone, I asked him how does he allow himself to talk to people like that? He said if we are here without a reservation we cannot go in especially that we are 3 guys, we just turned our backs and left the building without going in after such a reception from an illiterate security guard.
We directly called the number of Pre Beirut and a certain girl answers, so we explained to her the situation asking to talk with management, she said the security guard was not allowed to do this and its not his job but she said she won't do anything about it and seemed totally careless.
We were stunned by their lack of customer service as if they are acting like that to scare customers off on purpose.
This was the first time we face such insolent behavior from staff/security and it shows the poor quality of service you will be getting in this place. No wonder it is not working nor popular and bad reviews are pouring like rain.
My advice to you, avoid like plague.

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: If you are anyone with some class or self respect I wouldn't go there. I live in the USA and I go to lebanon to have a good time and enjoy myself i called the club to see if they had any reservations available and they told me they were full but hey could squeeze us in at that one table. They said it would be $40 minimum charge. When I got there it felt like I was in a crack house. There were no lights, it seemed as though the people there were high, the place was empty there was maybe 10 guys who were on some sort of drugs. It wasn't a place for couples and I went with my boyfriend and two other couples. As a girl we all felt really uncomfortable. We were lied to by the staff saying it was full when it was clearly empty. I came to Lebanon for two weeks and I waisted one of my Friday nights there because it was one of be most miserable times of my life. If I could give a negative rating I would.

address: Miele Building, Downtown Beirut 6&7th Floor, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
If I was to rate the welcome and service, I would put ZERO. But to be fair for the Dj and the lovely music I will put half the grade.

What a shame, I called to inquire about reservation, the guy told me to be as early as 9:30 to guarantee spot. When we arrived, the bouncers at the main entrance of building started interrogating us (three ladies) as if we are at the prison entrance! Stuff like who are you here for?! and why are you here that early, until he called them to check if they are ready to receive?! And so we were granted the permission to cross the borders.
We arrived at the 6th floor where the staff was hardly ready. No welcome whatsoever, we sat at the bar and the waitress as well as another waiter who both lack training, approached us. They said that they don't have drinks menu.
We ordered:
- One  Moscow mule that came with an average taste and no cucumber (not available)
-Another friend ordered rosé yet the waiter poured her white wine; she had to ask for him to replace it
- Seven up diet that was initially  served in a plastic cup until i asked her why not the normal glass one?!
They have a policy where you are requested either to pay each drink straight or to deposit the visa card until departure. Maybe they had few bad experiences. But the way they ask for it is not really appropriate; it was as if looking at the customer in his eye doubting him and unconsciously accusing him of theft!
 
The enjoyable thing is the Music, the age range is mid -late twenties.

I think that they have a potential with a beautiful location, they need an adequate training of the staff, a less suspicious attitude but most importantly more respectful bouncers.
P.S:  Don't drag people early if you are not ready to receive.

review_text:
Currently having dinner in this restaurant on April 9th 2024. 
I just couldn’t resist writing this review while sitting there to show how much I am furious about the very bad service they have…first of all …our waiter ,a Lebanese guy with dark short hair and goatee has terrible customer service manners. We try to order from him and he has such a grumpy look and barely waits for you to finish your sentence…as if he is too busy and we are wasting his pre IOU’s time. …service is terrible…we have to continuously ask them to take a look at our empty wine glasses to refill them. 
They bring appetisers without serving utensils. We have to call waiters to remove empty glasses and dishes from the table. 
We asked for a bottle of wine and bottle of water. They brought the water bottle without water glasses. 
No salt and pepper shakers on the table. 
I ordered the beef tenderloin and my son ordered the same. The attached picture shows his dish. My dish actually had 2 thin pieces of steak put on top of each to show as if it was s thick piece of tenderloin! 
Vegetables were dry and super all dente!!!
I am embarrassed to be writing this review while sitting there since I am invited for dinner…but this has been such s terrible experience …from ALL aspects.

address: Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Do not judge a book by its cover!
review_text:
This is a really nice place: food is just perfect to get out of the “Lebanese routine” that you fall into in Beirut. 
The location is great in this little, shady square in the Saifi village, the service was attentive.
The rating would be better if not for their attitude towards tourists (and tourists looking people). So the place was empty (and still with outside tables empty when we left) but we couldn’t be seated outside! Of course every rich and good looking person that arrived was welcomed outside (I can tell you that there were more Hermes bags in the restaurant that in the Hermes shop itself)....obviously not looking the part enough! However my Black Amex changed their attitude at paying the bill...do not judge a book from the cover! So I will not be running back...I cannot stand attitude!

address: Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: The simplicity and what seems like militant dedication to great ingredients sets Paname apart from all the so called contemporary restaurants of Beirut. Carrots, artichokes, asparagus, black rice are treated with the same reverence as Blue Fin Tuna, delivering not just photogenic plating but incredibly balanced tastes and textures that were music to my palates. In the hands of Paname, sea creatures deliver the unique and complex taste of the ocean without even the hint of fishy smell. Do not let the ambitious menu intimidate you, the service aims to please any cravings, requests, or food fantasies. When the sides of a dish that was 86 for the day appealed to me, it was generously offered with another fish. No ego at Paname, just good food.

address: Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Nice , delicious and fresh burgers are served at this joint . The prices are reasonable, the service is prompt and punctual and the ambiance is really nice as well.

address: Moukhallassiya Street Saifi Village, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Expensive hipster food with no soul.
review_text:
Unbelievably expensive.
Hipster staff care more about seeming cool than actually doing their job.

The concept of farm or market to table restaurants is a wonderful idea. However, when the farm of the meat you are selling is in Australia, it really loses its authenticity. The restaurant is very ‘hipster’ and is clearly full of young  Lebanese expats or student who live outside of Lebanon but have returned for their summer holidays-rich kids. As someone living in Manchester, I am used to expensive meals, however I was blown away by the prices in this restaurant- $27 for a burger is insane! 

Now onto the service, the staff look great, in fact, they seemed more interested in themselves than the customers. No one explained to us that we had to choose our own meat before ordering, or the fact you then pay extra for them to actually cook the meal. 

My honest advice is:
lower your prices 
Get staff to explain the concept and guide customers through the market
Lebanon is a great country so celebrate its produce rather than getting your meat from Australia.

address: 61 Sursock Street, Beirut, Beirut 2063-1111 Lebanon
review_text:
If you’re a fan of Lebanese food, this place is an absolute gem!

From the moment we walked in, the atmosphere was welcoming, with attentive service and a menu full of mouth-watering options.

For me, the star of the meal for me was the Tabbouleh which was, hands down, one of the best I’ve ever had. Perfectly balanced with just the right amount of lemon and olive oil. It was light, refreshing, and incredibly flavorful.
We also tried most of the mezza and grilled meats. Everything was delicious and beautifully presented. Portions were generous without being overwhelming.

Highly recommend this spot. I’ll definitely be coming back!

address: 61 Sursock Street, Beirut, Beirut 2063-1111 Lebanon
review_title: Delicious  food with Authentic Lebanese taste & amazing service
review_text:
Beit Kanz is truly a gem for anyone who loves authentic Lebanese cuisine. The dishes are absolutely delicious, full of rich flavors and prepared with great attention to tradition. Every bite feels homemade and satisfying.

The atmosphere is warm and welcoming, and the staff are simply amazing , friendly, attentive, and always ready to make sure you have the best dining experience. Whether you’re going with family, friends, or even for a casual meal, Beit Kanz makes you feel right at home.
Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy tasty Lebanese dishes with exceptional service!

A lot of locals, a lot of foreigners, this is a place where you can go and mingle with anyone, or just listen to the coolest music. They change DJs every night, so the music is pretty diverse.
Also a great place for food (try the QUINOA burger!) or just a coffee. It is quite during the day, so it's a good place to work (free wifi). Would definitely recommend for any visitor wanting a taste of the hip Lebanese night life.
