address: Bliss Street, Hamra facing AUB main gate, next to KFC., Beirut 1103 Lebanon
review_text: The place is quite nice and the staff are friendly, they arguably have one of the best desserts you could have but the price could be lowered I think; we paid 22000LBP for both of them, but to be fair, we might have used too many toppings. The concept is that you customize your own Cinnamon Sugar Coated Roll; what’s inside it, whether you want ice cream on top or not, the toppings, the tasty sauces, everything! It was a very pleasant experience.

address: Bliss Street, Hamra facing AUB main gate, next to KFC., Beirut 1103 Lebanon
review_text:
Having had some of these "conuts" in Prague, I knew what to expect. 

However, this exceeded my expectations. Crisp cinammon churro-like dough, wound into a cone, and filled with sweet treats. I got "The One" which is filled with ice-cream, and personalised mine by adding Nutella - all for 6000 LL ($4). The cone was sweet but not cloying, with a sugary cinnamon outer coating which gave it crunch. The dough was lovely and light/soft under that and paired perfectly with ice cream.

They had other options such as filling it with cheesecake, creme pat or cream, and lots of toppings such as berries and pistachio. All decent value, I think the most expensive option was 8000LL.

My only critique was that the end of the cone needs to be closed off as mine got a bit drippy with the ice-cream melting a bit in the hot cone (all captured by the paper bag it was held in, so no spills).

This will surely become a favourite on Bliss Street!

address: Jbeil Beach, Byblos 00961 Lebanon
Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Great place in the heart of downtown Beirut. We’ve been there plenty of times. Good food and atmosphere. Portions were reduced from what it was before. More importantly the service was sub-par considering the hospitality Lebanon is known for. We caught our server looking over at our table inappropriately for the setting, gossiping with his colleague, and laughing. It’s a surprise as this never happened before and frankly this will be the last time we will come.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Come as a group of at least four. Order a little of everything.
review_text:
Beirut is noticeably on the mend after the 2020 port explosion that devasted the city leaving hundred’s dead. With a favourable terroir, land and sea, and some decades of French influence, you can look forward to great food.

There’s a simplicity in great Japanese restaurants that adheres to the adage, same, same, but different. The décor is very simple and no less elegant and contemporary for it. Located between Zaitunay Bay, Downtown and the political district, it services a demanding clientele.

That the menu has no prices on the printed version might make a first-time visitor uneasy, but worry not. Due to currency instability, which has led to the beginning of nationwide dollarisation, pricing can be fluid in Lebanese Lira. 

There is a QR code that will give you current menu pricing in US Dollars. This is very common and should not worry you. You will be far too interested in the fine range of offerings, the wines every bit as appealing.

Sea salted edamame for the immediately famished enjoyed, it was time to place both feet firmly inside.

Fresh salmon maki put a smile on my wife’s face, pre and post, much like a child getting a small ice cream cone, albeit this one in wrapped glorious dark seaweed.

Ordered from the salad menu, odd given the greens were really tasty garnish, were two helpings of crispy spicy salmon. Sashimi salmon in popcorn popping panko crumb and a light late lingering spice. If we were ordering in stages, I’m not sure we’d have eaten anything else.

Tuna and salmon sashimi were on point, fresh ginger and wasabi likewise.

Three separate sushi platters arrived next. There were a few of us. I’m not sure I’ve seen a mango ura maki before. The colour was joyful and like its neighbours disappeared systemically into us happy diners.

I opted for the wagyu foie gras, ordered and served bleu. Merci chef. Both were similarly butchered for their plating, served as two fractured asteroids one atop the other. Holding it together was a thuddingly thickened teriyaki sauce. From the nose up to my eyes, everything was alight. 

It cut like butter and melted similarly in all our mouths. As I write this an hour later, its ghost still happily haunts my mouth.

The service is good, efficient, the waiters know their menu and can talk for it.

I don’t know when I’ll be back in Beirut again, but if I am, I’ll dine here at least once.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: A favorite for sushi is Beirut. The menu has quite a good selection and everything is delicious. The setting is nice and the service is very good. Their salmon sashimi salad and their tuna sashimi salad are very good. Highly recommend.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Very bad experience. Ordered soba noodles and got white fast food noodles, fried rice came with corn and mushrooms, miso soup had mushrooms, tomyum tasted like miso with red paste spice, tempura shrimps soaked in cocktail sauce. Food came in weird order, they forgot the drinks. And so expensive. So NOT worth it. Will never go again.

address: Minet el Hosn Paladium building, Down Town, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Good Japanese Food in beautiful ambience
review_text: Located in the trendy „Little Dubai“ district near Phoenicia Hotel. Very friendly bar staff, quality of food very good. Nice drinks and great music. Prices are on a higher level but compare well on an international level.

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Top end restaurant in downtown area focused on business lunch customers (as I was). 50/60’s chic style decor. Food was Mediterranean/French. Good quality food and cooking but nothing exceptional or Michelin standard. Service was attentive.

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
I go to Balthus once per month for business/friends lunch. The menu has been clinically improved. Offering many options for the healthy and the foodies.
Tuesday and Thursday are Kebbe Naye day, pre order it to guarantee your portion. Their crab salad, tuna salad made with fresh fish is second to none. The fresh ham , a la coupe is a house specialty but not always available.The omelette and meat options are excellent and the tomatoes and also the pea salads are exquisite! Must visit for a great meal and service.

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Great ingredients wonderful service professional staff. Superb wine list needs to be updated as you see a wine year on bottle and you get another. An efficient small effective menu with fresh ingredients.
Felicitations for Mrs. Frida

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: This is the place to do business in Beirut whether its politicians, bankers,  lawyers or leaders of industry...they are all invariably here at least once a week. Never sitting outside, always inside. The food is delicious whatever you order and the service is impeccable (which is hard to achieve in Lebanon). My favourites are the tomatoe salad, lentil salad and the steak.

address: Ghandour Building, Minaa El Hosn, Downtown, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Now very average and way overpriced
review_text: Used to be a fan but the last couple of meals were quite disappointing. While the service was good, the food was mediocre, except for a tasty ceviche. But the prices have risen to stratospheric levels for what is essentially an average bistro. I sense that with this approach, the place won't be around much longer. I will certainly not go there again.

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
I was disappointed as I didn’t get the service I was expecting. 
1- After my visit to the restaurant to pay a deposit and agreed on location of table, I was disappointed on the day of dinner that they did not seat us on the selected table 
2-I requested to include the names of my daughter and her husband on a private menu, as  we were celebrating their wedding, restaurant skipped writing their names and only apologized after ruining my surprise

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: We came to this restaurant on a family lunch. The food was good and the service very nice. I have heard comment on this place in the past saying that it was a place to “see and to be seen”, implying that the food was not great, though if that was the case before, I believe it isn’t anymore.

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Enjoyed a Business lunch here ..located in downtown area, really smart dining space with very attentive service. Excellent quality cooking  and ingredients...meats were all exceptional..the Chauteaubriand and veal chop in particular. Appetisers were all really fresh ingredients and well presented. Desserts were also good, particularly the traditional French Toast with caramel sauce. High end restaurant with high quality cooking and service. Highly recommended.

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
The risotto a la Truffe was terribly salty. Couldn’t eat it
The fillet dish was supposed to be served with potatoes and spinach. The spinach was not served. And when I ask for it They have added additional price. 
The waiters are not friendly and not attentive to the customers request
My experience was below average

address: Palladium building, Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: We were three girlfriends visiting the restaurant on Saturday night, despite our hotel suggestion not to.. The restaurant has a luxurious atmosphere. The cuisine is French. First complimentary little plates of carrots, cashius and Parmesan was a very pleasant surprise. Niçoise salad was ok, risotto very tasty, although fatty, chateaubriand and beef fillet of extremely high quality. Finally, dessert, apple crumble with Cointreau ice cream was the best ever!! Not to miss. The main complain that we are still talking about is the service: DISHES WERE TAKEN AWAY WHILE WE HAD NOT FINISHED EATING!!! Never happened to us in any other restaurant. It seems that they wanted us to leave the restaurant asap! Also, one of the waiters was unexperienced, almost spilled the food on my friend’s head!! Only exception, the parking valet service: boys held huge umbrellas helping us to reach our taxi protecting us from the heavy rain! Overall: nice expensive restaurant, tasty food coming at big portions. Recommended, just stand up against the overeager waiters ...

address: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street, Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
I walked in today and tried to make lunch reservations. August 27, 11:30 AM. I said to the hostess my dad is 90 and he needs a chair with armrests not too far from the entrance. She said that was not possible. I pointed to a chair with armrests and I said there’s one. She said she couldn’t move the chair. I said really? You can’t move a chair to help a 90 year old man? She said no nonchalantly. I said we were here last month and somehow a chair was arranged. She said she needed to ask management and get back to me (??!!) I said you need to ask management if you can move a chair for a 90 year old man? She said yes. I walked out. Don’t bother ask management.

We had lunch at this place last month and service was much better. The waiters were very nice and helpful. They arranged for a chair with armrests and cushions. What was different this time? We were 7 then and the bill came out to $830. I paid $1,000. 

The hostess today was very rude. She couldn’t care less…

address: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street, Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Avoid that place ! Pure scam ! Need major adjustments for food taste + service ! Mediocre taste !! Horrible experience ! What a waste of time & money!

address: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street, Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: We had dinner on a Saturday night in Le Sushi Bar Starco. We booked a table outside, the weather was wonderful, the restaurant ambiance as well. Everything we ordered was really delicious and prepared with premium ingredients. The only draw back was the excessive arrogance of the waitress. I will give this place another chance and in case we face the same approach from the staff, we will make sure not to visit again.

address: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street, Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: We arrived at Le sushi bar, because our hotel was close and we were very hungry but it was a very inspired choice because the food was amazing. The sushi was wow and service was great. I recomand this place if you are a sushi lover.

address: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street, Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: I visited with my husband, at the entrance we were welcomed nicely and escorted to our desired place to sit. As I was pregnant, I needed specific type of seafood. Our server Fares approached and introduced himself to us warmly. He went far beyond my expectations about helping me to make correct choice from menu. He knew all ingredients of each menu item very well and was very knowledgeable about sushi types for pregnancy. Another colleague Abdulla also joined him to assist me and they modified ingredients of sushi that I ordered according to my preferences! Both were very cheerful and patient to my "picky" character :) I felt very cared and respected. THANK YOU GUYS! YOU WERE AMAZING!! Keep it up!

address: Rafic Salloum street, M1 Bldg. Minet El Hosn. Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Have been a while since i didn’t have  any excellent cooked food with reasonable price in a very nice place! Loved the vibes , the service, the food ...so unique tasty dishes! #yokobeirut , you rock!

address: Sn Ahmad Chawki Street, Minet el-Ho, Beirut Down Town Lebanon
review_text: Restaurant located in new downtown Beirut area just up from Zaitunay Bay. Nice interior decor and huge outside terrace. Wide ranging menu all very reasonably priced for visitors to Beirut. Signature calamari was excellent as was the chicken with truffle linguine. A delicious tapenade with the fresh breads worth a mention.  Overall a nice dining experience in good location with the only surprise I was the only diner in the restaurant on an early Thursday evening for the entire meal.

address: Sn Ahmad Chawki Street, Minet el-Ho, Beirut Down Town Lebanon
review_title: Nice place in Down Town Beirut
review_text:
L’avenue du Parc  is one of our favourite restaurants down town Beirut. They have maintained the good quality of food and service that we experienced along the years . We come here for family lunches or dinners , friends gathering and sometimes for business  lunch .This place is suitable for every occasion. 
This evening we had dinner with friends and had a delightful culinary experience as well .
Good Job management , Chef and staff of L’Avenue du Parc.

address: Sn Ahmad Chawki Street, Minet el-Ho, Beirut Down Town Lebanon
review_text:
The place is nice having a simple classy decoration, having an indoor and outdoor.
The location is in the heart of the downtown where you can find many restaurants and Cafes.
Food is tasty and portions are fair.
The service was good and the waiter has knowledge about the menu and ingredients.

address: Sn Ahmad Chawki Street, Minet el-Ho, Beirut Down Town Lebanon
review_text: We always make sure to visit the place every time we are in beirut! It’s always consistent. Food is super tasty.. service is excellent and value for money is great. Thank you!

address: Sn Ahmad Chawki Street, Minet el-Ho, Beirut Down Town Lebanon
review_text: One of our favourite dining restaurants  in Beirut. This place is great for Family gathering , couples dates or business meals .  The staff are friendly and the service is great. Food is delicious.

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Beirut is a little tricky as far as location is concerned. This restaurant is somehwat hidden away, but don't let that put you off. Is is easy to locate on google maps or on Allo taxi. It is small, boutique and very, very friendly. 

So much for location.

But here is the kicker. The staff are attentive without being overbearing. Arabic, French and English are spoken with equivalent fluency. There are brilliant starters and coffee and desserts are available. But the real deal here are the steaks. 

Yes; you will pay for what you get; It is not cheap, but when it comes it is sublime. Or better. I will confess I had an Argentinian 200g fillet and chips. It was quite simply wonderful. 

I was in Beirut for two weeks and would (and will) go back here to get a real, quality and carefully well-cooked steak which you could compare with anything you could get in London, Munich, Sydney or Cape Town.

LBP, USD and mainstream credit cards are welcome (with a + for international credit cards)

If  you are hankering for a clearly wonderful beef meat meal with 100% quality, then this is your place.

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Our freind told us we must come here and try meet. And I am so happy we did. It is very nice place,  few tables,  great  salads and of course meat. Very tasty, you can feel different kind of source and spices in each bite.
Very nice wine list.

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Good service, great atmosphere, tasty burgers indeed
The vibe is pretty cosy, friendly waiters and staff

address: Assurex Bldg, Abdel Hamid Ghandour Street Downtown Beirut, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: The meat is consistently excellent. The ambiance is always nice and cosy. It is expensive though, especially if you don't order a piece of meat; i.e.: meat price is acceptable as it's top quality, but burger and appetizers are so overpriced.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Salads are very good.  the daily plate was good Moujadra.  it is pleasent to sit outdoor when the weather is good.  Freindly service.  Can order food from adjacent Pizza co and Burger co.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Our family spent a few days in Beirut. We wanted to avoid the 30$ per person usually charged in hotels. We found this nice restaurant, well located and offering excellent breakfast (eggs on toast, avocado-eggs bagels....) at a reasonable price.  A no brainer to start the day well!

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
My brother, his wife, kids & I went to Deli.co early on in December for breakfast in downtown Beirut in a Saturday.

The place is average sized - although the outdoor seating arrangement felt a little crowded and in close proximity to other patrons. 

The waiter was pretty helpful and offered us plenty of advice and a quick service. 

I ordered gluten free waffles & was truly truly impressed at how yummy they are, while me niece ordered scrambled eggs, which were pretty moist. My sister in law loved her avo on toast while my brother found his pancakes mediocre. I also ordered a gluten free pb&j with caramelized banana which was alright. 
The the star dish, and a must try of you try this place, was the gluten free waffles; everyone agreed that they were the best waffles we’ve tasted in a longtime - not to mention gluten free!  

Over all, Deli.co’s outdoor seating arrangement had a chill vibe, especially for a Saturday and the food was tasty.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Yes for the tastes and service
review_text:
Stopped twice during a business day in Beirut at Deli co where service was excellent and sandwiches were fresh from bread to ingredients. 
Only prices are a bit over the average when compared to the same meals bought everywhere in the country however these reflect the area and the quality of food being served

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
Deli.co is one of the four sisters restaurants Pizza.co, Burger Co and recently opened Ummi at Beirut souks. And it is not to be underestimated!

Same concept of ability to order from all four menus, we opted to honor Deli.co's menu only, and we did not regret!

Service is synchronized with the theme, the staff are knowledgeable off the menu, cheerful and helpful.
We enjoyed dinner on the terrace, savoring:

Signature item: the original NYC Pastrami on rye bread served with a side of dill pickles and chips: it is so flavorsome not the healthiest thing you can have so worth the try!
Open faced scottish Smoked salmon over spicy avocado on country bread, equally served with chips:premium quality of every single ingredient reflected in the taste
Classic grilled cheese: same presentation and sides
My favorite trio salad: first of all I loved the concept of being able to combine three types of salad. I also appreciated the generous quantity and in addition to that the fact that it is changed daily: we had a combination of grilled vegetables, freekeh salad with eggplants, pomegranate, cherry tomatoes and parsley and fell in love at first sight / bite! and lettuce hearts with olives and drizzled with yogurt based sauce. 
How could we miss their home made desserts:

Lemon poppy seed cake: I love the contrast sourness of lemon with the sweetness of cake and the small crunch of the seeds
Semolina cake with caramelized oranges: so moist.
Carrot cake: was my least favorite I felt it needed more spices though it had a beautiful presentation.
We had lovely times and would totally recommend you to visit.

La Petite

XoXo

address: Beirut Souks, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Contemporary friendly eating place in the Souks
review_text:
The Met is one a series of friendly contemporary eating places in the Souks. From sushi to burgers and from Meze to Beef in oyster sauce it has a big range but with that comes questions about authenticity and taste. The problem is that the Met is trying to appeal to everyone and risks pleasing no one. Its fine but really rather boring...

I ordered the lemonade and the cheese burger with fries. Congrats to them for putting the tomato, onion and lettuce on the side so you can decide whether to have these or not. The burger was well cooked and the fries were good. This came to US19 which is a lot for burger and fries but I enjoyed it. Staff were efficient but it had limited ambience...

address: Beirut Souks, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Sushying at The Met is over-priced, over-small, over-confusing.. watch out for their menu as the items showing 2 colored plates will be added!

address: Beirut Souks, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: An ellegance large state of the art delicacy desserts and icecream
review_text:
this rsturant is called the met metropolitan and they have another one for lunch and dinner what is striking is the way they present icecream and desserts artful way with vivid colours of cups and mixed with hot choclate woooo amazing and if you visit beirut souks is a must to go to the Met
enjoy

address: Beirut Souks, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: I tried the place with my friends, we tried Swiss mushroom burger, salad bar, and chili burger. The salad was rich and had a variety of items and it was worth it. But the burgers came cold and they had an average taste. The service was good. Their prices are high compared to the quality of food served

address: Beirut Souks, Beirut Lebanon
review_text:
My mom and I went unsure what we wanted to eat and honestly the variety of the dished was perfect to satisfy any craving. 
We decided to go for several appetizers and share them and everything we tried was great.

address: Lion Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_title: Refined juice spot in downtown Beirut
review_text: A refined juice spot in the Souks fo Beirut, having a quite high price tag but indeed offering an exquisite selection of juices. I highly recommend strawberry-banana smoothy.

address: Lion Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: They serve one of the best fresh juices and traditional desserts in Beirut, you can have with your healthy cold drink cream and honey dish and fruit toppings. I would recommend jellab drink it's really refreshing!

address: Lion Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: The Antabli square in the old downtown was an iconic place and nostalgia to the old place drove me to the new downtown branch. We ordered avocado juice with cream and nuts and were not disappointed though the prices are not cheap. Overall highly recommended

address: Lion Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: Lots of flavours to choose from! Three of us went, had 3 flavours each, and we kept sharing!  Raspberry and pistachio were both strong, but still natural and unsweetened.

address: Lion Street, Beirut Lebanon
review_text: No trip to downtown Beirut is complete without a stop at Al Antabli: Lebanese traditional desserts and fresh fruit juices at truly cheap prices of $2-4 a piece. Always fresh and tasty. A must-try.

address: Beirut 00961 Lebanon
review_text:
Well, this was disappointing. Went with two Lebanese people. After 15 plus years, we are always keen to find a really good Lebanese / Middle Eastern restaurant. This is not it.

The menu is straight forward - all the basics one would expect: Hummus, baba ghanoush, sambousik, kibbeh, shish taouk, and so on.  We were very excited to try the kibbeh nayeh (lamb tartare) which they said they didn’t have, because the quality of the meat wasn’t good enough. I can tell them where to get good enough lamb in Singapore, if they are struggling. The menu is pretty short anyway, so not to offer everything on it on a Saturday evening seems an odd choice.

The food generally is mediocre. Sadly. The fattoush had an odd aftertaste - like some of the lettuce wasn’t as fresh as it should have been. The garlic sauce with the chicken skewers didn’t taste the way it should. Perhaps there are different ways to prepare, but it ought to have a lemony taste. This one didn’t. We usually love it, always eat too much of it. This thou tasted strange and bland. Everything was a bit bland - we can make much better and fresher Lebanese food at home.

We had two cocktails which were supposed to have alcohol, but we could not really taste it. It tasted mostly of water. Two bears came and were so flat, we sent them back - we rarely sent anything back. Should have done the same with the cocktails. We got the safer option of two Little Creatures bottles instead.

The interior of the place is pretty, very small place though. We had hoped to sit outside as it looked pleasant in photos, ans we generally prefer outside seating. Unfortunately, that area is right next to the car drop off point, and not sufficiently protected, so its really just a waiting area. Service was good though. They have enough staff and they are friendly and effective.

I don’t think we will be back though. Why bother if they are not going to make an effort with this to being this amazing cuisine to Singapore.

address: Beirut 00961 Lebanon
review_text:
Everybody claims to cook Lebanese,but UMMI truly does it authentic with top of the line ingredients. Great taste . 
Try their Authentic Tabbouleh.

address: Beirut 00961 Lebanon
review_text:
Ummi

address: Beirut 00961 Lebanon
review_text: This is an adjacent Restaurent to beirut Souks ( prime location) the food experience is great , but what stands out the most is the quality of service where you feel welcomed like home , special thanks to Anwar who was very kind with the children, i know for sure this is not easy as it takes a lot of good leadership to have such a genuine services, well done 👍

address: Beirut 00961 Lebanon
review_text: This is a great new trendy restaurant!!! Very much enjoyed the concept and the food!!! Long live Phoenician cuisine and the style.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut 00961 Lebanon
review_text: After much indecision we decided to eat here and we are so glad we did. The food was outstanding and the service was attentive. The calamari was some of the best I have ever had.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut 00961 Lebanon
review_text: Great pizza. Kind staff. Very nice restaurant. Definetly recommended. If you get tired of Lebanese food after a while check this place out.

address: Patriach Howayek St, Beirut 00961 Lebanon

review_text:
..The perfect pasta in the whole world

the staff friendly

review_text: Nice clean restaurant, friendly staff, and the food is tasty, especially the nutella pizZa and Carmel with biscuits OMG, its heaven hehehe, actually its part of a group of restaurants Burgr.co which is another mouthwatering food, and the salad at the third restaurant (deli.co) is amazing... review_text: Staff is a bit rude... Food came not warm... I ordered a classic angus beef burger... I didn't like... I asked for a diet Pepsi can they don't serve it so I had a draft one that was not tasty... I don't recommend it!!!

address: Beirut Souks, Beirut Lebanon
Ashir el Dayeh Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:

address: Ashir el Dayeh Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: interesting menu, wonderful meal that was perfectly served. The best Mexican food in Lebanon. address: Ras Maska, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Overpriced,  the food was tasteless .
When we saw the menu,  we asked about the photos on the menu and we've been told that they're the same. The portion is very generous but the food was tasteless.  I ordered a plate with orange sauce and my friend asked for the mango one. To be honest we were shocked,  the plates were full of sauces, the chicken was chewy and it looks like its been reheated ,
The orange wasn't taste like an orange, it was like a very bad cheap barbecue sauce . We ate some because we were starving , but we couldn't finish as we felt sick. The star i gave is for the kind staff

address: Ras Maska, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Great dinner experience with excellent views .
review_text: Fantastic food great service had the chicken parmigiana and mushroom steak we finished the plates enjoying every bite you will not be disappointed

address: Ras Maska, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I visited this restaurant today and had mixed feelings about my experience. While the service was good and the staff were friendly and attentive, I was disappointed by the state of the prayer area. It was dirty and poorly maintained, which made it very unpleasant to use. As a customer who values cleanliness and hygiene, this was a big concern for me. I hope the management takes this issue seriously and improves the cleanliness of the prayer area, as it is an important part of the restaurant for many customers

address: Ras Maska, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Not an Italian restaurant but has its quirks
review_text: This restaurant is definitely not an Italian restaurant. At best, it's a diner s version of an Italian... the excess of cooking cream is telling... But its location and staff s friendliness make it a winner. Everyday. Especially when escaping the suffocating city.

address: Ras Maska, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
I regularly go to Punto Alto Restaurant, every time I would like to have a real Italian lunch or dinner.
The restaurant has a charming location.
The staff is very friendly, and serving you professionally and quickly.
Regarding the food, the restaurant serves generously wide range of Italian meals, and you can enjoy as well delicious snacks and desert too.

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Amazing sea view and  tasty Maza dishes. Shishtawooq and grilled Kuba are delicious and the free dessert of ice cream with GazelAlbanet is fabulous. The price is moderate.

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Lebanese mezze at its best. You must try the kafta in pomegranate sauce. Delicious...
Clean, friendly service, good food, good prices. What more can u ask for?

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
If ur in the middle of the city at the mountain or u need a sea view u can find ur branch.
Faysal offer u a great tasty food ,especially oriental food served with excellance 👍🏻i highly recommend

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: It's my favorite restaurant in north Lebanon, very clean and delicious food, the staffs are very friendly, highly recommend it

address: Al Kalamoun, Sea side road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Very nice place and amazing sea view plus a tasty arabic food always hot and yummy ! Extremely recommend it

address: Dam & Farez, 24 Street, Tripoli Lebanon
Old Beirut Road Miramar Resort, Hotel & Spa, Kalamoun, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
We visited this place as we invited friends for dinner, and they suggested this restaurant as it was close to their house. Fortunately we came, and ate at this place. Amazing view, authentic environment and super delicious food. Service was fast and waiters were friendly and professional. We finished every bite and plates on that table. The chef passed by to make sure everything was as we expected. They provide complimentary fruits plates and sweets after the meal. 
This restaurant is located in Miramar resort, and had a great beach scene. I am not sure though if you could access the resort from the restaurant, but it looked far walking distance. Highly recommended.

address: Old Beirut Road Miramar Resort, Hotel & Spa, Kalamoun, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: This restaurant at the end of a pier is very nicely located and has seaviews and views on a small yacht dock next to the Miramar hotel. It has a nice and spacious interior with a trendy lay-out. That night we had a group dinner during a conference with a Lebanese mezze. Many classic small dishes were offered and tasted with a friendly ambiance. Quality of the dishes was above average. Reception and service were good.

address: Old Beirut Road Miramar Resort, Hotel & Spa, Kalamoun, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
What a gorgeous view! What lovely food! What a relaxing ambiance! What wonderful staff! 
I absolutely loved Bahri Bay which is located in the Miramar Resort in Tripoli. I will definitely visit again, especially at sunset.

address: Old Beirut Road Miramar Resort, Hotel & Spa, Kalamoun, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Perfect place to eat and meet...
Every Sunday they served open international Buffet with amazing food and excellent hospitality 
During the weekdays it's a large Carte and what I love is they served fresh fish & mixed grill in the same time so what you want you can eat

address: Al Manar Tripol, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
SOHO premuim street food is the perfect place to eat with kids not being worried from tummy aches or bad quality. 
It's the best in cleanliness, hygiene, service and it's seriously premium as they use fresh and top food quality items!
The taste is unforgettable as we tried all the menu till now since it was opened, we love all the items, the fajitas, burgers especially beef woooow, the special boxes they have satisfy our cravings. As well they have a wonderful ambience which keeps my kids 😊 
I recommend it for every mom cares about her family 😁❤️ 
Special and big Thanks to it's Chef and lovely staff especially Iman 🌹

address: Ras Maska, Bohssas Lebanon

This place will be recommended for business

address: 32 Road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: This is one of many places in Lebanon where you cant go wrong. Almost everything is delicious and the place is clean and service good.

address: 32 Road, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Everything was absolutely perfect! Such a tasty variety of traditional Lebanese breakfast dishes, excellent service, impeccably clean, all with Feiruz softly playing in the background!

Always a reserved tables that is not used by anyone for more than two hours. They keep those tables for the customers who give higher tips.

Prices affordable with good portions.

Staff very friendly & welcoming

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon

address: Achier El Daya, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: The place is perfect for gatherings. The customer service is perfect and the food is super delicious and high quality. I would recommend only adding some traditional oldies Arabic songs or music morning time.

address: Achier El Daya, Tripoli Lebanon

review_text: I ordered chicken parmigiana without cheese, and baked potatoes instead of pasta. the first time they got the chicken with cheese, the second time they got the chicken without the tomato sauce, and pasta instead of potatoes. they apologized but they didn’t actually do anything about it. I heard the waitress talk with someone (not sure if he’s the manager or the supervisor) but still he just looked at me without doing anything, not even apologizing. Very bad experience!!!

review_title: Seeking for something special in north lebanon

Dar El Qamar is by far the best restaurant in Tripoli. And can compete for that title on a country level as well.. 
Great Lebanese cuisine, amazing and fast service, very spacious and continuously innovative and introducing new items to the menu.. can't recommend it enough.

address: Dam W Zarez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: The staff is so welcoming and caring, the food was perfect and delicious. One of the best lebanese restaurants. I would definitely recommend it, and im definitely coming back!😍

address: Dam W Zarez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: veryy generous amount of foodd with high quality ingredients!! The staff are very good and u feel like u're sitting in ur home

address: Dam W Zarez, Tripoli Lebanon

address: Dam W Zarez, Tripoli Lebanon

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon

address: Achir Daye Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: El Hajj Ali is a restaurant where you can sit and eat in Tripoli away from the crowd and noise.Its location is in Tripoli but you dont have to enter inside Dam w Farz(street where all restaurants and caffes are located).Its building is designed as a spanish building.The food is delicious and clean.The waiters are friendly.

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: One of the best cocktail places in tripoli
review_text:
Quite and very nice ambiance , excellent quality of fresh and other fruits, friendly and responsive staff, wide variety of fruits all over the seasons . moderate prices.
But what i like most about this place , is that i can order my customized cocktail whenever i like .
The icecream is also yummy and various .And fresh Juices are always available.

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon

When you visit tripoli you should pass by papaya the most delicious cocktail and ice cream in town 
You can find so many choices in ice cream like mango,  strawberry, lemon, blackberry, coconut, dry fruit, chocolate, Oreo, vanilla and so many ... ♥️😍😍😍🍧🍧🍹🍹🍹

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I went there twice, and ate Italian ice cream. The flavors are diverse and the taste very good and fresh. The prices are acceptable too and the inner hall is fine for sitting and enjoying my dessert.

address: Achir El Daya Street Dam West Farez, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Clean and fresh ingredients, staff is polite, 
My advise is to stay away from canned fruits, 
Better to use a smaller variety of fresh fruits than a larger one with some canned fruits

review_text: This is a neat restaurant, clean and friendly, most of its customers are polite and elegant, price is very good for the high quality provided, Papaya is good choice for families with children too, highly recommended.

address: Riad El Solh St Facing Mina central and Max Department Store, Mina, Tripoli Lebanon

review_text: Nice, friendly atmosphere and staff. Great location. Most importantly, there is no indoor smoking allowed.

address: Riad El Solh St Facing Mina central and Max Department Store, Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I did not know this place before and was just walking around for sightseeing. When i saw this cafe i thought: i can rest here and sip my coffee. I ordered an espresso and it was tasteful. I really liked the aroma and enjoyed my espresso. I didn't taste the other coffee but it seems that they are doing great in coffee roasters.

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Nice place to be when in Tripoli-Mina
review_text:
Mediterranean sea food place; Nice company, stories from a multi lingual Sea Captain.
Ask for the Mezze variety, insist if they have raw fish.
I recommend fried "Mallifa" Fish if available or any Grilled fish.

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Minos is a hidden jewel not far from the coast. it's a very nice place to enjoy some wine with seafood. the only annoying part is when other customers start drunk singing, it can be very loud and unpleasant because the setup is very small.

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Very good mezze and grilled fish in Mina, worth the visit. Small detail: the fish menu is rather small, but after the mezze with different raw fish options, you hardly want to eat more...

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Do not be afraid by the deco which is far from the bling bling Beyrouth style. The menu is only 25000 and for this you ll have 5 very good starters then a full dorade in the oven . The boss is a former captain and is very nice . The wine is at the same price than in the shop . Go there , a very nice time to spend .

address: Mina, El Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: This is a hidden gem once you discover you'll become a regular there! The place is tucked away between EL Mina buildings on Groupie roundabout so you will need to ask around if this is your first time. Trust me, it's worth it! For around 30 USD you will get a set menu of all kinds of mezza dishes plus the catch of the day. The place does not have shisha but they will accommodate your request if you would like one. If you are a large group of 20, you can request that the whole place be booked for you only. You can purchase alcohol and play whatever music you would like. The place also has a TV and AC's for when it's too cold/hot.

address: Tripoli Dam W Farez Main Road, Tripoli 0096 Lebanon
review_text: Cosy place, not recommend for family with young children, sheesha smoking restaurant,  average food, good service, mid range prices. If you are a  sheesha smoker, it is good to visit. It serves Food,  juices, coffee, sheesha all day.

address: Tall Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I visited Tripoli branch with family , wasn’t like before  specially the Barazi2 ZERO, I asked the lady on the cashier if the Barazi2 have pistachio she confirmed, I opened the first box No pistachio so I showed the cashier lady she point on her manage and said He’s responsible, so I showed the manager that there is no pistachio by flipping the small pcs one by one, he didn’t act and I end up putting the 2 box I. The garbage

address: Tall Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Took a prepared  baklava to visit family turns out to be old!  Will give it to the chickens on my farm!  

How many days do you leave items out to sell and claim to be fresh!  I would avoid this place I should have went to cremino!

address: Tall Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Located at Tripoli Lebanon. Visit this place for the first time for lunch. The food was just ok but the Lebanese sweets were tasty as usual. They service was good too. We paid around 18eur per person. To try the sweets only.

address: Tall Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I’m in Australia and I ordered baklava from #Rafaat Hallab  it just took a 5 days to get it and the taste was so good and fresh I loved it 😍

address: Abdul Hamid Karami Square, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text: Arabic sweets the best also knefeh my best one u can order anytime for every event its very fancy desserts

address: Abdul Hamid Karami Square, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_title: Delicious sweets, cake and lahm b ajeen
review_text: on the go pick up, this place is just near the city center, and if you're in a hurry to go from Tripoli heading south, this place is perfect to try some lebanese sweets or "lahm b ajeen" , take away or sitting for a while to eat then go.

address: Abdul Hamid Karami Square, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text: Not a maven of Middle Eastern desserts.  Being used to the cloyingly sweet version of Greek baklava, I was pleasantly surprised with the texture and less sweet version of various baklava's from Al Amina-tom sweet.  I'm sure that persons of Middle Eastern dissent have a far more discerning palate for various Palestinian, Egyptian and Lebanese confections, but I was very pleased with the assortment of sweets that I purchased.

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_title: olive half eaten found in foul plate
review_text: i came from canada straight to the restaurant missing authentic lebanese foul and hommus the surprise was tha i found olive half eaten in the foul plate what that mean ? the food reming from customer add to the new customer plate?? i give zero star

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_title: Typical restaurant in the middle of the Tripoli souk
review_text: Typical restaurant in the middle of the Tripoli souk, the most vibrant souk in Lebanon! wonderfully decorated, with an exhibition of clocks, telephones and old cameras. Very varied and excellent traditional Lebanese food, with dishes to share. The service is very friendly, as in most Lebanese restaurants.

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text: Might just be the best foul I ever had in Trablous, and thats saying a lot since foul is something they know how to make. Cool interior and efficient waiters.

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon
review_text: So disgusting, I felt like the dish only looked good on the outside and on the inside tasted like people's droppings, I was very disappointed

address: Al Koura Square Mohammad El Husseini Street, Tripoli 1300 Lebanon

best ambiance & service providd

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Had a lovely stop at Abdel Rahman Hallab in Batroun! The ice cream was absolutely delicious, the place is charming and well-kept, and the whole team was welcoming and professional. A special shout-out to Lea Farah, our waitress, she was so attentive, kind, and genuinely supportive. Her warm smile and thoughtful service made the experience even more enjoyable. Highly recommend visiting!

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Shipping to the US.
We paid more than $200 for your stupid variety box , you charged $35 to ship , and we found out that the sweet selection in it are more than a month old …. This  business  is an insult to any honorable Lebanese business , it’s just a Dumpster.

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Verry delicious but verrrry bad in changing the price in same day after 3 hours, they increase the price, this is unfair i prefer to change the manager

address: Riad El Solh Str., Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Excellent products. Service and Front Staff Needs Training (And Better Pay?)
review_text: Hallab 1881, known to everyone as Kasr el Helou, has by far the best quality sweets in Lebanon. No other establishment comes close. However, the front staff in their main  Tripoli branch desperately needs training in customer service. Their front servers need to THOROUGHLY clean their tables AND chairs. Their cashiers need to be better responsive to customers' requests. Both the front staff and cashiers seem aloof and uncaring. A smiley, friendly face is a dime a dozen over there.

address: Rasmasqua Tripoli Highway, Tripoli 0096 Lebanon
review_text: It is very good restaurant the food is excellent, the view is out of this world, all the ocean is in front of you from up a hill, the service is very good, there is access for wheelchairs, it is a must visit if you come to Lebanon. The food price is very reasonable.

address: El Mina Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Amzzaing food and very good quality really need to visit so many different types of food and so many places you can go to the restaurant especially the one in the mountains

address: El Mina Street, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: I visited the restaurant couple of times and everytime I am really amazed with the quality of food.  Indeed they are very generous in serving their customers.

address: El Mina Street, Tripoli Lebanon

Also great choice for delivery
excellent customer service

review_text: Very clean restaurant where you have have the typical popular Lebanese food: hommos, Foul, fateh. If you visit Tripoli don’t miss to pass by this restaurant.

address: Al moutran street., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Al Dannoun is famous for its Foul Mudammas and Hummus with the hot bread and free green mint and pickles with olive. It is a must when you visit Tripoly. Price is low.

address: Al moutran street., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Traditional restaurant, tourist point, must visit when going to Tripoli city. Solo or family visit. Average service . Very clean.

address: Al moutran street., Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
Being from Tripoli, Lebanon I grew up on the taste of Dannoun; not only for breakfast but anytime during the day whenever your crave that amazing taste of Humus or Fatteh and Foul. What amazes me is that after leaving the country for like 15 years, it still have the same exquisite taste and original flavor! This is a family business well kept.
Well done and keep it up.

address: Main Street Dam W Farz, Tripoli 3116 Lebanon
review_text: A nice place where one can enjoy goof food at great prices , prompt and punctual service, optimal hygiene and with the help of a very dedicated, friendly and professional staff.

address: Main Street Dam W Farz, Tripoli 3116 Lebanon
review_text: It has a good variety of healthy food. The waitresses and waiters are very friendly helpful and attentive.

address: Main Street Dam W Farz, Tripoli 3116 Lebanon
review_text: This place is one of the worse restaurants I have ever visited. We went to this place after some friends recommended it as the best place to have a breakfast in Tripoli! We drove like 30 minutes to get to this restaurant. Service was super super slow, it took 45 minutes to bring two omelette!!! We were the only people in the restaurant though. The waiter was not friendly and all. The power went off for the entire time we were there, and the manager didn’t hurt herself to even apologize for that inconvenience. To make it worst, there was re-construction for the place, so we had to sit eat in the heat, and hear all the wonderful nose coming from outside. I do not recommend this place.

address: Azmi Street, Tripoli Lebanon
Azmi Streer, Tripoli Lebanon
El Mina, Groopy Roundabout, Next to Shoppies Supermarket, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: Lunch at Sayad's Sandwishes in Tripoli
review_text: Walking into Sayad's Sandwishes, a shabby little kiosk, you might not expect to find some of the freshest fish sandwishes in Tripoli. Made to order, people stand in line to enjoy their tasty fried fish, spicy fish, shrimp, octopus and calamari sandwishes.

address: El Mina, Groopy Roundabout, Next to Shoppies Supermarket, Tripoli Lebanon
review_title: the tinny shop with unforgettable taste
review_text: all kind of sea food sandwiches, only the fresh ingredients and the exceptional cooking technique... Hungry for a quick sea food? do not hesitate

address: El Mina, Groopy Roundabout, Next to Shoppies Supermarket, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Although it has one of the best seafood sandwishes in tripoli, it is really expensive comparing to other restaurants in the city. Anyway, if you wish to have a great taste and fresh ingredients, it still is the best shop

address: El Mina, Groopy Roundabout, Next to Shoppies Supermarket, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Al Sayad is the best ever seafood sandwiches in Lebanon. Fresh shrimps, fish, calamari & octopus prepared with the chef sauce & displayed in front of you on a daily basis. Go there to Mina - mar Elias street / groupi roundabout, ask for the sayad - Ayoub Zaini. You won't regret it.

address: Minot, Tripoli Lebanon

review_title: The best authentic fish restaurant in town
review_text: The best fish restaurant in town. Family business, very nice team, great food and quick service. Bring your alcohol and enjoy a great fresh meal. They introduced non-sea food dishes also.  Price is great. Highly recommended.

address: Labban Street Across Beit El Nessim Tripoli, Mina Lebanon
Jeme'ei Omar, Mina Lebanon
review_title: A wonderful little gem of a restaurant
review_text:
Our food wasn't the best in our all inclusive hotel in Hurghada and we found this little gem of a place round the corner.
Owner and staff were lovely and friendly and made us feel very welcome.
Restaurant was clean and very tidy.
The food was so delicious I had prawns, calamari dishes galore with all the trimmings.
On our last couple of nights they made our butterfly prawns nice and spicey for us nothing was too much for them
The fruit juices were made fresh and were perfect with the meals.
Thank you team you were all wonderful and made our holiday extra special.
Many thanks
Joanne Wright

address: Jeme'ei Omar, Mina Lebanon
review_text: Staff were lovely lobster was so tender and juicy restaurant was very clean and we got a seafood feast for just £14! If you in hurghada this is a must go to

address: Jeme'ei Omar, Mina Lebanon
review_title: Fresh fish is the driver for this place
review_text:
The main course is an easy choice : the waiter brings you to the fresh fish market two steps down the road, you select the fish you want and you get it back grilled or fried back at your table. It cannot be a shorter way from sea to teeth, and price is very competitive. 
Recommended by a local, I approve this place too.

address: Corniche El Mina El Istiklal Street, Tripoli Lebanon
Al-Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text:
The place was recommended by a local and we were not disappointed! 
The view was nice and the food was super delicious, fresh and authentic.
The fish and sauces are very well done, and the price much better than similar fish restaurants closer to Beirut!
Would definitely recommend it..

address: Al-Mina, Tripoli Lebanon
review_text: Such a bad restaurant ...bad experience ..ooover rated...bad service ...cheat bill...written by hand ..poor meza...i dont recommand this place at all.

address: Corniche, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
An excellent local restaurant, chefs do an excellent job with all of the many varieties of fish they offer.  Some say anyone can cook fish, yes, but these guys are really careful and consistent, so it is not over or undercooked.  Salads are also very good, and the grilled eggplant is amongst the best anywhere.  Chicken livers are also very well prepared.   
My most regular meal is a mezze, tabbouleh, rocca, grilled eggplant, chicken livers, and then some malefeh (small grilled barracuda), or sultan ibrahim fish.  
There is also a full range of meat dishes, and I had good lamb on skewers with a friend several times.

address: Castle of Saida, Sidon 1600 Lebanon
review_title: Zawat review ask alot before you order
review_text:
Very disappointed with our recent visit to Zawat. As frequent visitors to Lebanon and long-time patrons of this restaurant, we were shocked to be charged nearly $250 just for fish. The menu listed prices without specifying they were per kilo, the fish wasn’t weighed in front of us, and no price was confirmed before preparing the order. Locals in Saida confirmed we were overcharged — something that feels like we were taken advantage of as foreigners.

To make matters worse, we were charged for parking despite using the restaurant’s own facilities. We’ve dined at many other well-known restaurants across Lebanon, with larger groups and similar meals, and never paid anywhere near this amount.

It’s not about the money — it’s about feeling respected and treated fairly. Sadly, this experience left us feeling scammed and extremely disappointed.

address: Old Saidon Souk Next To The Sea Castle, Sidon Lebanon
review_title: Terrible experience at Saida Rest House
review_text:
Terrible food, terrible service!
I went with my family on the 8th of June 2025, at 9:00 PM, during Eid al-Adha. I understand that the staff might be tired throughout the day and especially in eid but it’s never an excuse to treat customers that way that we were treated!

First, the staff had grim and very grumpy faces, which was super unprofessional! We didn’t say anything, we ordered food and it came quickly ( not surprised since they had barely anyone there except us probably due to their terrible service).

We ordered variety of food from grilled chicken to hot meza, and everything was terrible, anyone that intends to go to such place would expect amazing food and service but both were non existent. We didn’t give them any notes to their food or service we just sat and enjoyed the view.

It all went down to when they placed the check, without us asking for it, but it was fine, it was 11:40 i think. We started dividing the total based on what everyone ordered. They kept coming every 2 minutes asking if the check was ready and we told them that we still need some time until it became 11:50 and the cashier that closes the system came and started shouting at us! At first we were shocked, earlier we asked one of the staff what time do they close he said that when all customers leave, so he didn’t have any right to come barging on us with his terrible attitude demanding to pay the check so he could close the system and go home! That’s your job and like it or not you have to wait, especially for such restaurant. Plus the restaurant says that they close at 12:00 Am so we weren’t passed that time!

And the manager in charge or the one responsible for the reception came and told us that the waiter that told us that they close whenever all costumers leave doesn’t know, which was illogical how would we know that he doesn’t know! His response represents the restaurant so if you wanna blame someone blame your staff and yourself for the ingenuity and unprofessionalism!

We paid and went out, on our way out other customers were leaving too and they also said “m3ash yshofo wejna” which means that they won’t see our faces again probably they had a terrible experience too!

So for anyone that thinks of going there, don’t, it’s not worth the money spent and you could spend way less amount for better food and better service elsewhere. Never again!!

We had a bottle of wine, a fish to share, salad, 2 mezze and water for $40US. 

We’d recommend.

I do hope that they can start playing music later on when people are done with their dinners, but not before. I used to come here a lot before and had great dinners and after party experiences, but this is way too much. I hope the owner implement some changes thinking also about the well-being and harmonious experience of each client, and not just of those who were only want to party. You have a wonderful place to make wonderful memories, but give each clients the opportunity to have a good time without loosing their hearing. 

Thanks for your understanding and I hope you receive my feedbacks well and implement them for the success of your business and the collective health and welfare of your clients. Best wishes,
