address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Just beside Le Phenicien is Restaurant Tony a slightly shabbier but equally charming option. Tony, the owner of this small  white and blue decorated seafood restaurant, will greet you warmly and fry you up the latest catch.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Next door to Al Fakhoury and just as easily missed.
I had two excellent, in terms of choice, quality and price, meals there and I would highly recommend it.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
The restaurant is very small with about 4 tables. The owner spends most of his time trying to attract walkers/tourists to see his place. He doesn’t speak any English. It was an Ethiopian lady that was taken all the order and taking care of it. 

Location is very good. You can have sea view and see the fishermen. Then you can park the car in public space and walk around 750 meters or go almost to the restaurant, but than buy a parking ticket of 5,000 LBP. 

Food taste was mixed. Fresh sea bass fish was great! Grilled always. Hummus too oily and same was with baba ganoush. Cheese rolls were of good taste. Service ok. However it lacked a bit of that atmosphere one aims to see at the local place.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We came here twice, to eat the catch of the day. It's a small simple quite cheap venue. But a lot of ambiance.

address: Old Town Steps Accessible From The Marina Road. Blue Windows, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The owner, Tony, is unprofessional. He didn’t give me my change back. Twice. He got angry when I said I just wanted salad. The view was worth the visit but there are so many restaurants to choose from!! Tyre is incredible, but this restaurant is NOT!

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Great service and the food is even better. Order seafood with a few mezes and your day will be perfect. Nicely situated too.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Located right on the harbor, Le Phenicien is one of Tyre's most upscale restaurants. Specializing in fresh seafood and fish, the restaurant is known for its great service, tasty food and amazing view.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We had a lovely dinner of mezze and a sea bream wrapped in lettuce leaves and grilled. Excellent location by the port.  Not to be missed.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
A good choice. A little expensive but the food is good. 
Very busy, it may be a good idea to make a reservation before.

address: Port De Tyr, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: excellent local sublime cuisine fresh fish and Lebanese salads fantastic compliments ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ excellent wine professional and well organized staff compliments

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Had the opportunity to visit Tyre in June 2022. Discovered YAM via our hosts. The food was amazing as was the service. No less than 8 fresh catches of the day prepared in a variety of ways. We ended up eating there two, or three times! 

Thanks again to the staff and owner for a memorable experience.

Don

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Yam makes the best seafood in Soor. We had grilled and fried fish — it was delicious and so fresh. The atmosphere is warm and friendly. It is very conveniently located - just a one minute walk from the seaside. The service is excellent, and the manager Ibrahim was great. I highly recommend this restaurant!

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: I've been several times and have always been impressed. Offering stunning views of the sea, Shawatina is bright and clean. The service is friendly and efficient. The food is freshly prepared to the highest standards. A very enjoyable experience!

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: What that terrible food and serve it is the last time i will visted here we think that the lebanese food was famous and good but we are unlucky about that hope evrey thing will be good in the future

address: Nabih Berri Street below Shawatina Restaurant, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Nice restaurant in a very good location, facing Resthouse Hotel .It has a marvelous sea view. the food is so delicious . the staff is very friendly and the service is very good. if u like lebanese food , i recommed it .

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Dirty and not friendly staff / to be avoided
review_text: probably the worst starbucks we have visited: the toilets are extra dirty (a disaster) and the team not friendly...to avoid

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Actually it’s so good the service over there is so good and the man is so sweet they deserve the 5 stars

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Horrible quality  compared to legit starbucks experience, and their operating way is annoying  unfriendly staff!, trying to dig costumer service number, you guessed no numbers to do so

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Great Barista service and very friendly staff. I am not sure why some of the popular Starbucks Drinks are not available! I'd blame poor management for not supplying the store with enough supplies to make it a viable place to get your drinks.  Overall, this is a very popular spot for young americanized Lebanese teens, students, and professional entrepreneurs to hangout. This place is my home away from home!

address: Nabih Berri Avenue Tyre South Sea Side, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: this has to be the worst starbucks in the world. i ordered a caramel frapp and they gave me a white drink with whipped cream and asked if i would like a small drizzle of caramel for extra money. it was literally like i ordered a milk drink? also the coffee is gross and is always warm not hot. dont even get me started on their attitude. such rude workers, they think they’re cool and dont know how to treat a customer. 🤦🏻‍♀️🤦🏻‍♀️ it was my first and last time

address: Nabih Berri Boulevard, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Superb food and ambience !!!
Very friendly staff and management !!!
Affordable prices !!!
Visit recomended

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Located on a nice sandy beach with the clear sea. People are very relaxed and nice. 
Plenty of parking space. 
We sat at the far end of the beach bar, closer to do sea.
Otherwise, live DJ music is loud if you want to chat with the friends. Not that it’s a bad thing as it brings atmosphere up but not the best choice if you want to chat.
The waiters are nice and friendly. The sea food was fresh and decently prepared.
The drinks, especially the ones with alcohol, are inconsistent. Every new order of gin&tonic had less and less gin in it.
It’s not that it bothered us much as we truly enjoyed the location and experience overall so visited two days in a row.
Absolutely amazing sunset!!!!

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
A good place to spend the day. the staff were friendly. Amazing scenery. It is nice to spend sunset time there.
The food is good and affordable

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
The Tyre beach may be Lebanon’s nicest beach. It’s clean (comparatively to others), has beautiful sand and clear water. The water warm like a bath. 

So, if choosing a spot to wile away the day, I recommend Cloud 59. The service is excellent but not pushy, and the drinks menu as well as food menu has something for everyone. 

Minimum spend per person $15US for unlimited time. (Min cost depends on seating chosen.)

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
If you want to enjoy the beach wkith a drink and tasty seafood,  it is good to visit this plcae.
The staff is cool and friendly.
The service is fast.
Prices of food are fair, drinks prices could be better

address: Public Beach, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Best spot for food and drinks in Tyre/Sour beach
review_text:
Sour Beach is among the cleanest and best beaches in Lebanon.. And  Cloud 59 is here to give u an upscale experience.
It is one of the few spots where you can have alcoholic drinks, and food is also very good.
It is preferable that you reserve specially if u want a table for lunch. Service is very good and cheerful. 
There's a minimum charge of 10 Usd (weekend) per person that you can spend on drinks or food.
Highly Recommanded.

address: Costal Road, Sur Lebanon
review_text: Iopjkl the same thing as well as other than that of the best of all the other hand is not an intended recipient

address: Borj Ech Chemali Lebanon
review_title: Guys, Tyre is about 120 miles from Zgharta/ Tripoli!!!!
review_text: Guys, Tyre is about 120 miles from Zgharta/ Tripoli!!!! Why do you advertise it as being in Zgharta ??

address: Borj Ech Chemali Lebanon
review_title: The best Asian food in Tyre !
review_text:
From the service to the food and ambiance, i adore this hidden corner in Tyre !
Its friendly and polite staff just add to the calm atmosphere. 
My suggestion, if you don't know what to order ask the charming older gentleman for the recommendation, he never fails to suggest the best and sometimes if you are lucky he might share some amazing stories with you.

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Great environment and very friendly staff. You must visit! Ask for Ghinwa, Mohamed and Khider. The food and desserts are delicious

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: I don't recommend this restaurant at all. Management lies about prices and quantity of food. They offer something and you don't get it or you get the one person quantity to many persons. Then you get the invoice with very high prices than many other better restaurants in the area. Food quality is not good. All this happened when a new owner took over.

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Very bad experience. Portions of one person are served to 4 persons. Very slow service. Prices are very high with respect to portions served. Some food that were in offers are not served but charged in the invoice. Very very bad management. Staff tries to be very helpful in spite of the bad management. The previous management/owner is the best. I don't recommend this restaurant to anyone.

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: a great breakfast with vast variety of foods. lovely location and decoration. service was great. I highly recommend this restaurant

address: Jemeizat Street Sour, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Best Lebanese trendy food in tyre
review_text:
Simply Delicious food  ,  Always fresh clean and same test  great customer service , I believe that this was my 10th visit ,I'm in love with this place  and highly recommended 
Ps don't forget to order kahloons  kenefah

address: Shawakir Cornish, Borj Ech Chemali Lebanon
Tyr Coast, Jarjouaa Lebanon
Alfa Street, Tyre, Tyre Lebanon
Alalesa Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Fresh juices, delicious ice cream, good prices and a local atmoshpere
review_text:
You wanna feel the vibes of Tyre and its locals??
You wanna watch the typical Lebanese family outings: kids playing and parents enjoying themselves?
Niser Juice & Ice-cream have it all. The taste and the atmosphere combined with good prices. 
Did you have an enjoyable day in Tyre? Bathed in The sea and kissed sun? or strolled the ottoman souk and wandered in the colorful old Christian quarter? 
However your trip cannot end without refreshing yourself and boosting a bit your energy or your calories intake by visiting the Niser Juice & Ice-cream. Friendly staff, fresh yummy juice (especially the avocado) and  delicious Arabic/ Italian ice-cream. Whatever you choose Niser Juice will not disappoint you. 
Niser Juice & Ice-cream is the happy ending of a day trip to tyre. Order your juice then go sit at the nearby sea to and watch the sunset or relax in the air-conditioned hall of the Niser.

address: Sour Main Road, Al-Naqoura Iskandarounah, Naqoura Lebanon
review_title: Nice place with a lot to offer.
review_text: This is a nice beach-side restaurant with a lot of potential. The staff are very nice and service is top notch. It has a nice pool but as a woman I didn’t feel comfortable swimming in that atmosphere. The drink and desert menu needs serious improvements. They don’t keep their beach well-maintained. It left me wishing for more, much more!

address: Sour Main Road, Al-Naqoura Iskandarounah, Naqoura Lebanon
review_text:
Very nice location and quiet ! Near the Lebanese Israeli border! 
The sea view fantastic and the food delicious!! To be recommended

address: Sour Main Road, Al-Naqoura Iskandarounah, Naqoura Lebanon
review_text: Went here yesterday with a few friends. The resort is spotless and the pool is great very big. We had the mixed grill plate for $16 great value and delicious washed down with an ice cold beer. The staff are great and there is a lot of them, Well recommended it is a little expensive for the area but worth it. There also is changing rooms for the pool and showers. The views are excellent of both the Med and the mountains.

address: Sour Main Road, Al-Naqoura Iskandarounah, Naqoura Lebanon
review_text: Great quality food and a view to die for. Definitely worth a visit and will come again to spend the day on the beach .

address: Jimayzat Tyre Hosh main road, Tyre Lebanon
lebanon Tiro, Tyre 961 Lebanon
review_text: Nice place. Good preparation of food, fish and calamari, also barracudas on request. Just need a bit more knowledge about English language, but in a someway we could understand each other. Recommended.  I will come back next. Positive impression.

address: lebanon Tiro, Tyre 961 Lebanon
review_title: Easily missed but not to be missed.
review_text:
Tiny place close to Le Phenicien in Tyre/Sour port.
Excellent seafood, with a great Lebanese twist, and very good value.
Would highly recommend it.

address: lebanon Tiro, Tyre 961 Lebanon
review_text:
Small restaurant.
Located on the harbor of Tyre.
Serves national and sea food platters.
Very tasty dishes.
Very friendly owner.
Very affordable prices.
Worth a visit when visiting Tyre.

address: lebanon Tiro, Tyre 961 Lebanon
review_text: Stopped in for a traditional Lebanese lunch and they did not disappoint.  Clean, friendly staff and good food.   The whole restaurant looks over the ocean and small port so it is great to people watch.  Food was fantastic and service was top notch.

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
You want a cheap and good food in Tyre?
Abou Deeb is a good option where you can enjoy shawarma (the beef one is good), cocktail and fresh juices, chicken sandwiches, fattouch...
The service is swift.
Prices are cheap and portions are fair.

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: they don't respect their customers and lousy quality of food especially abbasieh branch I don't recommend for anyone to have my terrible experience

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Our guide took us here prior to touring Tyre. It was a good choice. There was few other patrons due to Ramadan. Had a couple of appetizers. In particular, the tabbouleh had a great level of mint in it. We ordered from menus but you could also go up to the counter to get items. There was a good range of plates, sandwiches and appetizers. The place was very clean. It had tables inside and outside. The food came quickly enough, With a late arriving flight last night, it was a fine introduction into Lebanese fare. With a 7-Up and tip, lunch was just 10,000 LL, less than $7 US.

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Nice place where to share time with the local people
review_text: Nice and clean place with typical lebanese food, where to share the time with the locals, thought a little crowded at lunch time, mainly on the weekends. They have take away service. They have many different amazing fruit juices.

address: Abou Deeb Street and Al Kouds Street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Had lunch there with family. The place offers a good variety of sandwiches and Lebanese plates. Try the Chinese chicken. Very good prices.

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: This was a special service where you book a table of capacity of eight persons, then the chef will make cook for you from the tipinyaki menu, with nice cook show and soft music, this experience was extraordinary, the food was more than perfect, if you are on a special date or occasion or want to impress someone just go for it. 😋😍👍

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: The place is nice and lively. The views are not stunning but are better than much of what you can get in Tyre. We didn't have lunch there as we were not appealed by Mexican food in Lebanon. We had two coffees instead. And it took ages to have them served. And even longer to pay. It was nice to see locals spending the afternoon there with food and sheesha.  A perfect place to chill a bit after a long journey from Beirut.

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Good place to meet friends beside the Med
review_text: I often meet friends in Tyre here at La Playa.  It used to have an upstairs section but this has disappeared.  They serve a limited range of Lebanese food - mezza, sandwiches and plated meals.  There are always a lot of people smoking shisha, usually outside on the terrace, but increasingly inside.  (I am anti-smoking).  Good for fresh juices, coffee and moderately priced food.  Service is friendly, polite and prompt.  Good views of the sea and the evening cruisers.

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Good cafe with sea view, and if you like Shisha this place would be good for you, the prices are more than others arround and best day to visit is SAT but you need to make sure to call before you go.

address: Nabih berri street, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: We planned a visit after hearing so much about this place . When we got there it was pretty crowded but we managed to find a comfy quiet seat on the second floor .The service was very good the food was good with lots of varieties i would say there speciality is sea food ,the prices are a bit on the high side but still affordable .I would definitely go back !!

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text: I used to live in the same building, I used to order dinner some times. Internaitonal food, good quality and good sushi, but with high prices, because it is at the corniche of Tyre the most fashion and expensive area of town.

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text: This is not my first passby to this restaurant but first to try their sushi menu, the service is so fast the dishes are just so yummy 😋 and the kids enjoyed there time, the stuff are cheerful and friendly 👍

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text:
Very nice place, good location ,good service and best of all delicious Italian food . For the sushi lovers , it is the only place that serves it in town.
I recommend for sure.

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text: This is an asian resturant its good but the staff not good should change the staff but the suchi is very delecious and tasty hope to come agaian with another staff and manager will be come back a twice time for this place

address: Rue Nabih Berri, Tyre 0096 Lebanon
review_text:
I went with my wife to have sushi for dinner as we read the reviews about the good sushi they offer. The surprise was when the second piece was too crunchy with a loud noise to discover that there is a 1 cm piece of glass in the sushi piece!
The staff justification was that the glasses in the restaurant, when broken, they splash all around & probably that's why the piece is in the rice!!
I didn't dare then to eat more sushi as it may contain more glass.

address: Tyre's Old Souk Fishermen Port, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: If you are a foul, hummos or balila fan, this place is a must visit. Located in the old souks of Tyre, it's one of the oldest and most authentic foul places in Lebanon

address: Tyre's Old Souk Fishermen Port, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Absolutely the best ful in Lebanon
review_text:
You have to taste the famous Baroud ful! It is the most delicious preparation of this traditional Lebanese fava bean dish I have ever had. It is only rivaled by one other of their own dishes 'msabha', which is chic peas. And their hummos is the tastiest, creamiest, most addictive you will ever experience. I say 'experience' because eating at Baroud is beyond just eating. No matter how many times I taste it, I can't help but literally say 'oh wow, yum!' It is simple, yet oh so satisfying. Douse it olive oil and scoop it up with the freshly baked pita bread. Comes with a plate of sliced tomatoes, Lebanese pickles, onion and fresh mint. And then there are the olives--so good!

The service is friendly and super efficient. This is where the locals go to get breakfast. (open till around noon) The seating is basic plastic chairs at wooden tables situated in the busy souk. Delicious, fresh, traditionally made healthy food at very reasonable prices.

address: Tyre's Old Souk Fishermen Port, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: A great traditional foul place in the heart of the old souk of tyre. Everything is so tasty there, but the balila was super extra there, so I recommend it.

address: Tyre's Old Souk Fishermen Port, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: My favorite fool restaurant in Tyre
review_text: Very simple setting. The staff is friendly and the food is the best in Tyre. My favorite dishes are fool and msabaha. Recommend: Yes for sure

address: Tyre's Old Souk Fishermen Port, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: One of the most delicious lebanese authentic food specially for breakfast, good service, excellent price , and a plate of lebanese foul , hummos you will never forget.

address: Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Felt like having a sushi dinner and a friend recommended this place. 
We were not disappointed. Ordered few sushi dishes and seaweed salad.
“Seaweed” salad was delicious and fresh, however we wanted to have seaweed and there was just a hint of it.
Otherwise all good. Looked clean and nice. 
It’s within a shopping mall but with the terrace. 
Good place to have various Asian dishes when in Tyr.

address: Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Our go-to restaurant when we feel like Asian food. Tested the whole menu and we come back for our favorite dishes. Sushi seems to be the best in town.

address: Old City, Tyre Lebanon
review_title: Terrible service. Food not at all 5 stars
review_text:
Probably the worst 5 star restaurant I have ever visited.
The waiters were totally unfamiliar with the menu. Couldn't explain basic things we asked.
We were 4 . 3 drinks arrived OK. The 4th never arrived untill we asked for it again 
Being on diet . I requested the steak to come with a simple side salad instead of fries and veggies. The waiter said its impossible. No way can this be done ,even after he went to enquire about my request.
Called the manager and he said "no problem and we agreed to be a simple lettuce and cucumber with No tomatoes. " 
3 meals arrived 1 didn't. When I enquired after 3 or 4 minutes . The waiter said , my medium steak takes longer than the well-done steak my friend ordered. Hmmmm
He brought the salad . Lettuce and TOMATO , no cucumber. I called the manager who insisted I ordered lettuce and Tomato. Hmmmmm I didn't 
 I ordered lettuce and cucumber and made it absolutely clear that I said NO TOMATO. . He eventually apologised and swapped it.
My stake came. ( Argentinian grass fed steak) I doubt it. It was not as tough as boots but by no means was  it lean/ soft.
Had to chew quite hard on it.
My wife's coffee tasted strange
The salmon salad was dry. ( almost no dressing on the salad)
Would give this restaurant 3 stars if I am being generous . Not a 5 star boutique Hotel restaurant. Deeply disappointed . Overpriced too

address: Old City, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: elegant restaurant, friendly and knowledgeable staff.  Cuisine rich in fresh fish that is to say all the appetizers are excellent to try salad with fresh seasonal fruit.  The staff always available attentive to their customers to meet the needs excellent wine.️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

address: Old City, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Nice hotel overlooking the sea.
Free parking on the road or in front of the hotel. 
Room was 3rd floor with double doors onto a balcony with table 2 chairs overlooking sea. 
2nd floor rooms have a balcony with sunloungers.
Assam on breakfast was a very good waiter.
Short walk into town for nice selection of restaurants.
Public beach is a mile away on foot.
Loads of cafes on beach.

address: Old City, Tyre Lebanon
review_text: Excellent location. Tasty food and very beautiful place to be with family and friends. Good service. I recommend strongly

address: Old City, Tyre Lebanon
review_text:
Pretty boutique hotel. So clean & food was excellent and all the Staff were very friendly. The suit is very spacious, comfortable with a nice sea view from the balcony!
Simply,
El Boutique Hotel, the best in Sour, Tyre, Lebanon

address: Thoum Seaside Road, Batroun, Batroun District, Batroun 1400 Lebanon
review_text: This place is a hidden gem. Food is great atmosphere is wow and most importantly owners make  their best to insure customer satisfaction. It's the chill place in Batroun with no compromise.

address: Thoum Seaside Road, Batroun, Batroun District, Batroun 1400 Lebanon
review_text:
This place is just by the beach, a wonderful beach site specially on sunsets, lovely at night, with nice not too loud Music. The staff are very friendly,  however, they lack a bit of Menu Variety of SEAFOOD, However the food is fresh and very tasty.. It is worth your money. We sat by the beach, even swam a bit. It is a very nice family place, which needs to have some renovation indoors and outdoors, update the seats and tables, give more a new look ambiance with the Mediterranean Beach breath taking view...
I would definitely come again

address: Thoum Seaside Road, Batroun, Batroun District, Batroun 1400 Lebanon
review_title: Best treatment best food best swim
review_text: The best good and treatment you can get. Staff are super friendly and helpful. Owners are heaven sent. Most importantly the food is yummy and well priced. Going every week end

address: Thoum Seaside Road, Batroun, Batroun District, Batroun 1400 Lebanon
review_text:
Food cooked to perfection especially the freshly caught fish.
Great setting with professional staff and lovely owners.

address: Thoum Seaside Road, Batroun, Batroun District, Batroun 1400 Lebanon
review_text:
We feel like we are between our family and friends when we go there.
The food is so tasty the music is very nice and the staff are super friendly.
For sure will go back and I highly recommend this place

address: Kfaraabida, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: Amazing Beach Party - Do not miss it!
review_text:
This place had wonderful load music playing until sooo late. Many people dancing and singing. Takes place outdoors.

Not every night though..when I was there they had this on Wed & Friday night.

The Thursday night didnt have a party.

Wonderful!!!

address: Kfaraabida, Batroun Lebanon
review_text: We were 4 couples and booked bungalows to stay over the weekend. The bungalows were smelly, bugs came out of bathroom and the shower doors were broken. The beach is very dirty. We cancelled our second night as we couldn’t stay longer. Very disappointing experience and i wouldn’t recommend it at all

address: Kfaraabida, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: Great  vibe great Shisha & great vibes
review_text: This is a good spot to tan all day or have a shisha and see the sunset. Great and easy access bar. Friendly staff and you can also take some surf lessons if the weather permits.

address: Kfaraabida, Batroun Lebanon
review_text: If you want to have a great day at the beach, enjoy beautiful people and beautiful weather, you MUST go to Barraudas. All seafood on the very limited menu. More finger foods than anything. The beach bodies are amazing, plenty of beautiful women to enjoy. Cold beers and drinks when you like, served by a very good staff that is always ready with fresh drinks. Make sure you spend at least a few hours here to enjoy the sand and sea.

address: Kfaraabida, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: Excellent Thursday night performance and ambiance
review_text:
One of the best live bands in Lebanon
Excellent repertoire
Good sound system 
I recommend this place and would definitely return there

address: Main Street, Batroun 1212 Lebanon
review_text: This is my first bad review but I guess it is necessary. I’ve always enjoyed your restaurant, but our recent visit was very disappointing. I received my salad half an hour before my husband’s order, so I had to eat alone. When his salmon finally arrived, the mashed potatoes were cold. The chef dismissed our concern, saying that’s how it’s served, though the manager later agreed with us. Unfortunately, we left hungry and frustrated, with no apology or gesture to make up for the experience.

address: Main Street, Batroun 1212 Lebanon
review_title: Mashed potatoes cannot be served hot
review_text:
I went with my wife and we ordered 2 plates, one crispy salmon and one Grilled Salmon Pineapple.

The grilled salmon arrived half an hr after the crispy salmon, their reason was it takes more time (you can start cooking the one that takes time first). The crispy salmon has mashed potatoes with it which arrived cold as if it was in the fridge (the chef's reason was that it can't be heated because it has milk, so it is not freshly done which is a worse outcome).

The end result worst experience and full bill paid.

address: Main Street, Batroun 1212 Lebanon
review_text: Great food with a creative twist and kind service, beautiful sea view right at the beach. Highly recommend this fun and simple place for chill and enjoyment.

address: Main Street, Batroun 1212 Lebanon
review_text: This restaurant is truly a gem! The staff are incredibly welcoming and attentive, and the food is absolutely delicious. Every dish is full of flavor and beautifully served. Can’t wait to come back again! Special thanks to Charbel, ask for him once you're there

address: Main Street, Batroun 1212 Lebanon
review_text: The atmosphere was absolutely amazing, the  staff extremely kind. The food was incredible, terrific presentation. The portion size was very generous! Ask for Charbel, he is super accommodating!

address: Marea, Batroun Lebanon
restaurant_name: Meat The Fish At The Beach

address: Sea side road, Batroun Lebanon
Batrouniyat St, Batroun | Next to Dagher Pharmacy, Batroun 0000 Lebanon
review_title: Great Lebanese homemade food in Batroun

address: Makaad El Mir, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: Disappointing Dining Experience at Chez Maguy
review_text:
Our visit to Chez Maguy in Batroun was a letdown from start to finish. The tabbouleh was overwhelmingly salty, the batata harra and fried fish were undercooked, and the grilled shrimp hadn’t been cleaned, leaving us to do it ourselves at the table. On top of that, the service was frustrating—an older gentleman from the staff took our salad plate before we were done and insisted it was finished, despite our objection.

We also ordered a grilled fish based on their recommendation, and while the choice itself was good, we were shocked when the bill arrived—$40 for a single piece of fish. Between the poorly prepared food, the dismissive service, and the unexpected price tag, this was not an experience I’d want to repeat or recommend.

address: Bahsa, Batroun Lebanon
review_text:
I had one of the worst dining experiences ever at Swiss Butter in Batroun. The food was mediocre at best, but the real problem was the dishonest way they handle orders. The waitress recommended a ribeye that wasn’t even on the menu and never told us the price — which turned out to be a shocking $55 per plate. We ordered it twice, still never being informed of the cost, only to find out at the end when the bill came. This is deceptive, unethical, and unacceptable. Any restaurant with integrity tells customers the price before selling something, especially when it’s outrageously overpriced. The service was disappointing, the experience left a bad taste in every way, and I will warn others so they don’t get ripped off the way we did.

address: Batroun, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: "Exorbitant Prices and Slow Service Mar Otherwise Promising Venue"
review_text:
The venue boasts a commendable location, strategically positioned near the highway in northern Lebanon, not far from the city of Batroun. They offer an electric cart service to transport guests from the entrance to the beach, where an array of comfortable tables and chairs are available. The service, albeit courteous, is noticeably slow. The DJ requires a considerable amount of time to play invigorating music; initially, the music was exceedingly monotonous, only improving after 10:00 PM when livelier tunes finally emerged. Unfortunately, by this time, a significant portion of my friends had already left, disheartened by the earlier dull atmosphere. 

The food, while decent, is exorbitantly priced. A bottle of gin, which typically costs $25, was outrageously marked up to $128. Our modest consumption of some snacks and soft drinks, along with the gin, culminated in a staggering bill of $462. The pricing is egregiously inflated, posing a severe detriment to tourism. In numerous other countries with far superior infrastructure (such as roads, cleanliness, and service) — like Spain, France, Italy, Greece, Kazakhstan, and even Dubai — the maximum expenditure per person, including excellent food and beverages, would be approximately $40. Adding insult to injury, the valet parking demanded an additional $5.50 as a fee. In many countries, parking is complimentary when dining at a restaurant.

In Lebanon, it seems exploitation is rampant, extending beyond just the banks and political system. I intend to boycott all restaurants and pubs this summer and strongly urge others to do the same to compel these establishments to reevaluate their prices and cease their exploitative practices.

However, upon arrival, our tables were given away. After a long wait, we were seated near the restrooms with no resolution. The entire staff, including the unhelpful manager, failed to address our concerns. This falls far short of the promises made during the booking process. We hope Butlers takes this feedback seriously and improves their service for future guests. #dissatisfiedcustomer #poorcustomerservice #butlersrestaurant"

address: 13 Mina Street, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: Rude Service and Poor Quality at Oysters – Never Again
review_text:
I had a very disappointing experience at Oysters this year. We ordered grilled calamari and were served only the tentacles instead of the actual calamari, which absolutely did not match the price. When we complained, both the waiter and the manager were extremely rude, insisting “this is how we serve it.” We even showed them the restaurant’s own Instagram photos, which clearly showed a completely different dish, but the manager dismissed us by saying those pictures are just for advertising.

I visited Oysters back in 2023 and had a wonderful experience — but this time, it was the complete opposite. The fish were tasteless, the fried shrimps were served with the heads and not cleaned properly, and the calamari was a total disaster. Not only did they refuse to apologize, but the manager was unprofessional, impolite, and even threw some unnecessary words at us before walking away.

We didn’t mind paying for the dish, but we simply didn’t want it. Instead of handling the situation with professionalism, the management’s attitude made everything worse. Honestly, this visit wasn’t even worth one star.

address: 13 Mina Street, Batroun Lebanon
review_title: IF IN BATROUN AND YOU SEE A RESTAURANT CALLED OYSTERS THST LOOKS SO INVITING FOR A SUNSET, DO NOT GO THERE!
review_text:
My last night in Lebanon, I go to watch the sunset and see this charming seafood place called Oysters near St Estephan Church.
I ask for oysters, the one server who spoke English said they didn’t have any, then said he would check. 10 minutes later he says yes, they have 6. I’ve eaten oysters all over the world and this place looked safe…..but it is NOT.
The oysters looked funny and had a white substance dripping off them. The server told me it was “the sauce they put on them”. Sauce? What? So I tried one with the least milky stuff on it and it tasted rancid. I spit it out into a napkin. 
I told the staff they are bad, and these were likely sitting out for hours.
They insisted I had to pay, and get this, they were 5 USDs an oyster!!! No oysters in the US are that expensive.
I was told to wait for the owner. 30 minutes later he walks up and I explained what happened. The 2 servers who served me ran off in their car. Apparently another oyster was missing. I swore I did not even eat the one and spit it out. 
He told me there was nothing he could do, he must pay for each oyster. I told him if he went to a restaurant and the food was bad, would he pay? He said “Of course”
WRONG!!!!
AVOID THIS PLACE! Maybe I was naive and stupid to try and eat oysters in Lebanon, but I have eaten many places and should’ve really been more careful, but just as a warning to others….DO NOT EAT HERE!
I usually write good reviews, but this could be one of my worst ones ever!

address: Mina street, Batroun Lebanon
review_text:
This seafood restaurant stood out as a culinary revelation that defied our expectations. Despite initially being overshadowed by a couple of other higher-ranked seafood restaurants in the area, we decided to give Bistr’eau a try as our first meal, upon arriving in Batroun. Little did we know, it would turn out to be the culinary highlight of our stay, ultimately the place we selected as our last meal and the grand finale of our time in Batroun.

Situated beneath a series of picturesque succession of ornate arches along the waterfront, within one of Batroun's historical buildings at the port of the Batroûn Old Souk, this restaurant stood out as a true outlier. What made this dining experience truly exceptional was the symphony of flavors orchestrated by the fusion of cuisines, the inventive pairing of traditional and rarely-used ingredients, and the meticulous execution of each dish. Between our two visits, we embarked on a culinary journey through almost the entire menu, relishing every morsel.

The appetizers alone were a testament to the culinary prowess at play – from the refreshing Fattouch to the innovative Hummus with shrimp, the smoky allure of the aubergine with sundried tomato tarator, and fresh zaatar. The culinary exploration continued with Grilled Calamari, Sicilian octopus, shrimp fokhara with tomato sauce, and perfectly roasted Patatas Bravas. All this food only served to pave the way for the triumphant main courses—seafood risotto and the pièce de résistance, a perfectly seasoned deep-fried whole fish. The culinary symphony reached its crescendo with the Pain Perdu à la mode, offering the perfect sweet note to conclude our culinary odyssey.

review_text:
I absolutely loved my experience at Taqueria Freedah! The tacos were bursting with flavor—perfectly seasoned and cooked to perfection. The homemade guacamole was a standout: fresh, creamy, and packed with just the right amount of lime and spice. I also tried the catch of the day ceviche, and it was incredible—super fresh, light, and zesty. And don’t even get me started on the churros—crispy on the outside, soft on the inside, and served with a heavenly dipping sauce.

Everything tasted homemade and authentic, and the service was friendly and welcoming. This place is definitely a must-visit for anyone craving top-notch Mexican cuisine. Highly recommend!

review_text:
This marks my third visit to Majlis Al Sultan; a delightful restaurant in Me’aisem City Center mall - Dubai.  International cuisine perfectly blended with local flavors, creating a unique dining experience. The spacious layout caters wonderfully to smokers and shisha enthusiasts, making it a relaxed environment where guests can unwind and enjoy their time.

From the moment we arrived, the service was impeccable. We were warmly greeted by a smiley Algerian hostess (unfortunately, I don’t  remember her name) who guided us to our table while taking the time to explain some of the restaurant’s features and menu highlights. Her attentiveness set a positive tone for the evening.

The atmosphere at Majlis Al Sultan is vibrant yet cozy, making it an ideal spot to catch a football game while indulging in a delicious meal. The blend of flavors in the dishes is truly commendable, with each bite showcasing the restaurant’s commitment to quality and authenticity.

Whether you’re looking to enjoy a leisurely dinner or catch a game with friends, Majlis Al Sultan offers a fantastic setting complemented by excellent service.

address: Al Talay Street Beside Avantgarde Warehouse, Opposite Naresco Contracting, Al Quoz, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Dubai Amaravathi is a predominantly South Indian restaurant which also serves veg and non-veg North Indian food; its Hyderabadi biryanis, in fact, are quite popular. Located in a warehouse-style building beside the Avantgarde warehouse, opposite Naresco Contracting on Al Talay Street in Al Quoz, Amaravathi reminded me of the labour camp eateries we’d dine at in Jebel Ali in the early 90’s. There’s nothing wrong with that mind you, I had some of the best South Indian breakfasts in those restaurants. And with that in mind my food buddies and I visited Amaravathi for our last breakfast before Ramadan.

Looks can be deceiving but in this case, they’re not; what you see from the outside is pretty much what you get on the inside. The interior is basic. Shockingly bright colours liven up its windowless walls; an almost florescent yellow can be a little in-your-face early in the morning. Typical of this sort of restaurant there is a separate family section towards the back. The surprisingly clean toilets were back there too. Along the wall we sat near was a hyperactive delivery counter where parcels were placed for pick up by their busy delivery team. I would have liked to have placed a video to record one day in the life of that counter. 

We started our breakfast with a idli-vada dip, which is basically a vada and two idlis in a bowl of sambar. The idlis were light and fluffy and easily broke apart with my fork (some are like plastic and actually need an effort). Although the sambar could have been a smidge tarter, I liked it, even though it was only lukewarm. Upon informing our smiley waiter of this, he immediately brought us all fresh piping hot bowls. Smiley obviously had his eye on our table because he timed the arrival of our masala dosas perfectly, no more than a minute after we had finished our idli-vada. While not as big as some of the hard-core Karama dosas, ours were crispy, not too oily and the potato filling was lovely. Our meal would have been perfect if they had filter coffee. The sugary instant stuff they served us in its place was the only disappointment of an otherwise lovely breakfast.

All of us were impressed with the friendliness of the staff as well as how well our waiter looked after us. I’m sure it helped that we were one of only two tables which were occupied. That, and the fact that we were probably not their typical diners. Irrespective of why, they did a great job, earning our thanks and a big tip. Dubai Amaravathi joins Chennai Curry Palace in the recently opened Al Khail Gate Community Centre as my favourite go-to places for South Indian breakfast in Al Quoz. I have heard so much about their biryanis that I have to go back for a try. If ambiance is not too high on your list of priorities this is definitely an outlet worth a visit.

Hey! Now read this –
Xerxes physically dines at, or orders from, each and every venue he reviews. He pays in full for whatever he and his companions eat, drink, take away or occasionally throw at each other. Xerxes accepts no money, gifts, discounts or free meals in return for reviews or favouritism. What you have read was NOT influenced in any way by the venue.

review_text:
The Grey Al Wasl has a nice ambiance and a modern design, and their matcha is really good. However, the vegan options are quite limited. I love açaí, but the only granola available contains honey, which makes it unsuitable for vegans. It would be great if they offered a vegan alternative.

I also had a very frustrating experience with the Wi-Fi. I visited on Wednesday (March 19) and asked the staff multiple times for help connecting, but they just kept telling me to “wait 7 seconds” without actually solving the issue. In the end, I couldn’t use the internet at all, and no one provided a clear explanation or an alternative solution. This really ruined my impression of the place, as I expected better service.

address: 9 A Street, Al Wasl Road, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
One of the most disappointing places I’ve visited in Dubai. The menu is extremely limited, and everything is grossly overpriced. They are claiming the concept of creativity.
The food presentation is very basic and lacks creativity, with portions that are small and far from satisfying. Even the coffee was served in an unusually small cup.

The back garden seating area is average at best—nothing special or worth mentioning. Overall, this place feels completely overrated and comes across as a scam.

We ordered almost everything was on the menu and the taste was really poor and lacks taste of freshness.

We were a group of five and ended up paying around AED 700 for a simple breakfast, which was absurd given the poor quality and experience. There are far better cafés in Dubai that offer superior ingredients, a more diverse menu, and far better value for money.

 I also felt it could use a stronger strawberry flavour, came premixed and was a tad under filled which I personally dislike.

address: 2 Al Nessnass St Behind Sunset Mall, Dubai 00000 United Arab Emirates
review_title: Never gonna visit this place again, I felt so bad for the first time in Dubai
review_text:
I visited Feels Café today and had a very disappointing experience. They have a digital queue system, and I received a message saying our table was ready. However, when we arrived, we were told to wait again. A couple who came after us was given a table before we were, and when I mentioned that we were ahead of them, no one seemed to care.

I was there with my 2-year-old son and my 75-year-old mother, and we ended up standing in the middle of the restaurant for about 20 minutes *after* being told our table was ready. Not a single staff member offered my elderly mother a seat while waiting. The couple where was one lady and GAY guy who took the table even said, “We don’t care, it’s our table,” which made the whole situation feel even more rude and heartless.

I understand that it’s a busy restaurant, but there should be a proper system in place, and basic courtesy should never disappear. This was my first and definitely my last visit to FEELS cafe

review_text:
How UNHYGIENIC and GROSS!!! 
Was craving for some juicy burger after beach day.
Food wise was great. The price was actually cheap for that size burger.
However, the nasty part is how the service staff “cleaned” the area. The corner table dropped some trashes like burger papers and plastic gloves on the floor (due to the wind). The service crew just kicked it off the terrace to the parking area. Like she could use some  broom and dust pan (which they actually have).
After eating, we finished only half of the bruger and asked to have packed for take away. Another nastiness and unhygienic practice that the service staff did, was that she put all the used napkins on the plate where I still have half of my burger to pack. I was stunned. Mind you, she’s the same service crew who kicked the trashes off the terrace instead of picking it up. She then gave the packed leftover. I just threw it out of the bin.
Never going back again! 🤢

review_title: Lovely breakfast Brunch with Kids + Great Service
review_text:
Breakfast Brunch with Kids at Harrie’s Pancakes!

My husband and I always loved going to Harrie’s, so bringing the kids along this time was a no-brainer. We went for the All You Can Eat Breakfast Brunch, and honestly, it didn’t disappoint.

⸻

🍽️ The Food - We ordered from the All You Can Eat Brunch, which runs Friday to Sunday, 8AM–1PM, and costs AED 85 per person – such good value for what you get!

The food was fresh, tasty, and full of flavour.one of the highlights was the Boerewors Shakshouka – scrambled eggs, tomato and onion relish, and beef sausage all wrapped in a pancake, absolutely delicious!

Also another favourite was the Chocolate Spread Pancake, it was rich, indulgent, and served with fresh seasonal fruit. Perfect for both adults and kids.

⸻

👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 The Service - Sillah served us and was amazing from start to finish. So kind and thoughtful with the kids, he brought over a high chair straight away and gave them colouring and paper which kept them busy, letting us take a moment to settle in and look at the menu. He also gave us great food recommendations which we loved. Honestly, 5-star service from him and the whole team.

⸻

📍 The Location - Located at Nakheel Mall, The Palm (Level 2), it’s just a 30-second walk to the arcade (Fabyland), which was perfect for the kids after they’d eaten.

⸻

We had a lovely morning, great food, lovely service, and a location that works well for families. If you’re looking for more family-friendly spots like this in Dubai, follow @mumsandgist_dubai on Instagram for more hidden gems and honest reviews.

address: First Floor, Reem Mall, Jumeirah Beach Road Near Emirates Islamic Bank, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Emphasising the “mmm” in ‘mmmbox’ because that’s exactly what we said the moment we dug into their dimsums, starters and curry. Starting off with their ‘Edamame & Truffle’: delicately wrapped dimsums filled with mashed edamame beans with a subtle note of truffle oil served with warm soy sauce and elegantly presented with whole edamame beans on top. A vegetarian dish that took us by surprise- mellow, simple, fresh and loved the beauty of it. Next up is ‘Spicy Rock Shrimp Tempura’: a starter that you just got to have! . Crispy rock shrimp generously tossed and coated in dynamite sauce is every spice lovers “ohhh yeesss”. Loved the crispy texture and hints of spice and the sauce is, we couldn’t stop popping these into our mouths. 

You can’t forget to order their curries, we had, ‘Green Curry Chicken’: a famous thai cuisine filled with fragrant and aromatic flavours that pulls you right in. Thinly sliced chicken cooked in thick green gravy conveying rich flavours of kaffir lime, galangal, spices and coconut milk paired with sticky rice got us saying “mmmmm” once again. I love thai cuisine and this right here hit the spot. Do keep in mind I’ve re-plated this dish so the quantity is not what it seems, head over to my story/highlights ‘mmmbox pt 2’ to know their original portions.

 ‘Sushi Triple Box’ - what’s cool about this is you can choose any three sushi flavours for a price of 69/- (aed) and each sushi tray has 4 pieces. Here’s what we got for our box- ‘Hanna’: this eat had all the go-to ingredients for any sushi, Spicy Tuna, Cucumber, Avocado all wrapped in Salmon and topped with more goodness, scored high on freshness. 

‘Enchanted Forest’: I love the name of this sushi and it’s their new flavour as well. A vegetarian dish elegantly bundled with Enoki, Avocado and the Sesame Wafu dressing made all the difference. A simple sushi that surprised us, we love the slight crunch of the enoki- has its own flavour. 

‘Dynamite’: now, now, spicy sushi’s are a must have they are just deliciously-good! . This goodie has Prawn Tempura wrapped inside, topped with slices of Tuna and generously drizzled with their Spicy Mayo Sauce is the definition of “food bomb” in our mouths. Too good of a sushi this flavour was- the crispy texture of the tempura, spicy mayo was soothed by the rice and the coolness of the sliced tuna made this a perfect bundle. 

Do check my stories/highlights ‘mmmbox pt 2’ for more on their food or check their menu- link in their bio, they have a wide range of scrumptious eats catering to vegetarians and non-vegetarians as well. If you don’t like raw sushi they got many others to choose from too.

Mmmbox surely brightened up our cravings with their deliverables and definitely their funky and cool presentation. Love their no-mess-style-packaging, just perfect for outings especially picnics. Fun colors, recyclable cutlery and ofcourse THE FOOOOODDDDDD. A second timer experiencing their goodies and it blew us away once again. This eatery is highly recommended, perfect east asian flavours.

address: First Floor, Reem Mall, Jumeirah Beach Road Near Emirates Islamic Bank, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Just in time for the weekend we received these scrumptious dishes. “Yummmy meals in a box” is exactly what they catered because we have to talk about how funky and upbeat these packages are, as you can see in the pictures I’ve posted it is certain how carefully prepared and packed each of them are into containers, then right into their signature boxes, and the curry container was heat sealed to prevent any spillage. A lot of thought process has been given as to how the freshness of their eats should be retained till it has been delivered. We definitely love how vibrant the boxes look, brightened up our moods entirely and we were super excited to gobble them up!.

Here’s what we got- ‘Chilli Cheese’: this right here was a mellow bomb in our mouths!, it tasted so good. Nah, this has nothing to do with being crazy spicy or anything to do with chilli’s being used in the filling itself, it’s rather a subtle touch of it as a side sauce to bring about this dim sum together. Stuffed in these rectangular pieces are a simple blend of mushrooms, water chestnut, cream cheese with chilli oil. Definitely loved the uniqueness of this eat, absolutely recommended!. ‘Chicken & Chilli Oil’: these dim sums were quite aromatic as we opened the box, the chilli oil sauce hits you right in the spot. Stuffed with chicken, covered with vinegar chilli oil and brown garlic, don’t be fooled this dish packs a flavourful punch. The sauce wasn’t too spicy nor did the acidity from the vinegar overwhelm these steamed pouches, and that hint of garlic made it perfect. 

From the asian curry box we got- ‘Black Bean Fish’: fragrant just as you open it, you know you’re hungry when you smell this. Succulent and moist pieces of fish covered in black bean curry, garlic, spring onions and whole dried chilli pieces as garnishes, this right here tasted scrumptious. The seasoning of the curry was just right, not too spicy or bland. We can taste the ingredients used here especially the black beans with little hints of garlic. We loved the fact that they chose to pair these with seafood and sticky white rice, just delicious.

Why order a single serving of sushi when you can have three different flavours in one box. Such a cool concept by mmmbox , here’s what our box of happiness consists of: ‘Wafu Salmon & Avocado’ - new to the menu this bundle had fresh avocados wrapped up in your usual seaweed and rice and topped with thinly sliced raw salmon and ofcourse the best part, the wafu dressing, loved this ideal combo of simplicity and softness and the japanese dressing just draws you in one bite. ‘Rock & Roll Shrimp’ - now this was a big bite, tasted amazing!. Shrimp in tempura batter fried till golden and, tossed in some spicy mayo. Not only is this unique sushi topped with shrimp, it is also stuffed inside. We liked how this wasn’t so spicy and the use of mayonnaise was just the right amount so as to not drown its true tones, certainly was a flavourful-rocky ride. ‘Enoki & Truffle’ - this combination of ingredients blew our minds, the proper and subtle use of truffle oil was a winner. It wasn’t overwhelming at all, the crispy enoki tempura, with the japanese mayo, all bundled up into a delicious roll was too good!. Like...that’s it, it was done, wholesome and wow!, highly recommended. 

Each of these sushi’s are served with soy sauce in cute sized glass bottles and wasabi/ginger pickle. If you’re wondering how would these cold eats remain fresh during this sweltering heat during your deliveries, no worries, they have considered all that whilst packing their goodies, check my instagram (@the.forensic.foodess) stories/highlights under ‘mmmbox 🥢’ to know what I mean. Freshness of their food is guaranteed!. Also, if you aren’t a sushi person due to raw seafood, they do have other varieties to choose from both vegetarian and non-vegetarian, so just go for it.

Such a stylish eatery to order from, ideal for picnics, eat from the comfort of your home, workplace, a snug night in or just call them in for a casual get together with your friends/family, you don’t have to worry about washing any dishes or messing up your place. They have a variety dishes to get your tastebuds going and tough decisions to make too, and most importantly we can reuse and recycle these containers and boxes, it is a win-win.

address: Opposite Med Care Center, Jumeirah 3, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
When I think of food delivery, I would instantly think of Pizza. I have tried many pizza deliveries before and many of them satisfies my cravings. Decided to try out Broccoli Pizza and Pasta for the first time (although I have been seeing this a lot of time). Did not know it is a pizza place, I thought it was a restaurant that offers vegetarian cuisine.

We ordered pizzas (Deluxe pizza and Hawaiian) which tastes very good! I am quite biased with Hawaiian as it is my favorite pizza. Bread was freshly made, I can taste so. I would recommend too the Pepperoni Rolls - I can finish one serving of it by myself. It is so good. I can tell their pizzas are very aromatic that you know the bread are made fresh. Exciting part of the menu too is the Nutella with strawberry on a pizza bread. I only have tried the lasagna among the pastas in the menu (beef). The serving is huge and is sure is good for 2 persons - it is very filling. However, I feel like it is missing an ingredient (just a feedback) - maybe cheese in between layers? Although pasta is good and satisfying, but I believe it could be better. Again, the restaurant can customize your meal, you can always request and add something in your pasta to your liking. We also had the Asian salad, which is really fresh and satisfying. The chunk of chicken they put is large and so is the portion of greens with it. Salad alone is a complete meal in itself.

I really wish (soon) to visit one of the outlets so I can also experience the service - as you may know product is one thing, service is another. Will soon visit!

review_title: Special thanks to Filip IRD Asst Manager, for being incredibly accommodating and ensuring we were well taken care of.
review_text:
Our first stay at Address Beach Resort was truly unforgettable. We were blown away by how beautiful the property is, with stunning views of Palm Jumeirah and a breathtaking 360 view of Dubai city.

The service was exceptional from start to finish warm, attentive, and very well organized. From the entrance to dining, everything felt seamless. The food selection may be limited, but everything was fresh, healthy, and thoughtfully prepared.

The infinity pool was definitely a highlight absolutely stunning views, and the water was perfectly warm despite the breezy weather.

A special thank you to Filip (Assistant Manager, IRD) for being incredibly accommodating and ensuring we were well taken care of. Inas, our server, was attentive and made sure we were always comfortable, and Amnita at reception welcomed us with such a sweet smile.

Overall, a beautiful experience we’ll definitely be back 🤍

review_text:
I was shocked to have such a terrible experience at this 5 star restaurant located in the Ritz Carlton JBR. 

On arrival two hostess were chatting yo each other and didn’t give any of us eye contact. Rude. 

The starters came and two yellowtail were off and smelly. There was really bad fish smell and they were both very fatty. We complained about this and server left dishes in table, smelling, and said she would remove from our bill. 

The very expensive main courses arrived while starters were still ongoing. They left food to side and tried to clean table and set up cutlery for mains. We asked them to stop and remove mains. We insisted they cook us fresh mains when we were finished starters and then after 10 to 15 minutes rest bring them. 15 minutes later they brought back same mains. We declined and again insisted we hit freshly prepared food. Again they brought back the, by now, stone cold mains which were frankly disgusting. 

Across every level the staff had no clue or training. After meeting the GM it is apparent why. He is not interested in his customers and sadly his team learn from him. 

A nice location restaurant fitted out well. Sad it is not run well at all. Ritz Carlton fix this. 

We asked to speak with the manager and no one came.  My friend then had to literally stand up and walk over to the manager. He done nothing, turns out he was beverage manager. There was zero communication. I asked to speak to a senior manager. I got restaurant manager. He said he was get GM of restaurant whom it transpired was sitting a a table adjacent to us oblivious to what was happening. He was 20 ft away. He said hadn’t heard of the complaint but would now get involved. He didn’t. 

Waitress told us they would now prepare fresh food. We arrived at restaurant at 1.30 and got mains at 4.15, no apology, no discount, nothing. Very strange.

address: Bvlgari Resort, Jumeira Bay Island, Jumeira 2, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
We had an absolutely fantastic evening here.

We started with raw shrimp with lemon and olive oil — incredibly fresh and perfectly seasoned. Simple, clean, and outstanding.

The lobster pasta was a highlight: perfectly al dente, full of flavor, and beautifully executed. Rich but not heavy, with excellent balance.

We also had the tuna with tomatoes, which was superb. The tuna was cooked to perfection and the tomatoes were exceptional — fresh, vibrant, and full of taste.

Dessert was unforgettable: pistachio ice cream with a warm, soft cake — indulgent, refined, and truly delicious.

The setting made the evening even more special: a beautiful table overlooking the marina, perfect weather, and a live three-piece band that added great energy without being overpowering.

Overall, a memorable experience in every way — food, service, ambiance, and view.
Highly recommended, and we will definitely be back.

review_text:
As a longtime resident of Greens, I was disappointed as I  visited it after quite a while.
The fact that they don't have an alternative to Beef sausages or beef bacon ( whole advertising themselves as 'healthy') and not respecting the food choices of others by offering an alternative like chicken or turkey (which cafes do offer) , was bad enough.

Add the horrible attitude of the manager to refuse to respect my food choices, and stupidly telling me it was 'european cuisine' made me laugh. 
He offered me roasted chicken instead for breakfast!! His attitude and lack of respect for customers beliefs are exactly why I won't ever be setting foot in the place again. It's empty most of the time. Possibly because of this type of attitude.

Sad. It was a decent place some tears ago.

address: Una Building, Street 2, The Greens, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text: have been visiting here once a week since the last 18months, not because there is something extra ordinary about this place. It's just conveniently located near the lake. after I finish my jog/walk ,I come here mostly for green tea, water or coffee. today was disappointing though. I decided to sit outside in the open area. I was refused to be served because my 8 year old son was carrying his KFC takeaway with him and we had planned that he will eat it wherever I will decide to have my coffee. the manager looked new and didn't even bother to walk up to me to talk. he just sent the waiter saying we should leave. well, I can't really comment on the restaurant policies as it's their right to decide how they want to operate. but being an entrepreneur and an ex-restaurant owner there is a feedback I must share with the owner. at the time of writing this review, I am sitting in Costa coffee in the open area (next to Bert's). the occupancy here is about 70% right now and there are 2 families enjoying their coffee and their kids are sitting with them munching on the KFC they bought from next door. Bert's occupancy is about 5% right now and I have seen 2 more groups already leave since they were also carrying KFC packages. while it was covid lockdowns that I thought was the reason why businesses were down throughout this area. while the others have picked up, Bert's still remains empty as compared to neighbouring joints. if there is anything that can help, it's a bit of extra hospitality towards regular guests and kids. and importantly having a manager with a bit of a business acumen. when I had a restaurant, my manager used to spot prospective customers more than 20 metres away. this manager here, even without talking to me, just shook his hands from 20 metres away to shoo me from sitting there. yuck, it was disgusting. Bert's lost me as a customer today. I guess it doesn't really matter to them. as I can see from their footfalls in last 18 months. I can see their business is going to zero eventually.

While visiting Dubai, One goes to the mall, the Dubai Mall.  Yes.

I am since back in the USA from my travels, however at the moment I am enjoying a great cup of tea from my Vivel purchase and writing this review.  I believe that speaks for itself.

address: 17 2 A St., Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Yummy Fish is a small, South Indian café located behind the Emarat Petrol Station near Box Park off Al-Wasl Road in Al Safa. Its menu includes everything under the moon, from desi to local and, of course, Chinese. My food buddies James and Mark, however, came to try their South Indian breakfast. The interior of Yummy Fish isn’t actually an interior at all, it’s a shaded exterior area partitioned with plastic to keep cool.

Our breakfast of idli, vada and dosa arrived one after another, the dosa neatly folded with masala snuggly tucked inside. I remember everything being a lot better than I expected, including the sambar which was deliciously tangy but quite spicy. I also remember thinking how well these two English lads have developed a pallet for spice – they hardly broke a sweat. We also ordered a Karak chai which Yummy Fish makes super sweet unless you ask for less or no sugar. I am a type 2 diabetic but can’t resist the occasional super sweet Karak.

Service? You must be joking. Actually, service is not so bad. Let’s call it prompt. So, if you’re in the area and must have a South Indian breakfast, rather than hopping across to al-Quoz or Satwa, Yummy Fish is a decent option. Just don’t expect anything in the form of creature comforts. This is a no-frills eatery but worth a try.

Hey! Now read this –
Xerxes physically dines at, or orders from, each and every venue he reviews. He pays in full for whatever he and his companions eat, drink, take away or occasionally throw at each other. Xerxes accepts no money, gifts, discounts or free meals in return for reviews or favouritism. What you have read was NOT influenced in any way by the venue.

Jumeirah 1 Town Centre Jumeirah Jumeira Rd, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
We visited Sea World Seafood Restaurant as a group of 3 friends celebrating our birthdays together, which has become a yearly tradition for us—always ending the day with a seafood feast. 🦞 One of our friends recommended this spot, and it was the perfect choice to wrap up our birthday bash.

When we walked in, the restaurant manager, Mr. Mohamed Mokhlis, welcomed us warmly and personally assisted us. He recommended all their best dishes, and we happily said yes to everything. Best decision ever, because every dish was incredibly delicious—I’m drooling just thinking about it!

Food Highlights:

•	We had the seafood tom yum soup, calamari, tiger shrimps, and lobster, and everything was pure heaven. The soup had the perfect balance of sour and spice, with that rich seafood flavor that was just chef’s kiss. 👌
•	We also tried their signature style calamari—no words, just order it. Every bite was yummy and crispy!
•	The fried rice was also good, though it’s not Chinese-style, so it doesn’t have that wok hei flavor, but it was still flavorful and paired well with everything else.
•	And then, the tiger shrimps and lobster thermidor… my goodness, no words—just absolute perfection.

As for the service, it was exceptional. Mr. Mohamed Mokhlis and the F&B Service Associate, Mr. Adiyar, made sure we had the best experience. Mr. Adiyar was attentive, helpful, and genuinely friendly—definitely an asset to the team.

We will absolutely be coming back here again!

address: Jumeirah 1 Town Centre Jumeirah Jumeira Rd, Dubai United Arab Emirates

review_text:
Shout for seafood 🦞 lovers, Sea world restaurant is a specious seafood restaurant, dominated by a large fish counter at the back of the venue. With a huge varieties of fish on display to select from. Lobsters, Jumbo shrimps, calamari, scallops, with more than 20 varieties of fish 🐠 all fresh 🎣 
Fish can be steamed, grilled, fried, with side dishes such as soups, salads, noodles, rice with special Thai twist.
The team is well trained specially Mohammad the  Moroccan manager.
If you are looking for a fresh and delicious seafood options than Sea world restaurant is the perfect 👌 place.

address: Jumeirah Street, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_title: An experience you don’t want to miss
review_text:
Overall this is possible one of the best restaurant in Dubai, is not about the glamour, it is not the fancy boutique restaurant, but is a true piece of Iraqi history in Dubai. 
If you are accustomed to  Iraqi cuisine, here you will find the real memories of the country. Great atmosphere, good service and amazingly tasty food. The price is very affordable. Note, the portions are abundant, don't let your eyes fool you when you order, or you will end up like is walk-in out with two bags of take-away 😎.

address: Jumeirah Street, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_title: Popular outlet, pleasant atmosphere, but overrated.
review_text:
It is an interesting restaurant worth trying.

The ambiance and location of the restaurant are nice, earning a rating of 4 out of 5. The staff is attentive and provides quick service; however, they lack the hospitality and friendliness one would expect, contributing to a score of 3 out of 5 for service.

As for the food, I was unable to order their famous fish, as it weighs 3 kg and is meant for four people. Instead, I tried the following dishes:

- Eggplant prepared the Iraqi way: This was a pleasant surprise with a new and delightful taste, deserving a rating of 5 out of 5.
- Iraqi seasonal salad: While fresh, it lacked dressing and any distinctive flavor, resulting in a rating of 3 out of 5.
- The main course was lamb meat with rice (Haroof-Mahshi - خروف محشي). Unfortunately, the meat was dry and lacked flavor, leading to a disappointing rating of 1 out of 5.

Definitely the main course was off the chart.

address: Jumeira Bay Island Bulgari Resort, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_text:
Went for dinner, with manageable temperature to still sit outdoors in their terrace, overlooking the small marina and the various hotel blocks. 
We were a party of three and had no reservation but could easily find a table to be seated. 
Ambience is good, distance between tables is right, service very professional and plenty of choices for both food and drinks. 
We had the usual burata, the bruschetta pomodoro, a wagyu tagliata, tonnarelli cacio e pepe, fici toscani accompanied by a wine (white, La Pettegola). 
Had tiramisu & creme brulee...
Taste wise not exceptional but above average. 
Cost for 3 pax 1750 AED, reasonable for the location (Bulgari hotel) and Dubai upscale dining. 
Overall, a good experience.

address: Jumeira Bay Island Jumeira 2, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_title: An unforgettable Italian dinner — flawless service
review_text:
We dined at Il Ristorante – Niko Romito at Bulgari Dubai as a family (two adults and one child) and it was, without question, one of the most memorable restaurant experiences we have ever had.

Yes — it was the most expensive dinner for three that we can remember (around AED 8,500, with a regular bottle of wine). But it was also the most authentically Italian Italian food we have had in Dubai, executed with extraordinary precision.

From the moment we arrived, everything was perfect: the welcome, the warmth, the smiles, the attention, and the thoughtful guidance throughout the evening. Not a single detail escaped the team.

A special thank you to Giorgia, Leo, and Marco. Their service was impeccable and their recommendations were consistently spot-on. More than the food itself — as exceptional as it was — these three people turned the evening into something deeper: a memory that will stay in our hearts for a very long time.

Truly outstanding. We will definitely return.

address: Jumeira Bay Island Jumeira 2, Dubai United Arab Emirates
review_title: The true Italian Michelin Experience that deserves its stars
review_text:
This restaurant was beyond my expectations. The service, the experience, the hospitality was absurd. It was a truly Italian dining experience from the bread to the olive oil quality to the aged and thick balsamic vinegar… each dish had its own story. 
My ratings: 
Bread choices - All three bread sorts 10/10 with their different textures & flavors 
Olive oil - pure and enhanced 10/10 
Balsamic vinegar - aged & thick with 10/10
3 Vegetables broth - compliments of the chef 10/10 

We hopped straight into main course: 
(Forgot the names of the dish) 
Sea food linguine - 10/10… the shrimp was devine 
Truffle pasta - 10/10 rich, creamy, the fresh pasta was the star of the show, & the white truffle was the perfect touch.
Lamb veal - 6/10 it was cooked perfectly and melts in the palette but didn’t meet our expectations

They complimented us with an anniversary dessert & chef complimented us with Pantone and lemon infused drink in spirits of Christmas. 

Of course they hadn’t sent us home empty handed, left with Bvlgari chocolate bags. 

Would definitely come back.

review_text:
Al Falamenki in Dubai presents a mixed dining experience that many might describe as average. The restaurant is themed around traditional Arabic decor, creating a unique atmosphere that reflects local culture.

The menu features a variety of Middle Eastern dishes, including grilled meats, mezze, and traditional rice dishes. While some items, like the kabobs and hummus, are tasty and flavorful, others can feel somewhat standard, lacking the depth one might expect from a more specialized establishment.

Service is typically friendly, though it can sometimes be inconsistent. During rush hours, wait times for food and drinks may be longer than anticipated, which can affect the overall experience. 

Prices are reasonable, making it a suitable option for casual dining. However, those seeking an exceptional culinary experience may want to explore other restaurants in Dubai that focus more on authenticity and flavor.

In summary, Al Falamenki offers an average dining experience with a decent selection of Middle Eastern fare. It’s a good choice if you’re in the area and looking for something familiar, but it might not stand out among the city’s renowned eateries.

address: Jumeirah Beach Road, Jumeirah 2 Four Seasons Resorts Dubai at Jumeirah Beach, Dubai 128777 United Arab Emirates
review_title: Afternoon Tea: Good, but lacked wow-factor
review_text:
I recently went to the afternoon tea together with my husband. 
When we arrived at our reservation time, it was a little unclear where to stand and wait to be seated. There was no staff at the cake counter, no staff anywhere close, so we waited for a bit and had to waive on some staff from the bar after a while. For a hotel like the Four Seasons, I would expected it to go a little smoother. 

The salon is gorgeous and had fresh flowers everywhere, and we really enjoyed the live harp music.
The food looked very, very beautiful, but the flavors were unfortunately nothing to rave about. It definitely lacked the wow-factor, especially for how beautiful it all looked.The savory treats were probably our favorite, but the desserts were all kind of bland. 
(A little suggestion for the kitchen: The sesame cheesecake slides right off the cookie, so for the future, I would not dip the bottom of the cheesecake in sesame before putting in on the cookie).  

In the price included are two drinks, which also includes shakes. Mine was just okay, however. 

So, all things considered, it was a nice afternoon tea, especially in a very beautiful setting with nice music. However, having had lots of afternoon teas in Dubai and Abu Dhabi, it did not quite compare to more affordable and better ones we've had.

review_text: I recently visited the Verde restaurant in Dubai and was truly impressed. The restaurant features a modern design with natural elements, creating a cozy and relaxing atmosphere. Large windows make the space bright, while green plants add a refreshing touch. The staff was very friendly and attentive, responding quickly to requests and offering menu recommendations. The menu is diverse, focusing on Italian cuisine. The pasta was incredibly delicious, with a rich aroma from the sauce. The fresh seafood and desserts, especially the tiramisu, were divine! Prices are quite reasonable for the level of the establishment, considering the quality of the food and service. Overall, I was very pleased with my visit to Verde. It's a great place for both romantic dinners and meet-ups with friends. I highly recommend trying it!

review_text: Eating at L Bayt Baytak was one of the best meals I had in Lebanon while I was there. The food was really delicious and stuck to both the traditional styles of Lebanese cuisine and current styles of cooking.The atmosphere of the restaurant is really homely. L Bayt Baytak offers an outdoor dining setting, with plastic screening to stop annoying insects getting in. The scenery around the sitting area is really beautiful, with gorgeous wild daisies growing nearby. The service is great, and the staff were really nice.The facilities are good, with a male and female toilet. I would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a good feed, especially families.

Behara has hospitality experience in Switzerland and Dubai that I know of. He took over the extended family restaurant that has been around for 82 years a year ago when he bought out his uncles. He's converted it into a Lebanese/International locale with abundant charm! 

His mother makes the kebnehnayyeh fresh each morning and his dedication and passion are clearly evident. Everything we ate was fresh and served just right. If you're nice he may even bless you with a drop of his homemade vodka with cedar sap. Retsina like but so much better 🤩

Don't miss stopping here for any meal including breakfast and don't miss a walk through the park across the street to see the incredible cedar trees. It's worth the drive without a doubt. Bravo!!🙌
